description: Primary key for the banquet_package_composition association

description: Foreign key linking to the banquet menu package that this composition line belongs to

business_glossary_term: Banquet Package Composition - Menu Item Id
description: Foreign key linking to the specific menu item included in this package composition

The meal course to which this menu item is assigned within the package (e.g., 1=Appetizer, 2=Soup, 3=Salad, 4=Main, 5=Dessert). Drives BEO course sequencing and kitchen production scheduling. Cannot reside on banquet_menu_package (varies per item) or menu_item (varies per package).

Detailed description of all food courses included in the package (e.g., 'Appetizer, Salad, Entrée with Two Sides, Dessert, Bread Service'). Used in BEO (Banquet Event Order) documentation. [Moved from banquet_menu_package: This STRING field on banquet_menu_package is a denormalized free-text description of course inclusions (e.g., 'Appetizer, Entrée, Dessert, Coffee'). With the banquet_package_composition association table now providing structured, item-level course assignments via course_number and sequence_number, this field becomes redundant and should be deprecated or replaced by a derived/computed description. It cannot accurately represent the structured M:N composition and is a data quality risk.]
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Indicates whether this menu item is a mandatory component of the package or an optional add-on that guests may decline. Mandatory items are always included in the per-person price; optional items may affect pricing. Belongs to the composition relationship.

The number of units of this menu item served per guest within the package (e.g., 2 bread rolls, 1 entrée, 0.5 for shared platters). Critical for food cost calculation and production planning. Belongs to the composition relationship, not to either parent.

Display and service order of this menu item within its assigned course. Used to control the order items appear on BEOs and are called from the kitchen. Cannot reside on either parent entity as it is specific to the package-item pairing.

Indicates whether guests or event planners may substitute this menu item for an alternative within the package. Drives catering flexibility rules and BEO amendment workflows. Belongs to the composition relationship, not to either parent entity.

Quantity of the item that was voided. Typically an integer for countable items, may be decimal for weight-based items.
Service charge or gratuity amount associated with the voided transaction that was reversed.
Tax amount associated with the voided transaction or item that was reversed.
Total monetary value of the void including base amount, tax, and service charges. Net impact on revenue.

description: Reference to the housekeeper who cleaned the room prior to this inspection. Links to workforce/employee master data for performance tracking.

The date and time when this inspection record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Number of critical deficiencies identified that directly impact guest safety or satisfaction (e.g., cleanliness issues, safety hazards), requiring immediate remediation.
Total number of deficiency items or quality issues identified during the inspection, used for quality scoring and housekeeper performance evaluation.
Detailed free-text description of all deficiencies and quality issues identified during the inspection, used for corrective action and training purposes.
Total time in minutes spent conducting the inspection, calculated from start to end timestamps, used for labor productivity analysis and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations.
The actual date and time when the inspector completed the room inspection, used for duration calculation and productivity tracking.
The scheduled arrival date of the next guest for this room, used to prioritize inspection urgency and ensure room readiness for check-in.
Human-readable business identifier for the inspection, typically formatted as property code, date, and sequence number for operational tracking and reference.

Current lifecycle state of the inspection: scheduled (assigned but not started), in_progress (inspector actively reviewing), completed (inspection finished and passed), failed (deficiencies found requiring re-clean), or cancelled (inspection voided).

Classification of the inspection based on its purpose: routine (standard post-cleaning check), deep_clean (periodic thorough inspection), turndown (evening service check), checkout (post-guest departure), maintenance_follow_up (verification after repair), or quality_audit (supervisory quality control).

description: The date and time when this inspection record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.

Additional free-text notes and observations recorded by the inspector, including positive feedback, special conditions, or contextual information for operational reference.
Final result of the inspection: pass (room meets quality standards and is released), fail (room requires re-clean), or conditional_pass (minor issues noted but room released with follow-up required).

Priority classification for the inspection based on guest arrival time, room type, and operational needs: urgent (VIP or immediate check-in), high (same-day arrival), normal (next-day arrival), or low (future availability).
Numerical quality score assigned to the room based on inspection criteria, typically on a 0-100 scale, used for GSS (Guest Satisfaction Score) correlation and housekeeper performance tracking.
Boolean indicator whether the room requires re-cleaning due to deficiencies identified during inspection. True indicates the room failed inspection and must be re-cleaned before guest occupancy.
Boolean indicator whether the room has been formally released as inspected and made available for guest check-in. True indicates the room is ready for sale and occupancy.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Inventory Management

description: The precise date and time when the room was released as inspected and made available for guest occupancy, critical for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) and inventory availability tracking.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Inventory Management

description: The room status set after the inspection is completed, typically inspected (if passed) or dirty (if failed and requires re-clean), critical for PMS (Property Management System) room availability updates.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Status Management

description: The room status immediately prior to the inspection, typically clean (post-housekeeping) or dirty (pre-cleaning), used for status transition tracking and operational workflow validation.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Status Management

description: The date on which the inspection was scheduled to occur, used for planning and workload management.
The precise date and time when the inspection was scheduled to begin, enabling time-based operational coordination.
Free-text notes capturing any special guest requests or room preparation requirements that the inspector must verify (e.g., hypoallergenic bedding, extra amenities, accessibility features).
The actual date and time when the inspector began the room inspection, used for duration calculation and operational analytics.

Reference to the specific room where the deficiency was found. Enables room-level deficiency trend analysis.

Foreign key linking to experience.service_case. Business justification: Critical deficiencies discovered during inspection that impact guest experience generate service cases. Real business process: Proactive issue resolution and guest notification when inspection reveals

Description of the corrective action needed to resolve the deficiency. May include re-cleaning instructions, maintenance work orders, or amenity replenishment requirements.
Date and time when this deficiency record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.
Primary classification of the deficiency type. Categories align with housekeeping quality standards and maintenance handoff protocols.
reference: AHLA (American Hotel and Lodging Association) Quality Standards

description: Detailed free-text description of the specific deficiency observed during inspection. Provides context for corrective action and quality improvement analysis.
Sequential line number of this deficiency within the parent inspection. Orders deficiencies for reporting and review workflows.

Detailed subcategory within the primary deficiency category. Examples: bathroom cleanliness, HVAC malfunction, missing amenity, torn linen, wall damage, fire safety violation. Enables granular trend analysis.
Indicates whether the deficiency was escalated to management due to severity, recurrence, or resolution delays. Triggers executive visibility.
Free-text explanation of why the deficiency was escalated. Provides context for management review and process improvement.
Estimated time in minutes required to resolve the deficiency. Used for workload planning and labor cost estimation.
Indicates whether the deficiency directly impacts guest experience or satisfaction. Used for GSS (Guest Satisfaction Score) correlation analysis.
Date and time when the deficiency was first identified during inspection. Business event timestamp for deficiency lifecycle tracking.
Additional free-text notes recorded by the inspector at the time of deficiency identification. Provides supplementary context.
Date and time when this deficiency record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Specific location within the room where the deficiency was found. Examples: bathroom sink, bedroom closet, balcony door, minibar area. Enables precise corrective action.
URL or file path to photographic evidence of the deficiency. Supports visual documentation for training, dispute resolution, and quality audits.
Description of preventive actions implemented to avoid recurrence of this deficiency type. Supports continuous improvement programs.
Indicates whether this deficiency requires expedited resolution. True for deficiencies blocking room availability or impacting guest safety.
Indicates whether this deficiency has been identified in previous inspections of the same room. Flags systemic quality issues requiring root cause analysis.
Date and time when the corrective action was completed. Used to calculate actual resolution time and SLA compliance.
Direct cost incurred to resolve the deficiency, including labor, materials, and supplies. Denominated in property base currency. Supports CPOR and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) analysis.
Free-text notes recorded by the resolver documenting the corrective action taken. Supports knowledge transfer and audit trail.
Date and time when corrective action was initiated. Measures response time from identification to action.
Current status of the deficiency resolution workflow. Tracks lifecycle from identification through verification and closure.
Classification of the underlying root cause of the deficiency. Supports continuous improvement and preventive action planning.
Detailed notes on the root cause analysis performed for this deficiency. Documents findings for knowledge management and training.

Severity classification of the deficiency. Critical deficiencies prevent room sale; major deficiencies impact guest satisfaction; minor and cosmetic deficiencies are tracked for continuous improvement.
Date and time when the deficiency resolution was verified by a supervisor or quality inspector. Ensures quality control closure.
Unique identifier for the housekeeping attendant record. Primary key for the attendant entity in the housekeeping domain.

Reference to the property where this attendant is primarily assigned. Supports multi-property workforce management.

Foreign key linking to experience.service_case. Business justification: Service cases about housekeeping quality need to track responsible attendant for accountability. Real business process: Performance management, coaching, and progressive discipline based on guest comp

Foreign key linking to spa.spa_facility. Business justification: Housekeeping attendants are often assigned exclusively to spa facilities requiring specialized training (chemical handling, sanitation protocols). Linking attendant to spa_facility enables spa-specifi

Indicates whether this attendant record is currently active and available for scheduling and room assignments. Inactive records are retained for historical reporting but excluded from operational workflows.

business_glossary_term: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Accommodation Flag
description: Indicates whether the attendant has an approved reasonable accommodation under the Americans with Disabilities Act. Accommodations may include modified duty assignments, assistive equipment, or adjusted productivity targets. Details of specific accommodations are maintained in confidential HR records.

Current attendance point balance under the property's attendance policy. Points are typically assigned for absences, tardiness, and early departures, with progressive discipline triggered at defined thresholds. Points may expire after a rolling period (e.g., 12 months).
Business identifier for the attendant used in daily operations, room assignment sheets, and housekeeping reports. Typically a short alphanumeric code displayed on badges and mobile devices.

Rolling average of actual room credits completed per shift over the most recent evaluation period (typically 30 or 90 days). Used to track individual productivity trends and identify training needs or performance issues.
Timestamp when this attendant record was first created in the housekeeping system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Name of the person to contact in case of workplace emergency or injury. Maintained for employee safety and OSHA compliance.
Phone number of the emergency contact person. Critical for rapid communication in case of workplace accidents or medical emergencies.

Current employment lifecycle status of the attendant. Active attendants are available for scheduling. On-leave includes FMLA, medical, and personal leave. Seasonal workers are employed during peak periods. Probationary status applies to new hires during their initial evaluation period.
Date the attendant was hired into the housekeeping department. Used to calculate seniority for union section bidding, vacation accrual, and tenure-based benefits.

Comma-separated list of languages the attendant can speak and understand. Important for guest interaction, safety communication, and team coordination in multilingual properties. Format: ISO 639-1 language codes (e.g., en,es,fr).
Date of the most recent formal performance review. Most properties conduct annual or semi-annual reviews aligned with merit increase cycles.
Timestamp when this attendant record was most recently modified. Tracks the recency of data for change data capture and incremental processing.
Assigned locker number in the employee locker room where the attendant stores personal belongings, uniform, and equipment during their shift.

Identifier of the mobile device (smartphone or tablet) assigned to the attendant for receiving room assignments, updating room status, reporting maintenance issues, and communicating with supervisors. Integrates with property management system mobile housekeeping applications.

Free-text field for operational notes about the attendant, such as special skills, assignment preferences, equipment needs, or temporary restrictions. Used by supervisors for day-to-day workforce management.

Most recent performance evaluation rating for the attendant. Based on quality inspections, productivity metrics, guest feedback, attendance, and adherence to standards. Used for merit increases, promotion decisions, and performance improvement plans.

Primary housekeeping role classification. Determines work assignment types, productivity benchmarks, and labor cost allocation. Room attendants clean guest rooms, turndown attendants provide evening service, house persons handle heavy cleaning and linen transport, supervisors oversee sections, inspectors perform quality checks, and public area cleaners maintain lobbies and common spaces.
Current section or floor assignment within the property. Sections are typically organized by floor, wing, or building. Section assignments may be permanent (based on seniority bidding) or rotational.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Housekeeping Section Configuration

description: Date used for calculating seniority ranking within the housekeeping department. May differ from hire date due to transfers, breaks in service, or union contract provisions. Critical for section bidding rights and shift preference allocation.

Current shift assignment for the attendant. AM shift typically covers morning checkout cleaning, PM shift handles afternoon turnovers and turndown service, night shift performs deep cleaning and public area maintenance. Split shifts cover peak periods. On-call attendants fill in for absences.

Productivity benchmark expressed as the target number of room credits the attendant should complete per shift. Room credits vary by room type: standard rooms = 1.0 credit, suites = 1.5-2.0 credits, checkout rooms = 1.2 credits, stayover rooms = 0.7 credits. Used for performance evaluation and labor cost per occupied room (CPOR) calculation.
Date the attendant's employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for historical workforce analysis and rehire eligibility determination.
Uniform size for the attendant used for ordering and inventory management of housekeeping uniforms. Format varies by property (e.g., S/M/L/XL or numeric sizing).

Union job classification code as defined in the collective bargaining agreement. Determines pay grade, work rules, and assignment restrictions. Examples include classifications for different room types, shift differentials, and specialized duties.
Indicates whether the attendant is a member of a labor union. Determines applicability of collective bargaining agreement provisions, grievance procedures, and union-specific benefits.
Unique identifier for the cleaning standard configuration. Primary key.
reference: HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) - Housekeeping Data Standards

Detailed instructions for amenity item placement and presentation (e.g., toiletries arrangement, towel folding style, welcome card positioning, turndown chocolate placement). Ensures brand consistency and guest experience quality.
Date this cleaning standard was approved for operational use. Required for compliance audit trail.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this cleaning standard (e.g., Director of Housekeeping, Brand Standards Manager). Required for brand compliance and audit trail.
Indicates whether this standard is mandatory for brand compliance audits. True if required for franchise/brand certification. Non-compliance may result in brand penalties.
Indicates whether attendants must complete certification training before executing this standard. True for specialized standards (e.g., deep clean, hazardous material handling, luxury suite service).
reference: OSHA Training Requirements, HTNG Housekeeping Certification

description: Approved cleaning chemical products and usage instructions (e.g., all-purpose cleaner brand/dilution, glass cleaner, bathroom sanitizer, disinfectant contact time). Ensures safety compliance and brand standards.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) Hazard Communication Standard, ISO 22000 Food Safety

description: Current lifecycle status of the cleaning standard: active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), draft (under development), archived (historical record).

Type of cleaning service this standard defines: stayover (occupied room daily service), departure (checkout deep clean), deep_clean (periodic intensive cleaning), turndown (evening service), public_area (lobby/corridor/amenity spaces), refresh (quick tidy between same-day checkouts).
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) - Housekeeping Department Standards

description: Estimated total cost in USD to execute this cleaning standard once, including labor, supplies, and equipment depreciation. Used for CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) budgeting and variance analysis.
Timestamp when this cleaning standard record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.
Date this cleaning standard becomes active and enforceable. Used for seasonal standard changes and brand compliance rollouts.
List of equipment and tools required to execute this standard (e.g., vacuum cleaner, microfiber cloths, mop, caddy, UV sanitizer). Used for attendant assignment and equipment allocation.
Date this cleaning standard is no longer valid. Null for indefinite standards. Used for seasonal or promotional service upgrades.
Minimum quality inspection score (0-100 scale) required to pass this cleaning standard. Rooms scoring below threshold require re-cleaning. Typical luxury threshold: 95+, select service: 85+.
Estimated labor cost in USD for this cleaning standard execution, calculated as target_time_minutes multiplied by average hourly wage. Primary component of CPOR.
Date this cleaning standard was last reviewed for accuracy and relevance. Standards should be reviewed annually or when brand requirements change.
Number of days between scheduled linen changes when protocol is every_n_days. Null for other protocols. Typical values: 2-3 for luxury, 3-5 for select service.

Linen replacement policy for this standard: full_change (all linens replaced), on_request (guest opt-in), every_n_days (scheduled rotation), departure_only (checkout only). Impacts sustainability metrics and laundry costs.
Maximum allowable time in minutes for this cleaning standard. Exceeding this threshold triggers supervisor review and quality audit.
Minimum time allocation in minutes required to complete this cleaning standard. Used for labor scheduling, productivity tracking, and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculation.
Timestamp when this cleaning standard record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking.
Scheduled date for the next review of this cleaning standard. Used for governance and continuous improvement workflows.
Free-text notes for additional context, historical changes, or operational guidance not captured in structured fields.

Structured quality inspection checklist and scoring rubric (e.g., cleanliness score, amenity placement accuracy, linen presentation, bathroom sanitation). Used by supervisors and quality auditors to validate standard compliance.
Additional notes or special handling requirements (e.g., allergy-friendly cleaning for sensitive guests, pet-friendly room protocols, post-construction deep clean procedures).
Business identifier code for the cleaning standard (e.g., STY_LUX_SUITE, DEP_STD_KING, DEEP_PREM_DBL). Used for operational reference and system integration.
Human-readable name of the cleaning standard (e.g., Luxury Suite Stayover Service, Premium Departure Clean).
Estimated quantity of consumable supplies per cleaning execution (e.g., 2 trash bags, 1 toilet paper roll, 50ml all-purpose cleaner). Used for inventory forecasting and CPOR calculation.
Estimated consumable supply cost in USD per execution (chemicals, amenities, linens, trash bags). Used for inventory budgeting and CPOR calculation.
Indicates whether this standard meets corporate sustainability and environmental compliance requirements (e.g., eco-friendly chemicals, linen reuse program, water conservation). True if compliant.
reference: AHLA Sustainability Guidelines, ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Target time in minutes for completing this cleaning standard under normal conditions. Used for performance benchmarking and efficiency KPIs.

Structured list of required cleaning tasks in sequence (e.g., strip linens, dust surfaces, vacuum, sanitize bathroom, restock amenities). Stored as delimited text or JSON. Used to generate attendant mobile app checklists.
Reference to the training module or learning content that teaches this cleaning standard (e.g., LMS course ID, training video URL, SOP document number). Used for onboarding and certification.
Version number of this cleaning standard configuration. Incremented when standard is updated. Supports audit trail and change management.

Comma-separated list of property codes where this package is available. Supports multi-property package offerings.

Operational notes and special instructions for spa staff regarding package delivery, setup requirements, or guest experience protocols.

Total duration of all included treatments and transitions in minutes. Used for scheduling and capacity planning.

Package-specific duration for this treatment in minutes, overriding the treatment's standard duration_minutes when the package bundles a shorter or extended version. Null if the standard duration applies.

The flag becomes redundant and its meaning is better governed by the association. However, it may be retained on treatment as a catalog-level pre-qualification filter — recommend review by spa operations to determine if it should be deprecated post-association implementation.]
Monetary amount attributed to this treatment within the package rack rate, used for revenue center allocation and financial reporting. Varies by package-treatment combination.
Number of times this treatment is included in the package (e.g., 2 for a couples package that includes two massages). Belongs to the composition, not to either entity alone.
Ordinal position of this treatment within the package experience flow (e.g., 1 = first treatment, 2 = second). Belongs to the composition, not to the package or treatment independently.

Type of treatment room or facility required to deliver this service (e.g., single room, couples suite, wet room, outdoor cabana). Used for facility scheduling and capacity planning.

Number of hours before the appointment that a guest can cancel without penalty. Used for revenue protection and scheduling optimization.
Indicates whether therapists or spa staff earn commission on sales of this treatment. Used for payroll and incentive compensation calculations.

Medical conditions, allergies, or circumstances under which the treatment should not be performed or requires physician approval. Critical for guest safety and liability management.

description: Indicates whether this booking has been cancelled (True = cancelled, False = active). Used for channel-level cancellation rate analysis and net booking volume reporting.

reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations Module; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Indicates whether the booked rate is compliant with the rate parity agreement for this channel (True = compliant, False = parity violation detected). Critical for OTA contract compliance monitoring and channel rate parity management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; AHLA OTA Partnership Standards; EU Rate Parity Regulations

description: The timestamp when this channel booking transaction record was last updated in the Silver layer data product. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role. Used for change data capture, incremental load processing, and audit compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); SOX Section 404 (audit trail); HTNG Data Exchange Standards

description: The number of days between the booking timestamp and the check-in date. Stored as a business attribute to support lead time distribution analysis by channel, demand forecasting, and last-minute booking pattern identification.

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Channel contracts create financial obligations requiring GL account assignment for proper revenue/expense recognition, audit trail compliance, and financial statement preparation per accounting standa

Foreign key linking to property.ownership_entity. Business justification: Legal contract counterparty process: in ownership-managed hotel structures, the ownership entity (not the operating property) is the legal signatory on channel distribution contracts. Finance and lega

reference: GDPR Article 28; CCPA; PCI DSS; AHLA Data Privacy Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal jurisdiction governing dispute resolution and contract interpretation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Contract Management; IFRS; GDPR Article 79

description: Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the hotel and the channel partner.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Contract Management; AHLA Legal Standards

description: Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and the channel is authorized to distribute inventory. Aligns with USALI period reporting.
reference: USALI; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA

description: Date on which the contract ceases to be binding. Null for evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and rate parity audits.
reference: USALI; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA Contract Management

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processing Agreement (DPA) Reference
description: Reference number or document identifier for the GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed with the channel partner. Mandatory for EU-based OTA and GDS partnerships under GDPR Article 28.
reference: GDPR Article 28; CCPA; AHLA Data Privacy

description: Frequency at which commission invoices or settlement statements are issued between the hotel and the channel partner.

Indicates whether the contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the hotel to make the last available room accessible to this channel even when inventory is restricted for other channels.
reference: AHLA Distribution Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Annual or contract-period monetary commitment for co-operative marketing spend with the channel partner (e.g., sponsored listings, display advertising on OTA platform). Expressed in the contract currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance; USALI Marketing Expense; AHLA

description: Indicates whether the contract permits the hotel to offer Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) through this channel. Relevant for revenue management strategy and cancellation policy governance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA

description: Commercial payment model governing how the channel collects and remits payment: merchant model (channel collects from guest and remits net), agency model (hotel collects and pays commission), net rate (channel marks up a net rate), or wholesale.
reference: USALI; SAP S/4HANA AR/AP; AHLA Distribution Standards

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry (PCI) Compliance Confirmed Indicator
description: Indicates whether the channel partner has confirmed PCI DSS compliance as required by the contract terms for handling payment card data.

Tier designation within the OTA or GDS preferred partner program (e.g., Expedia Preferred Plus, Booking.com Preferred). Higher tiers typically confer better placement, lower commission rates, or co-op marketing benefits.
reference: AHLA; Sabre SynXis CRS Partner Programs

description: Indicates whether the contract includes a rate parity obligation requiring the hotel to offer the channel the same or better rates as other distribution channels. Critical for revenue management compliance and OTA relationship governance.
reference: AHLA; EU Competition Law (rate parity regulations); IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation: broad parity (match all channels including direct), narrow parity (match only other OTAs, not direct), or no parity obligation. Reflects EU regulatory distinctions post-2015 investigations.
reference: EU Competition Law; AHLA; IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Strategy

description: Number of calendar days prior to expiration_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives automated contract renewal alerts.
Specifies whether the contract auto-renews at expiration, requires manual renegotiation, or is a one-time non-renewable agreement.
Date on which all parties executed the contract. Used for legal audit trails and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA Contract Management

description: Operational system of record from which this contract record was ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Contract Management, Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, or manually entered).
reference: HTNG Data Integration Standards; SAP S/4HANA

description: Actual date the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Distinct from expiration_date which is the scheduled end. Populated only when contract_status is 'terminated'.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contract record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse ETL pipeline and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5(e); SAP S/4HANA

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each commission accrual record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. ROLE: TRANSACTION_HEADER.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Commission accruals need to link to AR invoices for revenue recognition and reconciliation. When commissions are invoiced to OTA partners, this FK tracks the billing document. This is a standard finan

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Commission accruals are tracked by booking source for cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel attribution. Many accruals per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source_code which can be retriev

Reference to the distribution channel through which the booking was made (e.g., OTA, GDS, direct web, voice). Links to the channel master in the channel domain for channel mix and cost-of-acquisition reporting.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation / AHLA Technology)

description: Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: Commission rates vary by channel rate plan. The commission_accrual table has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to channel_rate_plan via FK. This links commission accruals to the speci

Reference to the commission schedule or rate agreement applied to this accrual record. Determines the applicable commission rate tier, calculation method, and contractual terms with the OTA or GDS partner.

Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Corporate bookings generate commissions that must be attributed to the corporate account for AR reconciliation, account performance reporting, and annual room-night target tracking. Finance and accoun

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission accruals are distribution expenses requiring cost center allocation for accurate departmental financial statements, enabling proper matching of channel costs to revenue per USALI reporting

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Commission accruals must be posted to the correct fiscal period for USALI-compliant P&L reporting and period-end close. Finance controllers use this link to produce period-accurate commission expense

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Commission cost-of-acquisition analysis by market segment is a standard revenue management report. Linking accruals to market_segment enables segment-level distribution cost benchmarking and USALI-com

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_negotiated_rate. Business justification: Negotiated rates carry specific commission terms (commission_pct, iata_number). Linking commission_accrual to revenue_negotiated_rate enables verification that accrued commissions match the contracted

Reference to the OTA or GDS partner entity responsible for the booking. Populated when the channel type is OTA or GDS; null for direct bookings. Supports OTA partnership performance and commission reconciliation reporting.
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which the commission is being accrued. Enables property-level commission cost reporting and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) analysis.

Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: Commission accruals must link directly to reservations for reconciliation, dispute resolution, cancellation-driven commission reversals, and complete audit trail from booking to financial posting. Cur

The accounting date on which the commission liability was recognized in the general ledger. Aligns with USALI period-end accrual practices and SAP S/4HANA GL posting date. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.

Current lifecycle state of the commission accrual record. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) workflow in SAP S/4HANA: 'accrued' = liability recognized; 'invoiced' = OTA invoice received; 'paid' = payment disbursed; 'disputed' = under reconciliation review; 'cancelled' = booking cancelled before stay; 'reversed' = accrual reversed due to no-show or refund. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.

The Average Daily Rate of the booking, calculated as gross_booking_value divided by room_nights. Stored for channel performance benchmarking and to support STR STAR Report comparisons without requiring recalculation from source.
The date on which the reservation was cancelled, if applicable. When populated, triggers commission accrual reversal logic per the cancellation policy terms. Null for active or completed bookings.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; SAP S/4HANA AP

description: Classification of the distribution channel through which the booking originated. Drives commission rate tier selection and channel mix reporting. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus); direct_web = brand.com; voice = call center; corporate = negotiated corporate account; wholesale = tour operator/wholesaler.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG / AHLA Technology; AHLA

description: The commission amount converted to the company's functional base currency (typically USD) for consolidated financial reporting and USALI-compliant P&L statements. Enables cross-property and cross-currency commission cost analysis. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: USALI; SAP S/4HANA GL; GAAP/IFRS; IFRS 21

description: The calculated commission amount payable to the distribution channel partner in the local transaction currency (gross_booking_value × commission_rate). Used for OTA invoice reconciliation and AP liability posting. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.

Defines the revenue base on which the commission rate is applied: 'room_revenue' = room charges only; 'total_revenue' = all charges including F&B and ancillary; 'net_revenue' = revenue net of taxes and fees; 'flat_fee' = fixed amount per booking regardless of revenue.

The contractual commission rate applied to the gross booking value, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Sourced from the applicable commission schedule. Used to calculate the commission amount and for rate parity and cost-of-acquisition analysis.

Classification of the commission arrangement: 'standard' = default contractual rate; 'override' = manually overridden rate approved by revenue management; 'promotional' = time-limited promotional rate; 'group' = group booking commission; 'negotiated' = bilaterally negotiated rate outside standard schedule.

The GDS or channel connectivity fee charged by the distribution technology provider (e.g., Sabre SynXis transaction fee) associated with this booking, in local currency. Included in total cost-of-acquisition (CoA) reporting alongside commission.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; USALI; SAP S/4HANA AP

description: Timestamp when this commission accrual record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and SOX financial controls compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.

Free-text notes describing the nature of a commission dispute with the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status is 'disputed'. Supports the channel dispute resolution workflow and AP reconciliation process.

The exchange rate applied to convert the local currency commission amount to the base currency at the time of accrual. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA treasury rates. Required for accurate multi-currency financial reporting and audit trail.

The SAP S/4HANA GL account code to which the commission expense is posted (e.g., USALI Schedule 6 - Sales and Marketing / Distribution Costs). Ensures correct financial statement classification per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL; USALI; GAAP/IFRS; SOX

description: The total gross booking value of the reservation on which commission is calculated, in the local transaction currency. Represents the full room revenue amount before any discounts or adjustments. Forms the base for commission rate application. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: USALI; SAP S/4HANA AP; PCI DSS

description: The date on the commission invoice received from the OTA or GDS partner. Used for AP aging analysis, payment due date calculation, and period-end accrual reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.

The OTA or GDS partner's invoice reference number received for commission payment. Used to match accrued commission liabilities against received invoices in SAP S/4HANA AP for three-way reconciliation. Null until invoice is received.

Indicates whether the booking is eligible for commission payment (True) or is a non-commissionable booking (False), such as direct loyalty redemptions, staff rates, or complimentary stays. Drives commission accrual logic and AP posting.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the booking was made and commission is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency commission reconciliation and FX conversion. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.
The actual date on which the commission payment was disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status transitions to 'paid'. Used for AP reconciliation and cash flow reporting.
The contractual due date by which the commission payment must be disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner per the commission schedule terms. Used for AP cash flow planning and SLA compliance monitoring.

The payment transaction reference or bank transfer reference number from SAP S/4HANA AP when the commission is disbursed. Enables end-to-end traceability from accrual to cash payment for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP; SOX; PCI DSS

description: The reason code for reversing a previously accrued commission. Populated when accrual_status is 'reversed' or 'cancelled'. Supports AP reconciliation and channel dispute management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cancellation|no_show|rate_correction|duplicate|dispute_resolved|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]

The number of room nights associated with the booking for which commission is being accrued. Used to calculate CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) and to normalize commission costs across bookings of varying LOS (Length of Stay).
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; STR (Smith Travel Research)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this commission accrual record originated: 'OPERA_PMS' = Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module; 'SYNXIS_CRS' = Sabre SynXis CRS channel management; 'SAP_AP' = SAP S/4HANA AP accrual; 'MANUAL' = manually entered adjustment. Supports data lineage and ETL audit.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; SAP S/4HANA; HTNG / AHLA Technology

description: The total distribution cost for this booking, combining commission_amount_local and connectivity_fee_amount. Represents the all-in cost of acquiring the booking through this channel. Used for channel profitability and net RevPAR analysis.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission accrual record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, reconciliation audit trails, and SOX financial controls. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
Unique surrogate identifier for each commission or fee schedule record in the channel distribution cost management system. Primary key for the commission_schedule data product.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Commission schedules are negotiated as part of OTA co-marketing campaigns; tracking which campaign drove specific commission structures is essential for marketing spend reconciliation, contract compli

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Promotional campaigns frequently include elevated commission incentives for OTA partners tied to specific offers. Finance and channel teams need to link commission schedules to the campaign offer that

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Commission schedules define the commission terms negotiated in channel contracts with OTA partners and GDS providers. Adding nullable channel_contract_id FK links commission schedules to their governi

Reference to the specific rate plan (BAR, LRA, NRR, package, negotiated corporate) to which this commission schedule is scoped. Null indicates the schedule applies to all rate plans for the channel partner.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS — Pricing

description: Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Commission schedules are negotiated per corporate account (e.g., TMC override rates, volume-tiered commissions). Account managers and finance teams need this link to apply the correct commission sched

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission schedules define expense structures that must be allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L reporting, enabling accurate distribution cost analysis and budget variance tracking.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Commission schedules define fee structures for specific distribution channels. Many commission schedules per channel (e.g., tiered by volume, rate plan, market segment). Removes redundant channel_type

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Commission schedules are aligned to fiscal periods for budget vs. actual commission expense analysis. Revenue managers and finance teams use this link to evaluate channel commission costs within defin

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Commission schedules are differentiated by market segment (e.g., corporate negotiated vs. leisure OTA vs. group). Proper FK enables segment-scoped commission schedule lookup and rate loading validatio
Reference to the distribution channel partner (OTA, GDS, CRS, channel manager, direct booking engine) to which this commission or fee schedule applies. Links to the channel partner master record.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) Channel Standards

description: Reference to the specific property or hotel to which this commission schedule applies. Null or a portfolio-level sentinel value indicates the schedule applies across all properties (chain-wide agreement).
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Property Configuration; USALI

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: OTA and travel agent contracts frequently specify differentiated commission rates by room type (e.g., suites vs. standard rooms). A room_type_id FK on commission_schedule enables room-type-specific co

Indicates whether the commission or fee is still payable when a booking is cancelled (True = commission owed on cancellations, False = commission waived on cancellations). Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) commission accrual and cancellation cost management.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Indicates whether the commission or fee is payable on no-show bookings (True = commission owed on no-shows, False = commission waived). Affects no-show revenue net-of-commission calculations and OTA contract compliance.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Name or identifier of the authorized approver (e.g., VP Revenue Management, CFO) who approved this commission schedule for execution. Required for SOX financial controls compliance and audit trail.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Financial Controls; SAP S/4HANA — Procurement MM

description: Date and time when this commission schedule was formally approved for activation. Part of the SOX-compliant approval audit trail for financial commitments.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Financial Controls; SAP S/4HANA — Procurement MM

description: Indicates whether commission and fee accruals under this schedule are automatically calculated and posted to the GL upon booking confirmation (True) or require manual accrual processing (False). Supports automated AP accrual workflows in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Finance GL/AP; USALI — Accrual Accounting; IFRS/GAAP

description: Frequency at which the channel partner invoices the property for commissions or fees: monthly (most common for OTA commissions), weekly, per_booking (real-time deduction), quarterly, annual (common for connectivity subscription fees). Drives AP accrual scheduling and cash flow forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; USALI — Accounts Payable; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Maximum advance booking window (in days before arrival) for which this commission schedule is applicable. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction. Used in conjunction with booking_window_min_days for last-minute rate commission scoping.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls

description: Minimum advance booking window (in days before arrival) for which this commission schedule is applicable. Used for LOS (Length of Stay) and advance purchase rate plan commission scoping. Null indicates no minimum booking window restriction.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls

description: The unit basis on which the commission or fee is calculated and accrued: per_booking (one charge per reservation), per_room_night (charge per occupied room night), per_segment (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction (CRS per-transaction charge), per_period (fixed charge per billing period regardless of volume). Drives accrual engine logic.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; GDS Industry Standards; HTNG Channel Standards

description: Negotiated commission rate expressed as a percentage of the applicable revenue base (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Applicable when fee_structure is 'percentage'. Represents the contractually agreed OTA or agent commission rate. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Date and time when this commission schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Financial Controls; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; USALI

description: The calendar date from which this commission or fee schedule becomes contractually binding and applicable to bookings. Used to determine which schedule version applies to a given booking date for accrual and invoice reconciliation.
reference: USALI — Contract Effective Dates; SAP S/4HANA AP Module; IFRS/GAAP — Revenue Recognition

description: The calendar date on which this commission or fee schedule expires and is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended agreement with no defined expiry. Used for schedule versioning and contract renewal tracking.
reference: USALI — Contract Effective Dates; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the flat fee or tiered threshold amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP reconciliation and cost-of-acquisition reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP — Foreign Currency Transactions

description: Defines how the commission or fee is calculated: percentage (applied as a % of booking revenue or room revenue), flat (fixed monetary amount per booking or period), tiered (rate varies by volume or revenue thresholds), per_unit (fixed amount per room-night, segment, or transaction unit). Determines the accrual calculation method.
reference: USALI — Cost of Acquisition; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Classification of the cost type captured by this schedule: commission (percentage of booking revenue paid to OTA/agent), per_segment_fee (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction_fee (CRS per-booking transaction fee), connectivity_fee (channel manager or switch connectivity charge), subscription_fee (fixed periodic platform fee), override_commission (negotiated override above standard rate). Drives AP coding and cost-of-acquisition (COA) categorization.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; HTNG Channel Standards; GDS Industry Standards (IATA/ARC)

description: Fixed monetary fee amount per booking, segment, transaction, or billing period, expressed in the schedule currency. Applicable when fee_structure is 'flat' or 'per_unit'. Used for GDS per-segment fees, CRS per-transaction fees, and connectivity subscription fees. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: The General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA to which commission and fee expenses are posted for financial reporting under USALI. Ensures correct P&L classification of distribution costs (e.g., OTA commission expense, GDS fee expense) per USALI departmental accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Finance GL; USALI — Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry; IFRS/GAAP

description: Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) in nights required for this commission schedule to apply. Null indicates no LOS restriction. Supports commission scoping for extended-stay or minimum-stay rate plans.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution; USALI

description: Contractual cap on the commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period. Applicable for schedules with a commission ceiling clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Contractual minimum commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period, regardless of the calculated percentage amount. Applicable for schedules with a commission floor clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Name or identifier of the internal team or individual (e.g., Revenue Management, Channel Distribution, Procurement) responsible for negotiating this commission schedule. Used for accountability tracking and contract ownership.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Procurement MM; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Financial Controls

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, special contractual conditions, negotiation history notes, or exceptions associated with this commission schedule. Not used for structured data; intended for operational reference by Revenue Management and AP teams.

Number of days from invoice receipt within which payment to the channel partner is due (e.g., 30 = Net 30). Used in AP aging analysis and cash flow management per SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; IFRS/GAAP — Accounts Payable; USALI

description: Indicates whether rate parity compliance is contractually required and actively monitored for bookings under this commission schedule (True = rate parity clause in effect). Supports rate parity monitoring workflows and OTA contract compliance management.
reference: AHLA Distribution Cost Standards; HTNG Channel Standards; EU Rate Parity Regulations

description: Defines the revenue component on which the commission percentage is calculated: room_revenue (room charges only), total_revenue (all charges including F&B and ancillaries), net_revenue (after discounts and taxes), gross_revenue (before deductions), f_and_b_revenue (food and beverage charges only). Critical for accurate commission accrual and invoice reconciliation.
reference: USALI — Revenue Classification; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this commission or fee schedule, used in AP reconciliation, contract references, and channel partner communications (e.g., OTA-EXP-COMM-2024, GDS-SAB-SEG-FEE).
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the commission or fee schedule, used in reporting dashboards, AP workflows, and channel profitability analysis (e.g., 'Expedia Standard OTA Commission FY2024', 'Sabre GDS Per-Segment Fee Q1 2024').
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Current lifecycle state of the commission schedule: active (currently in force), inactive (temporarily suspended), pending (approved but not yet effective), expired (past effective_until date), terminated (cancelled before expiry), draft (under negotiation, not yet approved). Drives AP processing eligibility and accrual inclusion.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; USALI — Channel Cost Management

description: Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 1 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Lower volume or revenue threshold (in booking count or currency amount) that triggers the Tier 1 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is 'tiered'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 2 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards

description: Upper volume or revenue threshold that triggers the Tier 2 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is 'tiered'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Classification; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Date and time when this commission schedule record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Financial Controls; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each booking source attribution record in the channel distribution master. Primary key for the booking_source reference master. [ROLE: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity is a reference master / code list for booking source attribution codes; per canonical-attrs-enforced rule, REFERENCE_LOOKUP role is exempt from per-role minimums. _canonical_skip_reason: booking_source is a reference master / code table classifying reservation origin at sub-channel granularity; it does not represent a party, agreement, resource, transaction, or event log.]
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Channel Attribution Standards

description: Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Booking source is a granular attribution code that rolls up to a parent distribution channel. Many booking sources belong to one channel. Removes redundant parent_channel_code and parent_channel_name

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Booking sources are associated with specific market segments for revenue attribution and commission calculation (e.g., corporate direct vs. leisure OTA). FK enables segment-level booking source perfor

Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Booking sources may be associated with specific OTA partners for attribution and commission tracking. Many booking sources can belong to one OTA partner. Removes redundant ota_partner_code which can b

Date on which this booking source was first activated and made live for reservation acceptance in the CRS and PMS. Used for channel performance benchmarking (measuring ramp-up period) and historical channel mix analysis.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Activation; Oracle OPERA PMS — Source Code Administration

description: Technical connectivity method used to exchange availability, rates, and inventory (ARI) data between the hotel's CRS/PMS and this booking source. Determines integration complexity, latency, and update frequency for rate and inventory distribution.
reference: HTNG (AHLA Technology) API Connectivity Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Connectivity

description: Numeric level in the booking source attribution hierarchy used for revenue reporting roll-ups. Level 1 = top-level channel (e.g., OTA), Level 2 = sub-channel (e.g., OTA Desktop), Level 3 = specific source (e.g., Expedia Desktop US). Enables hierarchical channel mix analysis from property to brand level.
reference: HTNG Channel Attribution Standards; USALI Revenue Reporting Hierarchy

description: Current lifecycle status of the booking source record. 'active' = currently receiving reservations; 'inactive' = temporarily disabled; 'pending' = configured but not yet live; 'suspended' = paused due to contract or compliance issue; 'retired' = permanently decommissioned. Used for channel activation management in Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; Oracle OPERA PMS — Source Code Administration

description: Maximum advance booking window in days applicable to this booking source, as configured in the CRS. Defines the furthest point in advance that a reservation can be made through this channel. Used for long-range demand forecasting and group block management.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Configuration

description: Minimum advance booking window in days applicable to this booking source, as configured in the CRS and RMS. Defines the earliest point before arrival that a reservation can be made through this channel. Used for demand forecasting and inventory control in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Configuration

description: Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to reservations booked through this source, as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Determines refundability rules (e.g., Non-Refundable Rate (NRR), flexible cancellation). Impacts revenue recognition and overbooking strategy.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Cancellation Policy Configuration; AHLA Cancellation Policy Standards

description: High-level classification of the distribution channel type indicating whether the booking originates from a direct brand channel, an indirect intermediary (OTA/GDS/Wholesale), a voice channel (call center/reservation agent), or a group/MICE channel. Used for strategic channel mix reporting and direct booking ratio KPI tracking.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management

description: Defines the basis on which commission is calculated for this booking source: net_rate (commission on net room rate), gross_rate (commission on gross rate including taxes), flat_fee (fixed amount per booking), or per_segment (GDS segment fee per booking). Drives commission calculation logic in accounts payable processing.
reference: USALI — Channel Cost Accounting; SAP S/4HANA AP Commission Processing

description: Standard commission percentage payable to the booking source intermediary (OTA, travel agency, GDS) per confirmed reservation. Expressed as a percentage of the net room revenue. Used for cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation, channel profitability analysis, and accounts payable commission processing in SAP S/4HANA. Null for direct/zero-commission channels.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) — Channel Cost Accounting; AHLA Commission Standards

description: Fixed monetary fee charged per booking or per GDS segment for this booking source, expressed in the property's base currency (USD). Applicable when connectivity_fee_applicable is True. Used alongside commission_rate to compute total cost of acquisition (COA) per channel.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — GDS Connectivity Fees; USALI Channel Cost Accounting

description: Indicates whether a GDS or channel connectivity fee is charged per booking for this source, in addition to or instead of a commission. True for GDS sources with per-segment fees (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). Used for total cost-of-acquisition calculation and channel profitability reporting.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — GDS Connectivity Fees; USALI Channel Cost Accounting

description: Expiration date of the distribution agreement or contract with this booking source partner. Null for open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal alerts, commission rate expiry management, and channel deactivation planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Contract Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Agreement Standards

description: Effective start date of the distribution agreement or contract with this booking source partner. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission rate validity tracking, and channel activation scheduling in SAP S/4HANA contract management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Contract Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Agreement Standards

description: Timestamp when this booking source record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and reference data change management. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability; SOX — Audit Trail Requirements

description: Source code as configured in Sabre SynXis CRS for this booking source. This is the operational identifier used during reservation creation and rate distribution. May differ from the internal source_code when CRS uses a legacy or partner-assigned code.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Central Reservations; HTNG CRS Interface Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which commissions and connectivity fees for this booking source are settled. Used for multi-currency commission processing in SAP S/4HANA accounts payable and foreign exchange reconciliation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA — AP Multi-Currency Processing; IFRS/GAAP

description: Date on which this booking source was deactivated or retired from the CRS and PMS. Null for currently active sources. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting continuity, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; Oracle OPERA PMS — Source Code Administration

description: Standard GDS identifier code for the booking source when the channel is a Global Distribution System participant. Applicable values include Amadeus (1A), Sabre (1S), Travelport Galileo (1G), Travelport Worldspan (1P). Null for non-GDS sources. Used for GDS connectivity reconciliation and GDS segment fee billing.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — GDS Connectivity; HTNG GDS Interface Standards

description: Defines the geographic reach of this booking source: global (worldwide distribution), regional (specific world region), domestic (single country), or property_specific (single property only). Used for channel distribution strategy and regional channel mix analysis.
reference: HTNG Channel Attribution Standards; WTTC Global Distribution Guidelines

business_glossary_term: International Air Transport Association (IATA) Agency Code
description: IATA-assigned numeric agency code for travel agency or OTA booking sources participating in IATA-governed distribution. Used for agency commission processing, GDS segment attribution, and travel agency performance reporting. Null for non-agency sources.
reference: IATA Agency Administration; Sabre SynXis CRS — Agency Management

description: Frequency at which room inventory and rate availability are pushed or synchronized to this booking source channel. Real-time channels receive instant updates via API; others receive batch updates. Impacts overbooking risk management and rate parity compliance.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Connectivity; IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls

description: Indicates whether this booking source is a direct brand channel (True) or an indirect intermediary channel (False). Direct channels include brand website, brand app, call center, and walk-in. Used for direct booking ratio KPI tracking and cost-of-acquisition benchmarking against indirect channels.
reference: AHLA Direct Booking Initiative; STR Channel Classification Standards

description: Indicates whether this booking source has Last Room Availability (LRA) contractual rights, meaning the channel must be offered inventory even when only the last room is available. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. Impacts inventory control rules in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; HTNG LRA Contract Standards

description: Defines the marketing attribution model applied to bookings from this source for campaign effectiveness measurement and marketing ROI (Return on Investment) reporting. Used by Salesforce CRM and marketing analytics to attribute revenue to marketing campaigns. 'none' for channels not subject to marketing attribution (e.g., GDS corporate).
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Attribution; AHLA Digital Marketing Standards

description: Defines the payment collection model for this booking source: merchant (OTA collects payment from guest and remits net to hotel), agency (hotel collects and pays commission to channel), net_rate (channel marks up a net rate), or direct_bill (corporate direct billing). Impacts revenue recognition, accounts receivable, and cash flow management.
reference: USALI — Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA AR/AP; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Source code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS for this booking source. Used for front desk reservation attribution, daily operations reporting (DOR), and property-level channel mix analysis. May differ from CRS source code due to property-level configuration.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Reservations Source Codes; USALI Channel Attribution

description: OTA or channel partner tier classification indicating the level of preferred partnership status. Preferred partners typically receive enhanced placement, lower commission rates, or co-marketing benefits. Used for OTA partnership management and channel strategy prioritization.
reference: AHLA OTA Partnership Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Partner Management

description: Indicates whether this booking source is subject to rate parity monitoring as part of the OTA contract or channel distribution agreement. When True, the Revenue Management System (RMS) and channel manager enforce rate parity rules to prevent undercutting on this channel. Critical for OTA contract compliance.
reference: AHLA OTA Partnership Guidelines; IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Parity Controls

description: Specifies the rate parity obligation for this booking source: full_parity (rates must match all channels), narrow_parity (rates must match other OTAs but may differ from direct), or no_parity (no rate parity obligation). Drives rate distribution rules in Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: AHLA OTA Partnership Guidelines; European Commission Narrow Parity Ruling; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Numeric sort order for displaying booking source records in PMS, CRS, and reporting tool dropdown lists and channel mix reports. Lower values appear first. Used for UI configuration in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS administration screens.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Source Code Administration; Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management

description: Standardized sub-channel category classifying the booking source by interface type and traveler segment. Drives cost-of-acquisition attribution, commission tier assignment, and channel mix optimization analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota_desktop|ota_mobile|gds_corporate|direct_web|direct_app|voice|group|wholesale — promote to reference product]
reference: HTNG (AHLA Technology) Channel Attribution Standards; STR (Smith Travel Research) Channel Classification

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the booking source within the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). This is the operational key used in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS for source attribution on reservations. Examples: 'EXPEDIA_WEB', 'GDS_AMADEUS', 'DIRECT_APP'.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; Oracle OPERA PMS — Reservations Source Codes

description: Detailed narrative description of the booking source, including its operational context, target traveler segment, and how it integrates with the distribution ecosystem. Used for onboarding documentation and channel strategy reference.

Human-readable display name of the booking source as used in reporting dashboards, revenue management analysis, and channel mix reports. Examples: 'Expedia Desktop', 'Amadeus GDS Corporate', 'Brand Direct App'.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; STR (Smith Travel Research) Channel Classification

description: Indicates whether this booking source is eligible to receive and display the Best Available Rate (BAR) as distributed through the CRS. Channels with full rate parity obligations typically receive BAR. Used in rate distribution configuration in Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Distribution; Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Availability

description: Indicates whether reservations booked through this source are eligible for loyalty program points accrual. Some OTA and wholesale channels contractually exclude loyalty point earning. Used by Salesforce CRM loyalty management module to determine points posting eligibility at check-in.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Loyalty Management; AHLA Loyalty Program Standards

description: Timestamp when this booking source record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability; SOX — Audit Trail Requirements

description: Primary key for channel

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Distribution channels incur operating costs (connectivity fees, commissions, marketing) that must be allocated to cost centers for USALI-compliant departmental P&L reporting and budget variance analys

Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Many distribution channels are operated by OTA partners (e.g., Expedia operates multiple channels/brands). This FK establishes the ownership relationship. Removes redundant platform_name which can be

Date on which this distribution channel was first activated and began accepting live bookings. Distinct from contract_start_date which reflects the commercial agreement date. Used for channel onboarding tracking and performance benchmarking from go-live.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether this channel is eligible to display and sell the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate at the time of booking. True = BAR rates are distributed to this channel; False = channel receives only specific contracted or negotiated rates. Drives rate distribution strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Per-transaction booking fee charged by the channel in addition to or instead of a commission percentage, expressed in USD. Common in GDS channels (e.g., GDS segment fee per booking). Used in cost-of-acquisition and channel profitability analysis.
reference: USALI; AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel (e.g., '24H_FREE', '48H_PENALTY', 'NON_REFUNDABLE'). Cancellation policies may differ by channel based on contractual agreements. Managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: High-level grouping of the channel by booking relationship: DIRECT = guest books directly with the property (web, mobile, voice, corporate direct); INDIRECT = third-party intermediary involved (OTA, GDS, wholesaler, metasearch); OPAQUE = rate and property identity concealed until after booking (e.g., Hotwire, Priceline opaque). Drives cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel mix strategy.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices; STR Channel Segmentation

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the distribution channel across enterprise systems including Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and IDeaS G3 RMS. Used for channel mapping and cross-system reconciliation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Free-text description of the distribution channel, including its market positioning, target guest segment, special contractual terms, or operational notes. Used for channel management documentation and onboarding reference.

Human-readable name of the distribution channel (e.g., 'Expedia Partner Central', 'Booking.com Extranet', 'Sabre GDS', 'Direct Web', 'Voice Reservations'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; AHLA Technology Standards

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the distribution channel. active = channel is live and accepting bookings; inactive = channel is configured but not currently selling; suspended = channel temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity violation, connectivity issue); pending_activation = channel onboarded but not yet live; decommissioned = channel permanently retired.
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Categorical classification of the distribution channel by its market segment and technology interface.
