OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo); DIRECT_WEB = brand.com web booking; DIRECT_MOBILE = brand mobile app; VOICE = call center reservations; CORPORATE_DIRECT = negotiated corporate accounts; WHOLESALER = tour operators and bed banks; METASEARCH = Google Hotel Ads, Trivago. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|DIRECT_WEB|DIRECT_MOBILE|VOICE|CORPORATE_DIRECT|WHOLESALER|METASEARCH — promote to reference product]
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; STR Channel Segmentation; AHLA

description: The commercial model under which commission is calculated for this channel. NET_RATE = channel marks up a net rate provided by the property; GROSS_RATE = commission deducted from the gross rate charged to the guest; MARKUP = channel applies a markup over the property's net rate; FLAT_FEE = fixed fee per booking regardless of rate. Critical for accurate revenue and cost-of-acquisition reporting.
reference: USALI; AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices; STR

description: Standard commission percentage charged by this distribution channel on each booking, expressed as a percentage of the room revenue (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Drives cost-of-acquisition calculations and net revenue reporting per USALI standards. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices

description: Annual or periodic connectivity/subscription fee charged by the channel or switch provider for maintaining the technical integration, expressed in USD. Included in total cost-of-acquisition analysis alongside commission. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
reference: USALI; SAP S/4HANA Procurement MM; AHLA

description: Technical integration method used to connect the property management system or CRS to this distribution channel. API = RESTful or SOAP API integration; XML = XML-based messaging (e.g., OTA XML); SFTP = file-based batch transfer; DIRECT_CONNECT = proprietary direct connection; SWITCH = via a channel manager switch (e.g., SiteMinder, Channex); MANUAL = manually managed extranet updates. Impacts SLA and rate update latency.
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Technology

description: Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities, policies) is actively distributed to this channel via a content API or content management system. True = content is pushed/synced to this channel; False = content is managed manually or not distributed. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels.
reference: HTNG Content Distribution Standards; AHLA Technology

description: Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal management and channel lifecycle planning. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Procurement MM; USALI; SOX Financial Controls

description: Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission audit trails, and regulatory compliance. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Procurement MM; USALI; SOX Financial Controls

description: Timestamp when this distribution channel record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management.
reference: SOX Financial Controls; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); AHLA

description: Channel identifier as configured in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the CRS source of record. Enables traceability of bookings back to the originating CRS channel configuration.

Date on which this distribution channel was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for currently active channels. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting, and audit trails. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; SOX

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processor Flag
description: Indicates whether this distribution channel acts as a GDPR data processor for guest personal data collected during the booking process. True = a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with this channel; False = channel does not process personal data on behalf of the property. Required for GDPR compliance and data governance.
reference: GDPR Article 28 (Data Processing Agreements); CCPA; AHLA Data Privacy Standards

description: Two-letter GDS chain code assigned to the hotel brand or property group within Global Distribution Systems (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, Worldspan). Required for GDS-type channels to route availability and rate requests correctly. Null for non-GDS channels.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG GDS Connectivity Standards; IATA GDS Standards

description: Geographic reach of this distribution channel. GLOBAL = channel operates worldwide; REGIONAL = channel serves a specific geographic region (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Europe); DOMESTIC = channel serves a single country; LOCAL = channel serves a specific city or metro area. Used for channel mix strategy and market segmentation analysis.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices; STR Channel Segmentation

description: Method by which room inventory is allocated to this distribution channel. POOLED = channel draws from the shared central inventory pool (real-time); ALLOTMENT = a fixed block of rooms is pre-allocated to the channel; FREE_SALE = channel can sell without prior allocation up to a defined limit; ON_REQUEST = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Indicates whether this channel has Last Room Availability (LRA) rights — meaning the property must make the last available room accessible through this channel at the contracted rate. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. True = LRA applies; False = LRA does not apply.
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Timestamp when this distribution channel record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Financial Controls; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); AHLA

description: Indicates whether bookings made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points accrual and redemption. True = loyalty points are awarded and redeemable; False = bookings through this channel do not qualify for loyalty benefits (common for opaque OTA channels). Managed in Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management; AHLA Loyalty Program Standards

business_glossary_term: Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE) Bookings Supported Flag
description: Indicates whether this distribution channel supports group and MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) bookings in addition to transient reservations. True = MICE/group bookings can be sourced through this channel; False = transient bookings only. Relevant for event sales and Delphi by Amadeus integration.
reference: Delphi by Amadeus; HTNG; AHLA MICE Standards

description: Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) products are distributed through this channel. True = NRR fences are available on this channel; False = only flexible/refundable rates are distributed. Impacts revenue management yield strategy and cancellation policy enforcement.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Defines who collects payment from the guest for bookings made through this channel. HOTEL_COLLECT = property collects payment directly at check-in/check-out; CHANNEL_COLLECT = the OTA or intermediary collects payment and remits net to the property; SPLIT = partial payment collected by each party. Impacts accounts receivable and revenue recognition per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.
reference: USALI; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance; AHLA

description: Indicates whether this distribution channel is certified as compliant with PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) for handling cardholder data. True = channel holds current PCI DSS certification; False = channel is not PCI compliant or certification has lapsed. Required for risk management and payment security governance.
reference: PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard); AHLA Technology

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market served by this distribution channel (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Used for geographic channel mix analysis, market penetration reporting, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU-based channels).

Numeric rank indicating the strategic priority of this channel in the distribution mix, where lower numbers indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority, typically direct web). Used by revenue management to determine inventory release sequencing and rate strategy. Configured in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this distribution channel contractually requires rate parity — i.e., the property must offer the same or lower rates on this channel as on any other channel. True = rate parity clause is active in the channel agreement; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management compliance and rate strategy.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Best Practices; EU Rate Parity Regulations; HTNG

description: Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation for this channel. BROAD = property must match rates across all channels including direct; NARROW = property must match rates on other OTAs but may offer lower rates on direct channels (common post-EU regulatory changes); NONE = no rate parity obligation. Drives revenue management and direct booking strategy.
reference: EU Rate Parity Regulations; AHLA; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Frequency at which the channel remits collected payments to the property for CHANNEL_COLLECT payment model bookings. Applicable only when payment_model is CHANNEL_COLLECT or SPLIT. Drives accounts receivable forecasting and cash flow management in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: USALI; SAP S/4HANA Finance AR; AHLA

business_glossary_term: Channel Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time (Seconds)
description: Maximum allowable response time in seconds for availability and rate queries from this channel, as defined in the channel's Service Level Agreement (SLA). Used to monitor channel connectivity performance and enforce SLA compliance. Typical values: 3 seconds for OTA/GDS, 5 seconds for XML channels.
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS SLA; AHLA Technology

business_glossary_term: Channel Service Level Agreement (SLA) Uptime Percentage
description: Contractually committed uptime percentage for this channel's connectivity, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.90 for 99.9% uptime). Used to monitor channel reliability and enforce SLA penalties. Managed against actual uptime metrics in channel performance reporting.
reference: HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS SLA; AHLA Technology

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes supported by this distribution channel for booking and payment (e.g., 'USD,EUR,GBP,JPY'). Relevant for multi-currency rate distribution and foreign exchange management. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; Sabre SynXis CRS; IFRS/GAAP

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 two-letter language codes supported by this channel for content display and guest communication (e.g., 'en,fr,de,zh,ja'). Relevant for content localization strategy and international market reach.
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes; HTNG Content Distribution Standards

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Channel Mapping ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each CRS-to-channel mapping configuration record in the Sabre SynXis CRS. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a configuration resource defining how a property's rates, room types, and inventory are translated and published to a specific distribution channel.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: CRS channel mappings define which booking source codes are used for reservations from each channel. Many mappings per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source_code which can be retrieved via F

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: crs_channel_mapping currently stores channel_contract_reference as a STRING column, which is an unresolved reference to a channel_contract record. Normalizing this to a proper FK (channel_contract_id
Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, voice, etc.) to which this mapping applies. Identifies the connected channel partner in the channel master.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module

description: Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: CRS channel mappings define how rate plans map between Sabre SynXis CRS and distribution channels. The crs_channel_mapping table has crs_rate_plan_code and channel_rate_plan_code (STRING). Adding chan

Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: CRS channel mappings can be specific to GDS connections when the channel is a GDS. Adding nullable gds_connection_id FK allows linking CRS mappings to specific GDS connection configurations (Sabre, Am

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: CRS channel mappings must link channel room type codes to physical inventory room types for rate loading, availability synchronization, and booking fulfillment. Essential for channel manager operation

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: CRS channel mappings are segment-specific for rate loading and inventory allocation (e.g., corporate rate codes mapped differently than leisure). FK enables proper segment-scoped mapping management an
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which this CRS channel mapping is configured. Links the mapping to the specific property in the property master.

Code identifying the cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel mapping (e.g., 'FLEX24H' for 24-hour free cancellation, 'NRR' for non-refundable). Determines the terms communicated to guests at the point of booking on the channel.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservation Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Policy Management

description: The property identifier code as recognized by the distribution channel (e.g., Booking.com hotel ID, Expedia property ID, GDS chain/property code). Used to match the CRS property to the channel's own property record during rate and availability pushes.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; GDS (Global Distribution System) Standards

description: Categorical classification of the distribution channel type. Determines the connectivity protocol, commission structure, and rate parity rules applicable to this mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|mobile|wholesaler|corporate|metasearch — promote to reference product]
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: The commission percentage charged by the distribution channel on bookings made through this mapping (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis, channel profitability reporting, and USALI-compliant distribution cost accounting. Classified as confidential due to contractual sensitivity.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; SAP S/4HANA Finance Module

description: Fixed or per-booking connectivity fee charged by the channel or GDS switch for maintaining the distribution connection, expressed in USD. Used in total cost-of-acquisition calculations and channel ROI analysis per USALI standards.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); Sabre SynXis CRS Connectivity Agreements

description: The technical messaging protocol used for rate and availability distribution between Sabre SynXis CRS and the channel (e.g., OTA XML, HTNG, OpenTravel Alliance ARI). Determines the message format and integration method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA_XML|HTNG|OpenTravel|ARI|TravelPort|Amadeus|direct_API — promote to reference product]
reference: HTNG (AHLA Technology) Connectivity Standards; OpenTravel Alliance Specifications

description: Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities) is synchronized to the channel through this mapping in addition to rates and availability. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels that require content distribution alongside rate/inventory feeds.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Content Distribution Standards

description: Timestamp when this CRS channel mapping configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with GDPR data processing records requirements.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 30 — Records of Processing Activities; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act)

description: The property identifier code as registered in Sabre SynXis CRS. This is the CRS-side property reference used in all rate and availability distribution messages to channels. Distinct from the internal property_id.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Central Reservations Module

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Rate Plan Code
description: The rate plan code as defined in Sabre SynXis CRS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate rate codes). This is the source-side rate plan identifier that is translated and mapped to the channel-specific rate plan code for distribution.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution Module; IDeaS G3 RMS Pricing Module

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are distributed to this channel (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency of rate publication and commission calculation for this channel mapping.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: The date from which this CRS channel mapping configuration becomes active and rates/availability begin distributing to the channel. Used to schedule future mapping activations and track the start of channel distribution periods.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Channel Connectivity Standards

description: The date on which this CRS channel mapping configuration expires and distribution to the channel ceases. Null indicates the mapping is open-ended with no scheduled end date. Used for contract-aligned channel distribution management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Channel Connectivity Standards

description: Code identifying the payment guarantee policy required for bookings through this channel mapping (e.g., 'CC_REQUIRED' for credit card guarantee, 'DEPOSIT' for advance deposit). Ensures PCI DSS-compliant payment handling requirements are communicated to the channel.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Cashiering Module; PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard); Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Defines how room inventory is allocated to this channel. 'pooled' means the channel draws from the shared inventory pool; 'allotment' means a fixed block is reserved for the channel; 'free_sell' means unlimited sell without allocation; 'on_request' means availability must be confirmed manually.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls Module; Sabre SynXis CRS Inventory Management

description: Indicates whether this channel mapping is configured to distribute mobile-specific rates or content optimized for mobile booking platforms. Supports mobile channel performance analytics and targeted mobile rate strategy.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; AHLA Digital Distribution Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent successful rate and availability synchronization push from Sabre SynXis CRS to the distribution channel for this mapping. Used to monitor channel connectivity health, detect stale distributions, and support SLA compliance reporting.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) SLA Standards

description: Indicates whether this channel mapping is configured with Last Room Availability (LRA), meaning the channel has access to the last available room in the property's inventory. LRA is typically granted to GDS corporate accounts and preferred OTA partners under contractual obligation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; AHLA Distribution Standards; GDS (Global Distribution System) LRA Contract Terms

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this CRS-to-channel mapping configuration within Sabre SynXis. Used as the business identifier for cross-system reference and audit. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module

description: Human-readable descriptive name for this CRS channel mapping configuration (e.g., 'Booking.com Standard Rate Mapping - NYC Midtown'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module

description: Current operational lifecycle status of this CRS channel mapping. 'active' means the mapping is live and distributing rates/availability; 'suspended' means temporarily halted; 'testing' means in QA validation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Channel Connectivity Standards

description: Classification of what this mapping configuration governs — whether it maps rate plans, room types, inventory controls, packages, or promotional offers to the channel. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Channel Connectivity Standards

description: Maximum number of days in advance that a booking can be made through this channel mapping (booking window). Controls how far into the future availability is published to the channel. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Maximum number of nights allowed for a booking through this channel mapping. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction. Used to control long-stay bookings on specific channels as part of yield optimization strategy.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Restrictions

description: Minimum number of days in advance that a booking must be made through this channel mapping. Used to enforce advance purchase restrictions (e.g., NRR non-refundable rates requiring 7-day advance booking) as distributed via the CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: Minimum number of nights required for a booking to be accepted through this channel mapping. A value of 1 means no minimum stay restriction. Used to enforce LOS (Length of Stay) controls as part of revenue management strategy distributed via the CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Restrictions

description: Classification of the rate type distributed through this channel mapping. Indicates whether the channel receives Best Available Rate (BAR), Last Room Availability (LRA) rates, Non-Refundable Rates (NRR), corporate negotiated rates, packages, promotional rates, or wholesale rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|lra|nrr|corporate|package|promotional|wholesale — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Pricing Module; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; AHLA Rate Standards

description: Defines whether a rate adjustment (offset) is applied to the CRS rate before distribution to this channel. 'none' means the CRS rate is published as-is; 'flat_amount' means a fixed currency amount is added or subtracted; 'percentage' means a percentage markup or markdown is applied.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution Module; IDeaS G3 RMS Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR)

description: The numeric value of the rate offset applied to the CRS rate before distribution to this channel. Positive values indicate a markup; negative values indicate a markdown. Interpreted as a currency amount or percentage depending on rate_offset_type. Null when rate_offset_type is 'none'.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution Module; IDeaS G3 RMS Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR)

description: Indicates whether rate parity rules are enforced for this channel mapping, requiring that the rates distributed to this channel match the Best Available Rate (BAR) published on other channels. Critical for OTA contract compliance and rate integrity monitoring.
reference: AHLA Rate Parity Guidelines; OTA (Online Travel Agency) Contract Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution

description: Maximum number of rooms that can be sold through this channel per night under the current allocation configuration. A null or zero value indicates no sell limit is applied (free sell). Used to control channel-level inventory exposure and prevent overbooking.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls Module; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: Configured frequency in minutes at which rate and availability updates are pushed from the CRS to this channel. Lower values indicate near-real-time distribution (e.g., 5 minutes for OTA partners); higher values indicate batch distribution schedules.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management Module; HTNG (AHLA Technology) Connectivity Standards

description: Timestamp when this CRS channel mapping configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Critical for detecting configuration drift in channel distribution settings.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 30; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); Sabre SynXis CRS Audit Log

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each GDS connection configuration record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master configuration entity.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: GDS connections are associated with specific booking source codes for attribution when reservations come through GDS channels. Many GDS connections per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source
Reference to the parent distribution channel record associated with this GDS connection, enabling channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: GDS connectivity fees (segment fees, monthly charges) are departmental operating expenses requiring cost center allocation for accurate distribution cost tracking and budget management per USALI stand

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: GDS connections incur monthly connectivity fees posted to specific GL ledger accounts. gds_connection already has cost_center_id but lacks ledger_id for direct GL account mapping. Finance teams requir

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: GDS connections are configured per market segment (corporate, consortia, leisure) for rate loading and segment fee billing. FK enables segment-level GDS performance analysis and rate loading complianc
Reference to the property for which this GDS connection is configured. Links the GDS connectivity record to the specific hotel or resort property.
Date on which this GDS connection was first activated and made live for bookings. Used for contract term tracking, connectivity health history, and channel distribution reporting.

Method by which room availability is synchronized between the property management system and the GDS. Real-time provides instant updates; batch updates on a scheduled interval; on_request queries availability at booking time; seamless provides live two-way availability.
reference: HTNG Availability Sync Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS GDS Interface

description: Two- to four-character hotel chain code registered with the GDS network, identifying the hotel brand or management company within the GDS. Assigned by the GDS provider and used for inventory and rate loading.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Chain Code Registry; HTNG GDS Connectivity Standards

description: Percentage commission payable to travel agents or GDS intermediaries on bookings made through this GDS connection. Used for commission accrual, accounts payable processing, and channel cost analysis per USALI standards.
reference: USALI Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry; GDS Agency Commission Agreement

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the GDS connection. Active indicates live booking capability; suspended indicates temporary halt; pending_activation indicates setup in progress; decommissioned indicates permanently retired.

Fixed monthly fee charged by the GDS provider or switch vendor for maintaining the technical connectivity of this GDS connection. Distinct from per-segment fees; used for OpEx budgeting and channel cost allocation.
reference: GDS Connectivity Agreement; USALI Channel Distribution Cost Standards

description: Technical connectivity model used for this GDS connection. Direct connect provides real-time two-way communication; switch connect routes through a switch provider; seamless provides full availability and rate transparency; standard uses batch updates.
reference: HTNG GDS Connectivity Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Technical Specifications

description: Expiration date of the GDS connectivity contract. Null for evergreen contracts. Used for renewal alerts, contract renegotiation planning, and channel distribution continuity management.
reference: GDS Distribution Agreement; USALI Contract Management

description: Effective start date of the GDS connectivity contract governing this connection. Used for contract lifecycle management, renewal planning, and financial obligation tracking.
reference: GDS Distribution Agreement; USALI Contract Management

description: Indicates whether negotiated corporate rates are loaded and accessible through this GDS connection. Corporate rates are a primary revenue stream for GDS channels and require specific rate plan configuration in the CRS.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Corporate Rate Configuration; Oracle OPERA PMS Corporate Accounts

description: Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the silver layer lakehouse.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SOX Internal Controls

description: Identifier for the CRS-to-GDS interface configuration within Sabre SynXis CRS. Links the GDS connection to the specific CRS interface instance managing rate distribution and availability synchronization.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are loaded and displayed to travel agents in this GDS connection. Ensures rate accuracy and currency consistency for international GDS distribution.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IATA GDS Rate Standards

description: Date on which this GDS connection was or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for currently active connections. Used for contract lifecycle management and channel distribution planning.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the property's display order in GDS search results for travel agents. Lower values indicate higher priority. Influenced by GDS tier, participation level, and performance metrics.
reference: GDS Merchandising Standards; AHLA Distribution Channel Guidelines

description: Human-readable name of the GDS provider (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, Worldspan, Travelport). Used for reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.
Industry-standard two-character GDS network identifier assigned by IATA/ARC. 1S=Sabre, 1A=Amadeus, 1G=Galileo/Travelport, 1P=Worldspan. Used for routing and segment fee attribution.
reference: IATA GDS Network Codes; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Participation tier level for this GDS connection, reflecting the level of GDS merchandising, display priority, and content features contracted. Preferred tier provides enhanced display and content capabilities; standard is base participation; basic is minimal listing.
reference: GDS Participation Agreement; AHLA Distribution Standards

description: Result of the most recent automated health check for this GDS connection. Healthy indicates full two-way connectivity; degraded indicates partial functionality; failed indicates connectivity loss; unknown indicates health check has not been performed.
reference: HTNG GDS Connectivity Health Monitoring Standards

business_glossary_term: Last GDS Connectivity Health Check Timestamp
description: Timestamp of the most recent automated connectivity health check performed for this GDS connection. Used for SLA monitoring, connectivity uptime reporting, and proactive issue detection.
reference: HTNG GDS Connectivity Health Monitoring Standards; SLA Management

description: Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is enabled for this GDS connection, requiring the property to make the last available room accessible to GDS bookers. LRA is a contractual obligation with some GDS providers and corporate accounts.
reference: AHLA GDS Contract Standards; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Maximum number of days in advance that reservations can be booked through this GDS connection. Controls the booking window loaded to the GDS and aligns with the Revenue Management System (RMS) inventory control horizon.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Control Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Booking Window

description: Minimum number of days in advance that reservations must be booked through this GDS connection. Supports Last Room Availability (LRA) and advance purchase rate controls managed by the Revenue Management System.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Control; Sabre SynXis CRS Booking Window

description: Free-text operational notes regarding this GDS connection, including special configuration requirements, known issues, contractual exceptions, or migration history. Used by channel distribution and revenue management teams.

Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) can be loaded and booked through this GDS connection. NRR support is a key revenue management capability enabling advance purchase rate strategies via GDS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Strategy; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Configuration

description: The unique property identifier as registered within the specific GDS network. May differ from the internal PMS property code. Used for inventory display and booking routing within the GDS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS GDS Interface; Sabre SynXis CRS Property Configuration

description: GDS-assigned Pseudo City Code (PCC) that identifies the travel agency or booking office within the GDS network. Used for commission tracking, segment attribution, and agency relationship management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS PCC Configuration; IATA GDS Standards

description: The technical protocol or standard used to load and update rate plans into the GDS. HTNG and OTA are XML-based standards; DHISCO is a switch-based protocol; manual indicates rates are loaded via GDS terminal; API indicates direct programmatic loading.
reference: HTNG Rate Loading Standards; OTA XML Specification

description: Indicates whether rate parity is contractually enforced for this GDS connection, requiring that rates loaded to the GDS match the Best Available Rate (BAR) offered on direct channels. Critical for OTA and GDS contract compliance monitoring.
reference: AHLA Rate Parity Guidelines; GDS Distribution Agreement

description: Number of distinct rate plans currently loaded and active for this GDS connection. Used to monitor rate plan coverage, identify gaps in GDS rate distribution, and support rate parity audits.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Loading

description: Frequency at which rate updates are pushed from the Revenue Management System (RMS) or CRS to this GDS connection. Real-time supports dynamic pricing; scheduled frequencies reflect batch update configurations.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Distribution; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Update Configuration

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the GDS segment fee. Enables multi-currency cost-of-acquisition reporting for properties operating in non-USD markets.

Per-booking segment fee charged by the GDS provider in US dollars for each reservation booked through this GDS connection. A GDS segment is one hotel booking transaction. Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.
reference: GDS Segment Fee Agreement; USALI Channel Cost Standards

description: Email address for the GDS provider's technical support team for this connection. Used for connectivity issue escalation, health check alerts, and SLA breach notifications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; GDS Support SLA Agreement

description: Name of the third-party switch provider (e.g., DHISCO, TravelClick, Pegasus) used to route availability and rate data between the CRS and the GDS, when connectivity_type is switch_connect. Null for direct connect configurations.
Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance in the silver layer lakehouse.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SOX Internal Controls

business_glossary_term: GDS Connectivity Uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) Percentage
description: Contractually agreed minimum uptime percentage for this GDS connection as specified in the GDS connectivity SLA. Used for vendor performance monitoring and contract compliance reporting.
reference: GDS Connectivity SLA Agreement; HTNG Technology Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the OTA partner record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the ota_partner master data product.
reference: HTNG Data Dictionary; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: OTA partnerships are brand-specific in multi-brand hospitality companies; Marriott Bonvoy partners differ from Ritz-Carlton partners. Brand-level partnership management, commission structures, and con

Indicates whether the OTA partner operates an affiliate or sub-distribution network through which third-party websites or agents can book on behalf of the OTA. True = affiliate network active; False = direct OTA platform only. Relevant for booking source attribution accuracy.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Standard commission percentage charged by the OTA partner on each confirmed booking, expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Foundational input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel profitability analysis.
reference: USALI Channel Cost Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Finance GL; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Default cancellation policy type applied to bookings made through this OTA channel. Flexible = free cancellation up to 24-48 hours; Moderate = partial penalty; Strict = high penalty; Non_Refundable = NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) — no refund on cancellation. Impacts revenue management and guest experience.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations; IDeaS G3 RMS; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Name of the channel manager or switch platform used to manage the technical connectivity to this OTA partner (e.g., Sabre SynXis, Siteminder, Amadeus iHotelier, RateGain). Relevant when connectivity is not direct API-to-API.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Distribution Channel Taxonomy

description: Commercial model governing how the OTA partner earns revenue. Net Rate = hotel sells at net, OTA marks up; Merchant = OTA collects full payment and remits net; Agency = OTA collects commission on hotel-collected payment; Retail = standard published rate with commission; Opaque = rate hidden from consumer until post-booking (e.g., Hotwire).
reference: USALI Channel Cost Accounting; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; HTNG Distribution Channel Taxonomy

description: Frequency at which the connectivity fee is billed by the OTA partner or channel manager. Annual = yearly flat fee; Monthly = recurring monthly; Per_Booking = transactional fee per confirmed booking; One_Time = setup/onboarding fee only.
reference: USALI Channel Cost Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Finance AP; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Annual or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the OTA partner or channel manager for maintaining the technical integration (API/XML/GDS switch). Expressed in USD. Used for total cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation and channel profitability reporting per USALI.
reference: USALI Channel Cost Accounting; SAP S/4HANA Finance GL; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Technical protocol used for two-way inventory, rate, and availability (IRA) connectivity between the hotel's CRS/PMS and the OTA partner. XML = legacy XML push/pull; REST_API = modern RESTful API; GDS = Global Distribution System switch; SFTP = file-based batch; OTA_XML = OpenTravel Alliance XML standard; HTNG = HTNG web services.
reference: HTNG Web Services Standard; OpenTravel Alliance (OTA) XML Schema; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the completeness and quality of the hotel's content (photos, descriptions, amenities) on the OTA partner's platform. Higher scores correlate with improved search ranking and conversion rates. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; Medallia Experience Analytics

description: Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner becomes legally binding and operational. Governs the start of commission obligations and rate parity clauses.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Procurement MM; IFRS/GAAP; SOX Section 302

description: Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates an evergreen/open-ended contract. Used for contract renewal management and risk monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Procurement MM; IFRS/GAAP; SOX Section 302

description: Unique reference number of the master distribution agreement or contract executed with the OTA partner. Links to the contract management repository in SAP S/4HANA for financial and legal audit purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Procurement MM; SOX Section 302; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Timestamp when the OTA partner record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement (DSA) is in place with the OTA partner governing the exchange of guest booking data, anonymised analytics, and performance reporting. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance when guest PII is transmitted.
reference: GDPR Article 28; CCPA; AHLA Data Privacy Guidelines; PCI DSS

description: Indicates whether the OTA partner is formally designated as a GDPR Data Processor under Article 28, processing guest personal data on behalf of the hotel as Data Controller. True = Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed; False = not designated as processor.
reference: GDPR Article 28; AHLA Data Privacy Guidelines; CCPA

description: Two-letter chain code assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) to identify the hotel brand/chain within GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Applicable only when connectivity_protocol is GDS. Null for non-GDS OTA partners.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG GDS Connectivity Standards; IATA GDS Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the OTA partner's legal headquarters is registered. Used for tax treaty application, withholding tax determination, and regulatory jurisdiction identification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS/GAAP; GDPR Article 4; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Model governing how room inventory is made available to the OTA partner. Free_Sell = real-time availability from CRS with no pre-allocated block; Allotment = pre-contracted room block allocated to the OTA; On_Request = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) clause, requiring the hotel to make its last available room accessible to this channel. LRA clauses impact revenue management inventory controls and are tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; HTNG Distribution Channel Taxonomy

description: Full registered legal name of the OTA partner organisation as it appears on the master distribution agreement (e.g., Expedia, Inc.; Booking Holdings B.V.). Used for contract management, AP reconciliation, and regulatory filings.
reference: IFRS/GAAP; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Description of the primary geographic markets or regions where the OTA partner has significant consumer reach and booking volume (e.g., 'North America, Western Europe'; 'Asia-Pacific'; 'Global'). Used for channel mix optimisation and market-specific distribution strategy.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; WTTC Tourism Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether the OTA partner operates a dedicated mobile application channel through which bookings are made. True = mobile app channel is active and tracked separately for attribution; False = desktop/web only. Supports channel mix and mobile booking trend analysis.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Business email address of the OTA partner's designated account manager. Used for commercial communications, promotional campaign coordination, and escalation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Account Management

description: Full name of the OTA partner's designated account manager or market manager assigned to the hotel account. Primary point of contact for commercial negotiations, promotional campaigns, and issue resolution.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Business phone number of the OTA partner's designated account manager. Used for urgent escalations and commercial negotiations. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Account Management

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the OTA partner within the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Used for channel mapping, rate distribution, and booking source attribution in Sabre SynXis and Oracle OPERA.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations

description: Current lifecycle status of the OTA partner relationship. Active = live and distributing inventory; Pending = contract under negotiation or connectivity setup in progress; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity breach); Terminated = contract ended.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Classification of the distribution partner by channel category. OTA = Online Travel Agency (Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (Sabre, Amadeus); METASEARCH = price comparison engine (Google Hotel Ads, Trivago); DIRECT = brand.com or call centre; WHOLESALE = tour operator/bed bank; AFFILIATE = sub-affiliate network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|METASEARCH|DIRECT|WHOLESALE|AFFILIATE — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; HTNG Distribution Channel Taxonomy

description: Identifies which party collects payment from the guest. Hotel = property collects at check-in/check-out (agency model); OTA = OTA collects full payment and remits net to hotel (merchant model); Split = partial payment collected by each party.
reference: PCI DSS; USALI Channel Cost Accounting; Oracle OPERA PMS Cashiering

description: Negotiated commission rate applicable when the property holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with the OTA. Typically lower than the base rate in exchange for enhanced visibility or placement. Null if preferred status is not applicable.
reference: USALI Channel Cost Accounting; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether the hotel or brand holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with this OTA, typically in exchange for a higher commission rate or marketing investment. Preferred partners receive enhanced search ranking and visibility on the OTA platform.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the OTA partner's primary consumer market or country of dominant booking volume (e.g., USA, GBR, SGP). Used for geographic channel attribution and market penetration analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTTC Tourism Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a rate parity clause requiring the hotel to offer rates no lower on other channels than those offered on this OTA. True = rate parity clause is active; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management and legal compliance monitoring.
reference: AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; EU Competition Law (rate parity regulations); HTNG Rate Parity Monitoring

description: Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation. Broad = hotel cannot offer lower rates on ANY channel including its own website; Narrow = hotel cannot offer lower rates on OTHER OTAs but may offer lower rates on its own direct channels; None = no parity obligation. Relevant for EU regulatory compliance following rate parity investigations.
reference: EU Competition Law; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; HTNG Rate Parity Monitoring

description: Number of days after the guest check-out date within which the OTA partner remits commission payments or net booking proceeds to the hotel. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., 30 = net-30 terms).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance AP/AR; USALI Channel Cost Accounting; IFRS/GAAP

description: Total number of guest reviews published on the OTA partner's platform for the hotel. Used alongside review_score to assess statistical significance and platform engagement. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.
reference: Medallia Guest Feedback; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Average guest review score for the hotel as published on the OTA partner's platform (e.g., Booking.com score out of 10, Expedia score out of 5). Used for reputation management, competitive benchmarking, and NPS/GSS correlation analysis via Medallia.
reference: Medallia Guest Feedback; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards; STR STAR Report

description: VAT, GST, or equivalent tax registration number of the OTA partner's legal entity. Required for tax invoice processing, withholding tax compliance, and AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance GL/AP; IFRS/GAAP; SOX Section 302; Local Tax Regulations

description: Consumer-facing brand name under which the OTA partner operates (e.g., Hotels.com, Agoda). May differ from the legal entity name when the OTA operates multiple consumer brands under one legal umbrella.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; AHLA Channel Distribution Standards

description: Timestamp when the OTA partner record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each channel-specific rate plan configuration record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: HTNG Data Dictionary; Oracle Hospitality OPERA PMS Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Promotional rate plans operationalize marketing offers; a "Summer Sale" campaign offer manifests as specific rate plans loaded into distribution channels. Linking rate plans to offers enables offer pe

Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rate plans are account-specific. Linking channel_rate_plan to corporate_account enables rate entitlement validation at booking, contract compliance auditing, and corporate rate lo

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Each channel-specific rate plan configuration belongs to one distribution channel. Many rate plans per channel. Removes redundant channel_code, channel_name, and channel_type which can be retrieved vi

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Channel rate plans drive revenue postings to specific GL ledger accounts (e.g., BAR revenue vs. OTA net rate). Finance teams map rate plan types to ledger accounts for revenue recognition and USALI de
Identifier of the hotel property for which this channel rate plan configuration applies. Enables property-level rate plan and channel management across a multi-property portfolio.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Property Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Property Setup

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Channel rate plans are the channel-facing distribution of revenue rate plans. FK from channel_rate_plan to revenue_rate_plan enables rate derivation tracking, parity compliance verification, and ensur

Minimum number of days in advance of arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on this channel. For example, a value of 7 means the guest must book at least 7 days before check-in. Supports advance purchase rate strategies and demand management.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Advance Purchase Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS Booking Window; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: Name or identifier of the revenue management or distribution team member who approved this channel rate plan configuration for publication. Supports governance, approval workflow tracking, and audit compliance for rate distribution decisions.
reference: SOX Section 404 (internal controls); AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Approval Workflow

description: The base room rate amount (in property currency) from which the channel-specific rate is derived. For BAR-derived plans, this is the current BAR value. For flat rates, this is the fixed rate. Expressed per room per night. Supports rate parity monitoring and channel pricing strategy analysis.
reference: USALI Room Revenue Reporting; IDeaS G3 RMS Base Rate; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading; AHLA Rate Standards

description: The last calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the closing of the booking window. Nullable if the rate plan remains bookable indefinitely until the stay date.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Booking Window Configuration; IDeaS G3 RMS Demand Management

description: The earliest calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the opening of the booking window for this rate plan on this channel. Used for promotional and advance purchase rate plan management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Booking Window Configuration; IDeaS G3 RMS Demand Management

description: Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy linked to this channel rate plan. Examples: FLEX24H (free cancellation up to 24 hours), NRR-NOCANCEL (non-refundable, no cancellation), SEMI-FLEX48H (partial refund if cancelled 48+ hours prior). Critical for NRR and advance purchase rate plans.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Cancellation Policy Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Cancellation Rules; AHLA Guest Policy Standards; GDPR Article 13 (consumer rights disclosure)

description: The final channel-specific rate amount (in property currency) published to this distribution channel after applying the rate derivation method and adjustment. This is the rate visible to consumers on the channel. Per room per night. Core metric for rate parity compliance monitoring.
reference: USALI Room Revenue Reporting; Sabre SynXis CRS Published Rate; HTNG Rate Parity Standards; PCI DSS (rate data confidentiality)

description: Current lifecycle status of the channel rate plan configuration. Active = currently published and bookable on the channel; Inactive = deactivated and not available; Suspended = temporarily paused (e.g., rate parity violation); Pending = awaiting activation or approval; Expired = past effective end date; Draft = configured but not yet submitted for distribution.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Status Management; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Lifecycle

description: Number of days before arrival after which this rate plan is closed out and no longer bookable on this channel. Complements advance_purchase_days to define the booking window. A value of 0 means the rate is bookable up to the day of arrival.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Close-Out Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Close-Out; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: The commission percentage payable to the distribution channel (e.g., OTA, GDS, travel agent) for bookings made under this rate plan. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Critical for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.
reference: USALI Channel Cost Reporting; SAP S/4HANA AP Commission Processing; AHLA Distribution Cost Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Commission Configuration

description: Fixed fee amount (in property currency) charged by the channel or connectivity provider per booking or per period for distributing this rate plan on the channel. Distinct from commission (which is percentage-based). Supports total cost of acquisition calculation.
reference: USALI Channel Cost Reporting; SAP S/4HANA AP Processing; Sabre SynXis CRS Connectivity Pricing

description: The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data accountability); SOX Section 404 (audit trail); SAP S/4HANA Audit Logging

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Rate Plan ID
description: The rate plan identifier as assigned in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRS. Enables traceability of rate plan configurations back to the source system.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Plan Management; HTNG System Integration Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rate amounts on this channel rate plan. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, AED. Supports multi-currency channel distribution and rate parity analysis across international markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Multi-Currency Reporting; Sabre SynXis CRS Currency Configuration

description: A 7-character binary string indicating which days of the week (Mon-Sun) this rate plan is applicable on the channel. Each position represents a day: position 1=Monday through position 7=Sunday. '1' = applicable, '0' = not applicable. Example: '1111100' = Monday through Friday only. Supports weekday/weekend rate differentiation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Day-of-Week Rate Controls; IDeaS G3 RMS Day Pattern Restrictions; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: The date from which this channel rate plan configuration becomes active and the rate plan is published to the channel. Defines the start of the rate loading schedule for this channel-rate plan combination.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading Schedule; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Dates; HTNG Rate Distribution Standards

description: The date on which this channel rate plan configuration expires and the rate plan is no longer published to the channel. Nullable for open-ended configurations. Supports rate loading schedule management and seasonal rate plan publishing.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading Schedule; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Dates; HTNG Rate Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether this channel rate plan is a package rate that bundles room accommodation with additional inclusions (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, F&B voucher). True = package rate with inclusions; False = room-only rate. Supports package revenue attribution and channel mix analysis.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Package Rate Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Package Rate Distribution; USALI Package Revenue Reporting

description: Indicates whether rate parity rules apply to this channel rate plan configuration. True = this rate plan is subject to rate parity monitoring and must match rates across applicable channels; False = exempt from parity (e.g., opaque rates, member-only rates, negotiated corporate rates). Supports rate parity compliance management.
reference: HTNG Rate Parity Standards; EU Rate Parity Regulations; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Parity Controls; AHLA Distribution Policy

description: Indicates whether bookings made under this channel rate plan are eligible for a refund upon cancellation. True = refundable (flexible rate); False = non-refundable (NRR). Key attribute for consumer transparency and rate plan classification.
reference: AHLA Guest Policy Standards; GDPR Article 13 (consumer rights); Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Attributes

description: The date and time when this rate plan was most recently successfully loaded or updated on the distribution channel. Supports rate loading schedule management, troubleshooting, and audit of channel rate distribution operations.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading Audit; HTNG Channel Connectivity Standards

description: Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under this rate plan on this channel. Nullable if no maximum stay restriction applies. Used in conjunction with min_los to define the eligible stay window for this channel rate plan.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS LOS Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS Stay Restrictions; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: Code indicating the meal plan inclusion for this channel rate plan. RO = Room Only; BB = Bed and Breakfast; HB = Half Board (breakfast + dinner); FB = Full Board (all meals); AI = All Inclusive; EP = European Plan (no meals); CP = Continental Plan (continental breakfast). Relevant for package rate plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RO|BB|HB|FB|AI|EP|CP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: HTNG Meal Plan Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Package Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Meal Plan Codes; IH&RA Accommodation Standards

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this rate plan on this channel. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction. Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions are a key Revenue Management System (RMS) tool for yield optimization.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS LOS Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS Stay Restrictions; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this channel rate plan configuration. Examples: rate loading exceptions, channel-specific negotiation notes, promotional campaign references, or revenue strategy rationale.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Notes; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Comments

business_glossary_term: Property Management System (PMS) Rate Plan Code
description: The rate plan code as configured in Oracle OPERA Property Management System (PMS). Used for reconciliation between CRS channel distribution and PMS rate plan setup. Ensures rate plan consistency between the CRS and PMS for accurate reservation processing.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS-PMS Integration; HTNG System Integration Standards

description: Indicates whether the channel-specific pricing adjustment is a markup (rate higher than base, e.g., to cover OTA commission) or a markdown (rate lower than base, e.g., promotional discount). NONE indicates no adjustment is applied and the base rate is published as-is.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Pricing; IDeaS G3 RMS Channel Strategy; HTNG Rate Parity Standards

description: Specifies whether the rate_adjustment_value is expressed as a PERCENTAGE of the base rate or as a fixed AMOUNT in the property currency. Determines how the channel-specific rate is computed from the base rate and adjustment value.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Pricing Rules; IDeaS G3 RMS Pricing Adjustments

description: The numeric value of the rate adjustment applied to derive the channel-specific rate from the base rate. Interpretation depends on rate_adjustment_unit: if PERCENTAGE, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%); if AMOUNT, this is the absolute currency amount. Used in conjunction with rate_adjustment_type to compute the final channel rate.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Pricing Rules; IDeaS G3 RMS Pricing Adjustments

description: The method used to calculate the channel-specific rate from the base rate. FLAT = fixed absolute rate amount; PERCENTAGE = percentage markup or markdown applied to a base rate; BAR_DERIVED = derived as a percentage or offset from the Best Available Rate (BAR); YIELD_MANAGED = dynamically set by the Revenue Management System (RMS); NEGOTIATED_FIXED = fixed contracted rate for corporate or wholesale agreements.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Derivation Logic; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Derivation; HTNG Rate Distribution Standards

description: Operational status of the rate plan loading process to the channel. Loaded = successfully transmitted and active on the channel; Pending Load = queued for transmission to the channel; Load Failed = transmission error requiring remediation; Not Loaded = not yet submitted; Deactivated = previously loaded but now removed from the channel.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading Operations; HTNG Channel Connectivity Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Distribution

description: Code identifying the rate parity group to which this channel rate plan belongs. Rate plans within the same parity group are monitored for price consistency across channels. Enables grouping of comparable rate plans for parity compliance reporting.
reference: HTNG Rate Parity Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Parity Configuration; EU Rate Parity Regulations

description: The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as configured in the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Examples: BAR, NRR, LRA, CORP001, PKG-BB. This is the business identifier used across Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate loading and distribution.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Management; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Configuration; HTNG Rate Distribution Standards

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the rate plan as displayed in the CRS and channel interfaces. Examples: 'Best Available Rate', 'Non-Refundable Rate', 'Last Room Availability Corporate', 'Bed and Breakfast Package'.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Configuration

description: Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR = Best Available Rate (dynamic public rate); NRR = Non-Refundable Rate (discounted, no cancellation); LRA = Last Room Availability (guaranteed availability for corporate); CORPORATE = negotiated corporate rate; PACKAGE = bundled rate with inclusions; PROMOTIONAL = limited-time promotional rate; WHOLESALE = net rate for tour operators; NEGOTIATED = contracted rate; OPAQUE = channel-opaque pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAR|NRR|LRA|CORPORATE|PACKAGE|PROMOTIONAL|WHOLESALE|NEGOTIATED|OPAQUE — promote to reference product]
reference: USALI Rate Classification; IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Type Taxonomy; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Plan Types; AHLA Rate Standards

description: Comma-separated list of room type codes to which this channel rate plan applies. If null or 'ALL', the rate plan applies to all room types at the property. Examples: 'STD,DLX,STE' or 'ALL'. Supports room-type-specific channel pricing strategies.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Type Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Plan Room Type Mapping; HTNG Room Type Standards

description: The latest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings must have an arrival date on or before this date. Nullable for open-ended rate plans. Supports seasonal and promotional rate plan management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Stay Date Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Stay Dates; IDeaS G3 RMS Seasonal Pricing

description: The earliest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings made under this rate plan must have an arrival date on or after this date. Supports seasonal rate plan publishing and blackout date management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Stay Date Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Plan Stay Dates; IDeaS G3 RMS Seasonal Pricing

description: The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data accountability); SOX Section 404 (audit trail); Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Capture

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory allocation record in the channel distribution system. Primary key for the inventory_allocation data product.
reference: HTNG Data Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.allotment. Business justification: Channel inventory allocations are operationalized from formal allotment contracts. Revenue managers reconcile channel-level allocation pickup against contracted allotment quantities for performance re

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Marketing campaigns (e.g., flash sales, loyalty promotions) require dedicated inventory allocations on specific channels. Revenue management teams create channel inventory blocks tied to campaigns; th
Reference to the rate plan associated with this inventory allocation. Determines which pricing rules apply to the allocated inventory on this channel.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: RMS-generated channel inventory allocations are directly driven by demand forecasts (is_rms_generated flag exists). FK links each allocation to the forecast record that triggered it — essential for RM

Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Revenue managers create channel inventory allocations specifically tied to MICE group blocks (group allotments). This link enables inventory control teams to track which allocation record governs a sp
Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, voice, etc.) to which this inventory allocation applies. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: Reference to the room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe double, suite) for which inventory is being allocated to the channel.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Type Configuration; HTNG Data Standards

description: Reference to the property for which inventory is being allocated. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Property Configuration; HTNG Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Channel inventory allocations are tactical executions of revenue strategies. Linking allocations to strategy enables strategy-level performance tracking — did the channel allocation decisions achieve

The type of inventory control action that generated this record. CREATE indicates a new allocation was established; UPDATE modifies an existing allocation; CLOSE applies a stop-sell or close-out; REOPEN lifts a restriction; RELEASE returns unsold inventory to the pool; OVERRIDE applies a manual revenue management override.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: Current operational status of this channel inventory allocation. Active allocations are enforced by the channel management system.
The total number of room nights (units) assigned to this channel for the specified stay date range and room type. Represents the allotment block size or sell limit granted to the channel.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) to uniquely identify this inventory allocation action. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Central Reservations; Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations

description: Current lifecycle state of the inventory allocation record. ACTIVE indicates the allocation is live and controlling channel availability; INACTIVE means it has been superseded; EXPIRED means the stay date range has passed; CANCELLED means it was manually revoked; PENDING means it is awaiting activation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: Method by which inventory is allocated to this channel for this room type. POOLED = shared inventory pool, ALLOCATED = fixed allocation, FREESALE = unlimited access, BLOCKED = no access.

Classification of the inventory allotment method applied to this channel. FREE_SELL allows the channel to sell without a fixed block; ALLOCATION assigns a fixed block of rooms; STOP_SELL halts sales on the channel; CLOSE_OUT removes all availability; ON_REQUEST requires manual confirmation before booking is confirmed.
reference: HTNG Data Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: The username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system user who applied this inventory control action. Used for audit trail, accountability, and change management reporting.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; HTNG Data Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The number of room nights currently available for sale on this channel, calculated as allocated_units minus consumed_units. Represents the real-time sellable inventory for the channel on the specified stay dates.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: The number of bookings cancelled on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Used to track net pickup and assess channel-level cancellation patterns for demand forecasting.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations; HTNG Data Standards

description: Percentage of total room type inventory allocated to this channel. Used for allocation-based inventory management strategies.

The category of distribution channel for this allocation. OTA (Online Travel Agency), GDS (Global Distribution System), DIRECT_WEB (brand.com), VOICE (call center), GSO (Global Sales Office), WHOLESALE (tour operators/wholesalers), METASEARCH (Google, Trivago, etc.). Supports channel mix optimization analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|gso|wholesale|metasearch — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards; AHLA Distribution Standards

description: The commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.15 for 15%) payable to the distribution channel for bookings made under this allocation. Used for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance; USALI; OTA Partnership Agreements; AHLA Distribution Standards

description: The fixed or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the GDS or CRS for distributing inventory through this channel. Expressed in the property's local currency. Used for total cost of distribution analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance; USALI; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Distribution Standards

description: The number of allocated room nights that have been booked or consumed by the channel as of the last system update. Used to calculate remaining availability and monitor channel pickup performance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.
The date and time when this inventory allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5; HTNG Data Standards

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial fields (connectivity_fee) in this allocation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency reporting across global properties.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: The number of days between the allocation action date and the stay_date_from (booking window). Used in revenue management analysis to understand how far in advance inventory controls are being applied relative to the arrival date.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting; HTNG Data Standards

description: Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration expires. Null for currently active allocations.
Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration becomes effective.

The date and time when this inventory allocation action became effective and was applied to the channel. Represents the business event timestamp when the allocation, stop-sell, or restriction was activated in the CRS.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; HTNG Data Standards
