description: Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the first date of the stay period for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check in on the stay_date_from date, preventing new bookings that start on that date.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; HTNG Data Standards

description: Indicates whether departures are blocked on the stay_date_to date for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check out on that date, preventing bookings that end on that date.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; HTNG Data Standards

description: Indicates whether this inventory allocation action was automatically generated by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) as opposed to being manually entered by a revenue manager. Supports analysis of automation adoption and RMS recommendation acceptance rates.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Infor EzRMS Revenue Strategy; HTNG Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a stop-sell control has been applied to this channel for the specified stay dates and room type. When TRUE, the channel is prevented from accepting new bookings regardless of remaining available units.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: Timestamp when this allocation record was last updated.

Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is contractually required for this channel. When TRUE, the channel must be offered the last available room at the property, typically a contractual obligation with certain OTA or corporate accounts.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards; OTA Partnership Agreements

description: The date and time when this inventory allocation action was lifted, reversed, or superseded. Null if the allocation is still active. Used to track the full lifecycle of each inventory control action.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; HTNG Data Standards

description: Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this room-type-channel combination, overriding property-level LRA settings.
The maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MAX_LOS. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction is applied.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; HTNG Data Standards

description: Channel-specific maximum length of stay restriction for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.
The minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MIN_LOS. A value of 1 means no effective minimum restriction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; HTNG Data Standards

description: Channel-specific minimum length of stay requirement for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.

The number of additional room nights above the physical inventory that this channel is permitted to sell as part of the property's overbooking strategy. A value of 0 means no overbooking is permitted on this channel.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Data Standards

description: Maximum overbooking percentage allowed for this room type on this channel. Used for revenue management optimization.
The number of new bookings received on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Supports pickup reporting and demand forecasting in the Revenue Management System (RMS).
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Pickup Reporting; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting; STR Benchmarking Standards

description: Priority ranking for this channel when allocating inventory for this room type. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Indicates whether rate parity rules are actively enforced for this room type on this channel.

Indicates whether rate parity compliance is enforced for this channel allocation. When TRUE, the allocated inventory must be offered at rates consistent with other channels per rate parity agreements. Used for OTA contract compliance monitoring.
reference: AHLA Distribution Standards; OTA Partnership Agreements; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution

description: Standardized code identifying the business reason for the inventory control action (e.g., HIGH_DEMAND, GROUP_BLOCK, OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION, RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION, CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE, MAINTENANCE). Used for revenue management analysis and audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIGH_DEMAND|GROUP_BLOCK|OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION|RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION|CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE|MAINTENANCE|FORECAST_ADJUSTMENT|COMPETITIVE_RESPONSE — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; USALI; HTNG Data Standards

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the inventory control action beyond the standardized reason_code. Captures specific business justification entered by the revenue manager or system.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; HTNG Data Standards

description: The date on which any unsold allocated inventory is released back to the general property inventory pool. After this date, the channel no longer holds exclusive rights to the allocated units, enabling the property to sell them through other channels.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: The type of booking restriction applied to this inventory allocation. MIN_LOS enforces a minimum Length of Stay (LOS); MAX_LOS enforces a maximum LOS; CLOSED_TO_ARRIVAL (CTA) prevents new arrivals on the specified date; CLOSED_TO_DEPARTURE (CTD) prevents departures; FULL_PATTERN_LENGTH requires exact stay duration; NONE means no restriction is applied.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; HTNG Data Standards

description: The maximum number of room nights the channel is permitted to sell regardless of total property availability. Acts as a cap to prevent overselling on a specific channel and supports channel mix optimization.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Data Standards

description: The operational system of record that originated this inventory allocation action. Identifies whether the action was initiated by Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, or entered manually by a user.
reference: HTNG Data Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: The first date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the start of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations; Sabre SynXis CRS Central Reservations; HTNG Data Standards

description: The last date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the end of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservations; Sabre SynXis CRS Central Reservations; HTNG Data Standards

description: The date and time when this inventory allocation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the allocation for audit trail and change management purposes.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5; HTNG Data Standards

description: Monotonically incrementing version counter for this inventory allocation record. Incremented each time the allocation is modified, enabling optimistic concurrency control and historical version tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: HTNG Data Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake Versioning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each rate parity audit record captured during channel monitoring. Primary key for this transactional audit entity.
reference: HTNG Data Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Rate parity compliance audits must distinguish authorized campaign-offer-driven rate deviations from true parity violations. Auditors and channel managers need to validate whether an observed rate dis

Reference to the OTA or distribution channel contract under which parity obligations are defined. Links to the channel contract master record for commission rates, parity clauses, and dispute resolution terms.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, etc.) on which the audited rate was observed. Links to the channel master record.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Reference to the property for which the rate parity audit was conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Property Standards

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Rate parity audits compare channel-observed rates against the source revenue rate plan. FK enables direct compliance verification: was the channel rate within tolerance of the contracted revenue_rate_
Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard) for which the rate was observed during the parity audit.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Room Type Standards

description: The number of days in advance of the stay date that the rate requires booking (advance purchase restriction). Used to ensure parity comparisons account for advance purchase rate conditions that may legitimately differ across channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; revenue.rate_restriction

description: Externally referenceable business identifier for this audit record, used in OTA contract compliance disputes and parity violation correspondence. Format: RPA-YYYY-MM-DD-XXXXXXXX.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Current lifecycle state of the rate parity audit record. 'open' indicates a new observation; 'violation_confirmed' indicates a confirmed parity breach; 'dispute_raised' indicates a formal dispute filed with the OTA/channel; 'resolved' indicates the channel corrected the rate; 'closed' indicates no action required; 'waived' indicates the violation was contractually waived.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: The precise date and time at which the rate observation was captured during the automated or manual parity monitoring scan. This is the principal business event timestamp for this audit record.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS; HTNG Data Standards

description: The cancellation policy associated with the observed rate (refundable, non_refundable, partially_refundable). Ensures parity comparisons are made on equivalent rate conditions, as NRR rates are expected to be lower than refundable rates.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA

description: The URL or deep link to the channel page where the rate was observed at the time of the audit scan. Used as evidence in OTA parity dispute resolution and contract compliance documentation.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; GDPR Article 5 (data minimization)

description: Classification of the distribution channel type. OTA (Online Travel Agency) includes Booking.com and Expedia; GDS (Global Distribution System) includes Sabre and Amadeus; direct_web is the hotel's own booking engine; metasearch includes Google Hotel Ads and TripAdvisor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|direct_web|voice|metasearch|wholesaler|corporate|consortium|bedbank — promote to reference product]
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; AHLA

description: The contractually agreed parity rate threshold for this channel, rate plan, and property combination as defined in the OTA or GDS distribution agreement. May differ from the direct rate if a contracted rate floor or ceiling applies.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: The date and time at which this rate parity audit record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 5; SOX Internal Controls; HTNG Data Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the observed rate, direct rate, and contracted parity rate are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures accurate cross-currency parity comparisons for international properties.

The rate available on the hotel's direct booking channel (own website or CRS direct) for the same room type, rate plan, stay date, and LOS at the time of the audit. Used as the parity benchmark for comparison.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS; AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines

description: The date on which a formal parity dispute was raised with the OTA or distribution channel partner. Null if no dispute has been filed. Used to track SLA compliance for dispute resolution timelines.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; SLA Management Standards

description: The reference number assigned when a formal parity dispute is raised with the OTA or distribution channel. Populated when audit_status transitions to 'dispute_raised'. Used for tracking dispute resolution correspondence.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: The date on which the parity dispute was resolved and the channel corrected the rate or the dispute was formally closed. Null if the dispute is still open. Used to measure dispute resolution cycle time.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; SLA Management Standards

description: Indicates whether this audit record represents a confirmed rate parity violation, where the observed channel rate is lower than the direct booking rate or contracted parity rate beyond the allowable tolerance threshold. True = violation detected.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

business_glossary_term: Rate Inclusive of Taxes and Fees Flag
description: Indicates whether the observed channel rate is inclusive of taxes and fees (True) or is a net/exclusive rate (False). Critical for accurate parity comparison as some channels display gross rates while others display net rates.
The length of stay in nights for which the rate was observed. Rate parity monitoring may vary by LOS as some channels offer LOS-based discounts that can trigger parity violations.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; USALI

description: The meal plan included in the observed rate (RO=Room Only, BB=Bed and Breakfast, HB=Half Board, FB=Full Board, AI=All Inclusive). Ensures parity comparisons are made on a like-for-like basis when channels bundle different meal inclusions.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Rate Standards; IH&RA

description: Identifies how the rate observation was captured. 'automated_scraper' indicates a rate shopping tool or API; 'manual_review' indicates a revenue manager manually checked; 'ota_alert' indicates the OTA itself flagged the discrepancy; 'partner_report' indicates a channel partner reported it; 'revenue_manager' indicates direct CRS/PMS review.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS

description: The actual room rate (in the property's local currency) observed on the audited channel at the time of the audit scan. This is the raw rate as displayed to the consumer on the channel.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: Name of the rate shopping or competitive intelligence tool used to capture the channel rate observation (e.g., OTA Insight, RateGain, Duetto, Infor EzRMS). Relevant when monitoring_source is 'automated_scraper'.
reference: Infor EzRMS; IDeaS G3 RMS; HTNG Technology Standards

description: The absolute monetary difference between the observed channel rate and the direct booking rate (observed_rate minus direct_rate). A negative value indicates the channel is undercutting the direct rate, constituting a parity violation.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: The percentage difference between the observed channel rate and the direct booking rate, calculated as (observed_rate - direct_rate) / direct_rate * 100. Used to assess the severity of a parity violation relative to the direct rate.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; IDeaS G3 RMS; STR Benchmarking Standards

description: Free-text notes documenting the outcome of the parity dispute, including the channel's response, corrective action taken, or reason for waiving the violation. Supports audit trail and OTA contract compliance documentation.

The date and time at which the parity audit record was reviewed by a revenue manager or channel distribution analyst. Supports audit trail and SLA compliance tracking for parity violation response times.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; SOX Internal Controls

description: The name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or channel distribution analyst who reviewed and actioned this parity audit record. Populated for manually reviewed or escalated records.
reference: AHLA; SOX Internal Controls; Oracle HCM Cloud

description: Reference identifier or storage path to the screenshot or screen capture taken of the channel rate at the time of the audit. Serves as evidentiary documentation for OTA parity dispute resolution.
The specific arrival or stay date for which the rate was observed on the channel. Rate parity is evaluated per stay date to detect date-specific pricing discrepancies.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; STR Benchmarking Standards

description: The allowable percentage variance between the channel rate and the direct/contracted rate before a parity violation is flagged. Defined per channel contract or property policy. Violations are only flagged when rate_variance_pct exceeds this threshold.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: The date and time at which this rate parity audit record was last modified, such as when the audit status changed, dispute details were added, or resolution notes were updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail.
reference: GDPR Article 5; SOX Internal Controls; HTNG Data Standards

description: Severity classification of the rate parity violation based on the magnitude of the rate variance percentage. 'critical' typically indicates >10% variance; 'high' 5-10%; 'medium' 2-5%; 'low' <2%; 'none' for no violation. Drives escalation and dispute prioritization.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Classifies the nature of the rate parity violation. 'below_direct' means the channel rate is lower than the direct booking rate; 'below_contracted' means the channel rate is below the contracted parity rate; 'below_both' means the channel rate violates both thresholds; 'no_violation' means parity is maintained.
reference: AHLA OTA Contract Compliance Guidelines; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each channel stop-sell, close-out, or restriction control record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.
reference: HTNG Data Dictionary; Sabre SynXis CRS Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Stop-sell restrictions can be applied to specific booking sources within a channel for granular inventory control. While stop_sell already has channel_id for channel-level restrictions, adding nullabl
Identifier of the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, etc.) to which this stop-sell restriction is applied. Links to the channel master record.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Identifier of the rate plan (BAR, NRR, LRA, package, etc.) to which this restriction is scoped. Null indicates the restriction applies across all rate plans for the channel and room type combination.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Plan Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.inventory_control. Business justification: Stop-sell actions are triggered by inventory_control records (hurdle rates exceeded, overbooking caps reached, sell limits hit). FK links the channel-level stop-sell action to the revenue-side invento

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Stop-sell restrictions are segment-specific in revenue management — closing OTA transient while keeping corporate open is a standard tactic. FK enables segment-scoped restriction audit and RMS-driven
Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this stop-sell restriction applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Property Configuration; AHLA Property Standards

description: Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard) subject to the stop-sell restriction. Null indicates the restriction applies to all room types at the property.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Type Configuration; HTNG Room Type Standards

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Stop-sell actions are tactical executions of revenue strategies (closing channels when demand exceeds forecast thresholds). FK links each stop-sell to the governing strategy, enabling strategy-level a

Type of inventory control action recorded in this transaction. apply: restriction manually applied; lift: restriction manually removed; modify: restriction parameters changed; auto_apply: restriction applied by RMS automation rule; auto_lift: restriction lifted by RMS automation rule.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the affected room type and date range at the time the restriction was applied. Provides revenue management context for the stop-sell decision and supports yield optimization analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS ADR Metrics; STR STAR Report ADR Benchmarking; USALI

description: Username or employee identifier of the revenue management or channel management user who applied this restriction. Supports accountability and audit trail for inventory control actions.
Indicates whether this restriction action required managerial approval before being applied. Supports internal control workflows and SOX compliance for material inventory control decisions.
reference: SOX Section 404 (internal controls); AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Username or employee identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved this restriction action when approval_required is True. Null if no approval was required or if the action was system-generated.
Date and time when the restriction action was approved by the designated manager or supervisor. Null if no approval was required. Supports SOX audit trail for material inventory control decisions.
reference: SOX Section 404 (internal controls); AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: External reference identifier assigned by the channel manager or connectivity platform (e.g., SiteMinder, Amadeus Central Reservations) when the restriction was transmitted to the distribution channel. Supports cross-system reconciliation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Manager Integration; HTNG Channel Connectivity Standards

description: Categorical classification of the distribution channel. ota: Online Travel Agency (OTA); gds: Global Distribution System (GDS); direct: brand.com or direct booking; voice: call center or CRS voice; wholesaler: tour operator or bed bank; metasearch: Google Hotels, Trivago; corporate: negotiated corporate accounts; group: group/MICE bookings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct|voice|wholesaler|metasearch|corporate|group — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Types; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; STR Channel Mix Benchmarking

description: Audit timestamp recording when this stop-sell restriction record was first captured in the system. Distinct from effective_timestamp which records when the restriction became operationally active.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); SOX Section 404 (audit trail)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the ADR and any monetary values recorded in this restriction record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency property operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Multi-Currency Reporting; USALI

description: Number of days between the date the restriction was applied and the first affected stay date (stay_date_from). Represents the booking window horizon at which the restriction was enacted, used for revenue strategy analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Pickup Analysis; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting; STR Benchmarking

description: The real-world date and time when this restriction became active and was enforced across the channel. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transactional record, distinct from the audit creation timestamp.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Inventory Control Activation; HTNG Inventory Control Standards

description: Indicates whether this restriction applies to all distribution channels simultaneously rather than a single specific channel. True when a property-wide close-out is executed across all channels; False when scoped to a specific channel.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Global Inventory Controls; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Indicates whether this restriction applies to all room types at the property rather than a specific room type. True when the restriction is property-wide across all room categories.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Inventory Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Room Type Configuration

description: Indicates whether this restriction was automatically generated by the Revenue Management System (RMS) or channel manager automation rule, as opposed to being manually applied by a revenue manager.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Automation Rules; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting

description: Standardized code identifying the business reason for lifting the restriction. Examples: DEMAND_SOFTENED, RATE_PARITY_RESTORED, MANUAL_OVERRIDE, SCHEDULED_EXPIRY, CHANNEL_RENEGOTIATED. Null if restriction has not been lifted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEMAND_SOFTENED|RATE_PARITY_RESTORED|MANUAL_OVERRIDE|SCHEDULED_EXPIRY|CHANNEL_RENEGOTIATED|SYSTEM_AUTO — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Date and time when the restriction was lifted or removed, re-opening inventory on the affected channel. Null if the restriction is still active or has not yet been lifted.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Inventory Control Lift; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Username or employee identifier of the user who lifted or removed this restriction. Null if the restriction has not been lifted or was lifted automatically by the system.
Maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay when booking on this channel during the restricted date range. Applicable when restriction_type is max_los. Null for non-LOS restriction types.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS LOS Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS LOS Restrictions; HTNG Inventory Control Standards

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible for booking on this channel during the restricted date range. Applicable when restriction_type is min_los. Null for non-LOS restriction types.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS LOS Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS LOS Restrictions; HTNG Inventory Control Standards

description: Additional free-text notes or comments entered by the revenue manager or channel manager regarding this restriction. May include context about competitive positioning, demand events, or channel negotiation details.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Property or room-type occupancy percentage at the time the restriction was applied. Provides context for the revenue management decision and supports post-hoc analysis of restriction effectiveness. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.50 = 85.50%).
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Occupancy Metrics; STR STAR Report OCC Benchmarking; USALI

description: Indicates whether this stop-sell action was triggered by a rate parity breach on the affected channel. True when the restriction is applied to enforce rate parity compliance across distribution channels.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Parity Monitoring; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; OTA Rate Parity Agreements

description: Standardized code identifying the business reason for applying the stop-sell or restriction. Examples include: HIGH_DEMAND, RATE_PARITY_BREACH, CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE, OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION, RENOVATION, ALLOTMENT_EXHAUSTED. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIGH_DEMAND|RATE_PARITY_BREACH|CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE|OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION|RENOVATION|ALLOTMENT_EXHAUSTED|SYSTEM_AUTO — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Restriction Reason Codes

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the restriction beyond the standardized reason code. Entered by the revenue manager or channel manager at the time of action.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Number of calendar days spanned by the restriction date range (stay_date_to minus stay_date_from + 1). Supports analysis of restriction scope and revenue impact assessment.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Externally-known business identifier for this stop-sell or restriction control action, as generated by the CRS (Sabre SynXis) or channel manager. Used for cross-system traceability and audit reconciliation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Inventory Control Reference; HTNG Inventory Control Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the stop-sell or restriction control record. active: restriction is currently enforced; lifted: restriction has been removed; pending: restriction is queued for activation; cancelled: restriction was voided before activation; expired: restriction passed its scheduled end without explicit lift.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Inventory Control Lifecycle; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Classification of the inventory restriction being applied to the channel. stop_sell: no new bookings accepted; closed_to_arrival (CTA): no check-ins on the stay date; closed_to_departure (CTD): no check-outs on the stay date; min_los: minimum Length of Stay (LOS) required; max_los: maximum LOS allowed; close_out: full inventory closure.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Restriction Types; HTNG Inventory Control Standards

business_glossary_term: Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) at Apply
description: Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the affected property or room type at the time the restriction was applied. Key revenue management KPI used to contextualize the stop-sell decision and evaluate channel mix optimization outcomes.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS RevPAR Metrics; STR STAR Report RevPAR Benchmarking; USALI

description: Operational system of record that originated this stop-sell or restriction control action. Identifies whether the action was initiated from the CRS (Sabre SynXis), RMS (IDeaS G3), PMS (Oracle OPERA), demand forecasting system (Infor EzRMS), or entered manually.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; Infor EzRMS; HTNG Data Lineage Standards

description: First stay date (inclusive) in the date range to which this restriction applies. Represents the beginning of the affected inventory window in the property calendar.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Date Range Controls; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Last stay date (inclusive) in the date range to which this restriction applies. Represents the end of the affected inventory window in the property calendar.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Date Range Controls; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Status of the restriction transmission to the distribution channel via the CRS or channel manager. pending: queued for transmission; transmitted: sent to channel; confirmed: acknowledged by channel; failed: transmission error; retrying: re-attempting after failure. Critical for ensuring inventory control actions are successfully propagated.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS API Integration; HTNG Channel Connectivity Standards; SLA Compliance

description: Date and time when the restriction was successfully transmitted to the distribution channel via the CRS or channel manager. Null if transmission is pending or failed. Supports SLA monitoring for inventory control propagation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS API Integration; HTNG Channel Connectivity Standards; SLA Compliance

description: Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this restriction record, such as a status change, date range extension, or reason code update.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); SOX Section 404 (audit trail)

description: Unique identifier for the event account. Primary key for the B2B client entity that books MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) events.
reference: HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) - Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Corporate event accounts are classified into segments (high-value, regional, industry vertical) for targeted marketing. Enables account-based marketing, personalized campaign targeting, and segment-sp
Identifier of the parent event account if this account is part of a corporate hierarchy or national account structure. Enables roll-up reporting and consolidated billing.
Identifier of the primary or preferred property where the event account typically books events. Used for account assignment and relationship management.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Corporate event accounts carry loyalty tier classifications (e.g., Platinum corporate) determining pricing concessions, service levels, and benefit entitlements. The existing plain-text 'account_tier'
Legal or operating name of the corporate, association, or group client organization that books MICE events.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the event account. Used in contracts, proposals, and invoicing. Typically assigned by the CRS (Central Reservation System) or Delphi system.
Current lifecycle status of the event account. Active accounts can book new events; suspended accounts require approval; closed accounts are archived.
Classification of the event account based on the nature of the client organization. Determines pricing strategies, contract terms, and service offerings.
Average revenue per event for this account. Calculated as lifetime event spend divided by total events booked. Used for forecasting and opportunity sizing.
First line of the billing address for the event account, including street number and name.
Second line of the billing address for additional address details such as suite, floor, or building number.
City name for the billing address of the event account.
Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address of the event account.
Postal or ZIP code for the billing address of the event account.
State or province code for the billing address of the event account.
Date when the event account was closed or deactivated. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle reporting and reactivation analysis.
reference: CRM Best Practices - Account Lifecycle Management

description: Date and time when the event account record was first created in the system. Used for account age analysis and audit trails.
Maximum credit amount approved for the event account. Used to control exposure and manage accounts receivable risk.
Current credit approval status for the event account. Determines whether the account can book events on credit terms or requires prepayment.
Account identifier from the source system (e.g., Delphi Account ID, Salesforce Account ID). Used for system integration and data lineage tracking.
Primary industry sector or vertical market of the client organization (e.g., Technology, Healthcare, Finance, Education, Manufacturing). Used for market segmentation and targeted sales strategies.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is a national or enterprise-wide account with negotiated rates and terms across multiple properties.
reference: MICE Industry Standards - National Account Programs

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is a VIP or strategic account requiring special handling, executive attention, or premium service levels.
Date of the most recent event held by this account. Used for recency analysis and account reactivation campaigns.
Total cumulative revenue generated from all events booked by this account since inception. Key metric for account tier classification and relationship value assessment.
Free-text notes capturing special requirements, preferences, historical context, or relationship management information for the event account.
Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Defines the credit period for the event account.
Most frequently booked event type by this account (e.g., Conference, Training, Board Meeting, Product Launch). Used for targeted marketing and service customization.
Primary email address of the main contact person for event inquiries, proposals, and communications.
Full name of the primary contact person at the client organization responsible for event planning and booking decisions.
Primary telephone number of the main contact person for event coordination and urgent communications.
Geographic or market-based sales territory code to which this event account is assigned for sales planning and performance tracking.
Code identifying the operational system of record where this event account was originally created (e.g., Delphi by Amadeus, Salesforce CRM).
Tax identification number or employer identification number (EIN) of the client organization for tax reporting and compliance.
Total number of events booked by this account since inception. Used for relationship depth analysis and loyalty assessment.
Average number of attendees for events booked by this account. Used for capacity planning and function space allocation.
Date and time when the event account record was last modified. Used for data freshness tracking and change audit.
Unique identifier for the event attendee record. Primary key.
reference: HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) - Event Management Standards

description: Foreign key linking to experience.amenity_fulfillment. Business justification: VIP event attendees receive amenity fulfillments (welcome gifts, room drops, F&B setups). Linking enables tracking of what was delivered to which attendee, supporting VIP program reporting, cost attri

Foreign key linking to experience.experience_special_request. Business justification: Event attendees submit special requests (dietary, accessibility, VIP amenities) that are fulfilled through experience_special_request. Linking enables fulfillment tracking, personalization delivery, a
Reference to the guest profile in the Property Management System (PMS). Links attendee to their master guest record in Oracle OPERA PMS for loyalty tracking and preference management.

Foreign key linking to event.invoice. Business justification: Individual attendees may have per-person registration fees, meal charges, or other billable items that are invoiced separately or rolled into a master invoice. Linking attendee.invoice_id -> event.inv

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Individual event attendees are frequently loyalty members earning points for event participation and room nights. Essential for loyalty point accrual, tier qualification tracking, and personalized eve
Reference to the room reservation booking associated with this attendee, if the attendee is part of the group room block. Null for day attendees or virtual participants.

Foreign key linking to experience.service_case. Business justification: Individual attendees raise service cases during or after events (dietary failures, accessibility issues, room problems). Service recovery teams need attendee-level case linkage for personalized resolu

Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: Event attendees book individual spa appointments during event stay. Critical for cross-sell revenue tracking, attendee experience management, and spa capacity planning around event dates. Real operati

Foreign key linking to spa.membership. Business justification: Event attendees convert to spa members post-event. Key conversion funnel metric for marketing ROI analysis. Tracks which events drive membership acquisition, informing future event-spa package design

Foreign key linking to marketing.survey_response. Business justification: Post-event satisfaction surveys are sent to attendees to measure event experience, NPS, and identify service recovery needs. Critical for event quality measurement, attendee satisfaction tracking, and
Description of any accessibility accommodations required by the attendee, such as wheelchair access, hearing assistance, visual aids, or service animal. Ensures ADA compliance.

Mode of participation for the attendee: in-person (physical attendance), virtual (remote online participation), or hybrid (combination of both). Critical for capacity planning and platform provisioning.
reference: HTNG Event Management Standards, Post-COVID MICE Standards

description: Unique badge identifier assigned to the attendee for event access control and session tracking. Printed on physical badge or used as digital credential.
Date when the attendee registration was cancelled. Null if registration is active. Used for refund policy application and attrition tracking.
Current check-in status of the attendee at the event. Tracks whether attendee has arrived and collected their badge or credentials.
Date and time when the attendee checked in at the event registration desk or virtual platform. Null if not yet checked in.
Date when the attendee registration was confirmed, typically after payment or approval. Null if not yet confirmed.
System timestamp when the attendee record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) - Internal Controls, GDPR Article 5

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the attendee has provided explicit consent for data processing and storage in compliance with GDPR and CCPA. True if consent given, False otherwise.
reference: GDPR Article 7 - Conditions for Consent, CCPA

description: Free-text description of attendee dietary requirements, allergies, or preferences for Food and Beverage (F&B) planning. Examples: vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, kosher, halal, nut allergy.
reference: ISO 22000 Food Safety Management, ADA Compliance

description: Primary email address for attendee communication, event confirmations, and virtual platform access credentials.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data, CCPA, CAN-SPAM Act

description: Name of the emergency contact person for the attendee. Used in case of medical or safety incidents during the event.
reference: OSHA Safety Standards, Event Risk Management Best Practices

description: Phone number of the emergency contact person. Used for urgent communication in case of attendee emergency.
reference: OSHA Safety Standards, Event Risk Management Best Practices

description: Free-text feedback comments provided by the attendee in post-event survey via Medallia. Used for service recovery and continuous improvement.
First or given name of the event attendee as provided during registration.
Professional title or role of the attendee within their organization. Used for badge printing and attendee segmentation.
Last or family name of the event attendee as provided during registration.
Boolean flag indicating whether the attendee has consented to receive marketing communications from the property or event organizer. True if opted in, False otherwise.
reference: GDPR Article 6 - Lawful Basis for Processing, CCPA, CAN-SPAM Act

description: Net Promoter Score provided by the attendee in post-event survey, ranging from 0 (not likely to recommend) to 10 (extremely likely to recommend). Null if not provided.
reference: Medallia NPS Standards, SALT (Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking)

description: Name of the company, institution, or organization the attendee represents. Used for badge printing and networking purposes.
Date when the registration fee payment was received. Null if payment is pending or waived.
Current payment status of the registration fee. Tracks whether attendee has completed payment, is pending, or received a refund or waiver.
Primary contact phone number for the attendee, used for event coordination and emergency contact.
Date when the attendee completed their event registration. Used for early-bird pricing determination and registration pace analysis.
Total registration fee charged to the attendee in the event currency. May vary based on registration type, early-bird pricing, or group discounts.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry), IFRS 15

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the registration fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Current lifecycle status of the attendee registration. Tracks progression from initial registration through confirmation, cancellation, or no-show.
Precise date and time when the attendee registration was submitted. Used for audit trail and registration sequence tracking.
Category of attendee registration indicating their role at the event. Determines access levels, pricing tier, and badge type.
reference: HTNG Event Management Standards, MICE Industry Best Practices

description: Post-event satisfaction score provided by the attendee via Medallia survey. Typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-5. Null if survey not completed.
reference: Medallia Experience Management Standards, SALT (Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking)

description: Comma-separated list or JSON array of session identifiers that the attendee has pre-registered for. Used for capacity planning and personalized agenda management.
Name of the source system from which this attendee record originated (e.g., Delphi by Amadeus, Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.
Unique identifier of the attendee record in the source system. Used for reconciliation and bi-directional synchronization with Delphi by Amadeus or other event management platforms.
Free-text field capturing any additional special requests or notes from the attendee, such as seating preferences, networking interests, or other accommodations.
System timestamp when the attendee record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) - Internal Controls, GDPR Article 5

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the attendee has been provisioned with access credentials to the virtual event platform. True if access granted, False otherwise.
Username or login identifier for the attendee on the virtual event platform. Used for hybrid and virtual attendance modes. Null for in-person-only attendees.
Unique identifier for the event booking. Primary key for the event booking entity representing a confirmed or tentative MICE engagement.
reference: HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) - Event Management Standards

description: Reference to the corporate account or organization booking the event.

Foreign key linking to channel.commission_schedule. Business justification: OTA- and GDS-sourced MICE/event bookings are subject to channel commission schedules. Finance and event sales teams need to reference the governing commission schedule on event bookings to calculate,

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Event bookings are cost centers for USALI departmental P&L reporting. Hotels allocate event labor, space, and overhead costs by cost center for operational performance analysis and budget variance tra

Channel or touchpoint through which the identity document was captured. Values: front_desk (traditional check-in), mobile_checkin (mobile app), kiosk (self-service kiosk), online_precheckin (web portal), concierge (concierge desk).
Date and time when the identity document information was first captured into the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Represents the business event time of document capture.

Boolean flag indicating whether this document record meets all regulatory compliance requirements for the jurisdiction. True if compliant, False if additional documentation or verification is required.

Values: standard (normal retention period), extended (longer retention for compliance), legal_hold (litigation hold), purge_eligible (eligible for deletion).

description: Gender marker as recorded on the identity document. Values: M (Male), F (Female), X (Unspecified/Non-binary), U (Unknown/Not stated).

Status of government reporting submission for this identity document. Values: not_required (no reporting obligation), pending (awaiting submission), submitted (sent to authorities), confirmed (acknowledged by authorities), failed (submission error).
Date and time when the identity document information was reported to government authorities. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if not yet reported or not required.

Boolean flag indicating whether this identity document record must be reported to government authorities per local regulations (e.g., police registration, immigration reporting). True if reporting is required, False otherwise.

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary identity document on file for the guest. True if this is the main document used for identification, False otherwise.
Date on which the identity document was issued by the issuing authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Name of the government agency or authority that issued the identity document (e.g., U.S. Department of State, UK Home Office, Ministry of Interior).
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country or jurisdiction that issued the identity document.
Date and time when this identity document record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field for record modification.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the guests nationality as stated on the identity document.
City and country of birth as recorded on the identity document. Used for enhanced identity verification in some jurisdictions.

Method used to verify the authenticity of the identity document. Values: manual (staff visual inspection), automated (OCR/document scanner), biometric (facial recognition or fingerprint match), third_party (external verification service).
Free-text notes recorded by staff during the verification process. May include observations about document condition, discrepancies, or reasons for rejection.

Current verification status of the identity document. Indicates whether the document has been validated by staff or automated systems. Values: pending (awaiting verification), verified (validated and accepted), rejected (invalid or fraudulent), expired (document past expiry date), flagged (requires additional review).
Date and time when the identity document verification was completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Entry type permitted by the visa. Values: single (one entry only), multiple (multiple entries allowed), transit (transit visa). Null for non-visa documents.
Type or category of visa if the document is a visa (e.g., tourist, business, student, work). Null for non-visa documents.
