Indicates whether this segment has Last Room Availability commitment, meaning inventory is available at the contracted rate even when the property is sold out. Typically True for top-tier corporate contracts and loyalty elite members.
Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

Pricing strategy applicable to this segment. Dynamic uses RMS (Revenue Management System) optimization, Fixed uses BAR (Best Available Rate) or published rates, Negotiated uses contracted rates, Promotional uses discount codes, Opaque uses non-branded OTA channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Strategy Framework

business_glossary_term: RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) Contribution Percentage
description: Historical percentage contribution of this segment to total property RevPAR. Sum across all segments equals 100%. Used for strategic segment mix planning.
Broader business category for revenue and demand analysis. Used for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) decomposition and TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) attribution.
Short alphanumeric code identifying the market segment (e.g., TRANS, GRP, CORP, WHSL, COMP, CREW). Sourced from OPERA PMS Market Segment codes and IDeaS G3 RMS segment taxonomy.
reference: OPERA PMS Market Segment Standards, USALI

description: Full descriptive name of the market segment (e.g., Transient, Group, Corporate Negotiated, Wholesale, Complimentary, Airline Crew).

Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are available for assignment and reporting, Inactive segments are hidden from selection but retained for historical analysis, Deprecated segments are being phased out, Pending segments are awaiting approval.

High-level classification of the segment type. Transient represents FIT (Free Independent Traveler) guests, Group represents MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) and leisure groups, Contract represents negotiated corporate accounts, Wholesale represents OTA (Online Travel Agency) and tour operator bookings, Complimentary represents comp stays and employee rates, Crew represents airline and travel industry staff.
reference: USALI, STR (Smith Travel Research) Segment Standards

description: System of record that originated this segment definition or assignment. OPERA for PMS market segments, IDEAS for RMS segments, SALESFORCE for CRM segmentation, MANUAL for analyst-created segments.
Unique identifier of this segment in the source system. Used for data reconciliation and lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) Mapping Code
description: Standard USALI account code for financial reporting and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) attribution. Ensures consistent revenue classification across properties.
Indicates whether this segment is subject to dynamic yield optimization and inventory controls by the RMS. True for transient and qualified segments, False for contracted and complimentary segments.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS, Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each VIP designation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the vip_designation data product. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS VIP code assignments and Salesforce CRM VIP profile records.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Guest Profile Module

description: Reference to the corporate account record when the VIP designation is granted based on a corporate relationship (e.g., key account executive, contracted corporate partner). Null for individual leisure VIP guests. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account object.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; Oracle OPERA PMS Company Profile

description: Reference to the loyalty program membership record associated with this VIP guest. Links VIP designation entitlements with loyalty tier benefits to ensure non-duplication and correct benefit stacking. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management module.
reference: Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management; AHLA Loyalty Program Standards

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: VIP designation drives room assignment priority and upgrade eligibility to specific room types. Pre-arrival VIP checklists and front-desk upgrade workflows require knowing which room type a VIP is ent

Reference to the guest profile record for whom this VIP designation is assigned. Links to the master guest profile in the guest domain. A single guest may hold multiple concurrent VIP designations across brand and property levels.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Guest Profile Module; Salesforce CRM Guest Object

description: Reference to the property at which this VIP designation applies. A designation may be property-specific (e.g., Celebrity at a single resort) or brand-wide (null indicates brand-level designation). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Property Configuration; AHLA Property Standards

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: VIP designation rules are tier-dependent: only Platinum members receive GM greeting, security escort, or incognito check-in. Operations and loyalty teams need to know which tier threshold governs or t

Indicates whether a complimentary or arranged airport transfer service is required for this VIP guest as part of their designation entitlements. Triggers coordination with the concierge and transportation team during pre-arrival preparation.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Pre-Arrival Checklist; AHLA Concierge Service Standards

description: Pseudonym or alias name used for incognito check-in and reservation bookings to protect the identity of high-profile guests. Used in place of the guest's legal name on folios, room assignments, and staff communications when incognito_checkin is true.
reference: GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798.100; Oracle OPERA PMS Guest Profile Privacy Fields

description: Code referencing the amenity entitlement package associated with this VIP level. Determines the standard amenity setup (e.g., welcome fruit basket, champagne, floral arrangement, personalized stationery) to be prepared prior to guest arrival. Sourced from OPERA PMS amenity configuration.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Amenity Configuration; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Number of active VIP designations currently held by this guest across all scopes (brand and property level). Supports operational awareness that a guest may hold multiple concurrent designations (e.g., VIP3 at brand level and Celebrity at property level) and ensures all applicable protocols are honored.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Table; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Timestamp when this VIP designation record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse. Represents the audit trail creation event. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data accountability); SOX Section 404 (audit trail); ISO 27001

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code identifying this VIP designation record, as assigned in Oracle OPERA PMS VIP code table (e.g., VIP1, VIP3, CELEB, ROYAL, OWNER). Used for cross-system reference and pre-arrival checklist triggers.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Table; HTNG Guest Profile Standards

description: Internal narrative notes providing additional context about the VIP designation, such as the relationship history, specific events that triggered the designation, or instructions for the guest relations team. Visible only to authorized staff. Sourced from Salesforce CRM case notes.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Management; GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Business rationale for granting the VIP designation. Revenue threshold indicates the guest meets a minimum annual spend threshold. Corporate relationship indicates a key account or contracted corporate partner. Public figure, diplomatic, and celebrity reasons trigger additional privacy protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue_threshold|corporate_relationship|public_figure|loyalty_elite|owner|board_member|celebrity|diplomatic|media|government — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM VIP Classification; AHLA Guest Experience Standards; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the VIP designation applies across the entire brand portfolio, is restricted to a specific property, or applies to a regional cluster of properties. Drives which properties must honor the designation during pre-arrival preparation.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Table; HTNG Guest Profile Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the VIP designation record. Active designations drive pre-arrival checklists and upgrade eligibility. Suspended designations are temporarily paused. Revoked designations have been withdrawn due to policy violation or relationship change.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Table; Salesforce CRM VIP Status Field

description: Indicates that the guest has a standing do-not-disturb preference that must be honored by housekeeping, engineering, and all operational departments unless a safety emergency arises. Overrides standard housekeeping scheduling in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Housekeeping Configuration; OSHA Safety Standards

description: Calendar date on which the VIP designation becomes active and service protocols are triggered. Used to schedule pre-arrival preparation and amenity entitlements. Aligns with OPERA PMS VIP code validity start date.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Validity; HTNG Guest Profile Standards

description: Calendar date on which the VIP designation expires and service protocols are deactivated. Null indicates an open-ended designation with no planned expiry. Annual review cycles typically reset this date. Aligns with OPERA PMS VIP code validity end date.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Validity; HTNG Guest Profile Standards

description: Indicates whether the General Manager (GM) or designated senior property leader is required to personally greet this VIP guest upon arrival. Triggers a GM greeting task in the pre-arrival preparation checklist within Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Pre-Arrival Checklist; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Indicates that the guest requires a discreet, incognito check-in process to protect their identity and privacy. When true, the front desk must use a private check-in area, suppress the guest name from public-facing displays, and limit staff awareness of the guest's presence. Critical for celebrity and diplomatic guests.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Privacy Flags; GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Date of the guest's most recent completed stay at any property under this VIP designation's scope. Used during annual VIP review cycles to assess continued eligibility based on recency of engagement. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS reservation history.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Reservation History; AHLA Guest Loyalty Standards

description: Indicates that all media inquiries, press requests, and social media references related to this guest's stay must be suppressed and escalated to the Director of Communications. Distinct from no_photo_policy as it governs external communications rather than on-property photography.
reference: GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798.100; AHLA Communications Standards

description: Indicates that the guest has explicitly prohibited photography, video recording, or any visual documentation of their presence on property. Staff must be briefed on this restriction during pre-arrival preparation. Applies to celebrity, royalty, and diplomatic designations.
reference: GDPR Article 9; CCPA Section 1798.100; AHLA Guest Privacy Standards

description: Code identifying the standardized pre-arrival preparation checklist template to be activated for this VIP designation. Templates define the sequence of tasks (amenity setup, room inspection, GM briefing, transportation coordination) required before the guest's arrival.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Pre-Arrival Task Management; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Minimum annual revenue contribution (in USD) that qualified this guest for the VIP designation based on the revenue threshold reason. Used for annual VIP eligibility reviews and LTV (Lifetime Value) analysis. Sourced from OPERA PMS revenue history and SAP S/4HANA financial records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance GL; USALI Revenue Classification; AHLA Revenue Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the revenue_threshold_amount field. Ensures correct currency context for multi-currency properties and international guests. Defaults to USD for US-based properties.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Multi-Currency Configuration

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this VIP designation to confirm continued eligibility based on revenue thresholds, relationship status, or public figure standing. Supports annual VIP audit processes managed by the guest relations team.
reference: Salesforce CRM VIP Review Workflow; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Reason code explaining why a VIP designation was revoked or suspended. Populated only when designation_status is revoked or suspended. Supports audit trail requirements and VIP eligibility governance reviews. Sourced from Salesforce CRM case closure reason.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Management; SOX Internal Controls; AHLA Guest Relations Governance

description: Numeric priority rank (lower value = higher priority) used by the front desk and revenue management systems to sequence room assignment and upgrade eligibility for this VIP guest relative to other arriving guests. Integrates with IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Assignment; IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls

description: Indicates whether a dedicated security escort or personal protection detail coordination is required for this VIP guest during their stay. Triggers security briefing and coordination protocols with the property security team and any external protection services.
reference: AHLA Safety and Security Standards; OSHA Workplace Safety

description: Defines the escalation path and response urgency for service recovery incidents involving this VIP guest. Immediate_gm requires the General Manager to be notified within minutes of any complaint. Drives Medallia case routing and Salesforce CRM service recovery workflows.
reference: Medallia Service Recovery Module; Salesforce CRM Case Management; AHLA Guest Satisfaction Standards

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this VIP designation record originated. OPERA_PMS indicates the designation was created in Oracle OPERA PMS. SALESFORCE_CRM indicates origination from Salesforce CRM. MANUAL indicates a direct data entry by the data team. MIGRATION indicates a historical data load.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Salesforce CRM; HTNG Data Exchange Standards

description: The native VIP code or identifier as stored in the originating source system (Oracle OPERA PMS or Salesforce CRM). Retained for traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Table; Salesforce CRM VIP Field; HTNG Data Exchange Standards

description: Free-text field capturing bespoke service instructions specific to this VIP designation, such as preferred room temperature, dietary restrictions for amenity setup, security escort requirements, or specific staff interaction protocols. Sourced from Salesforce CRM case notes and OPERA PMS profile remarks.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Profile Remarks; Salesforce CRM Case Management; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Count of completed stays by the guest within the current designation validity period (effective_from to effective_until). Used to validate continued VIP eligibility at review date and to support LTV (Lifetime Value) reporting. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS stay history.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Stay History; USALI Guest Statistics; AHLA Loyalty Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this VIP designation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Tracks changes to designation status, special handling instructions, privacy flags, or host assignments. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data accountability); SOX Section 404 (audit trail); ISO 27001

description: Indicates whether this VIP designation entitles the guest to complimentary room category upgrades subject to availability at check-in. When true, the front desk system surfaces upgrade options during the check-in workflow in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Upgrade Rules; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Tiered classification of the VIP designation indicating the service protocol level assigned to the guest. VIP1 is entry-level recognition; VIP5 is the highest standard tier. Celebrity, Royalty, Owner, and Board Member are special-class designations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VIP1|VIP2|VIP3|VIP4|VIP5|Celebrity|Royalty|Owner|Board Member — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS VIP Code Table; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the corporate account record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to event.event_account. Business justification: Corporate accounts often have both transient room business (guest domain) and MICE business (event domain). Account managers need unified view of total account revenue, consolidated billing, and integ

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Corporate production reporting and rate strategy: corporate accounts are classified into revenue market segments to apply negotiated rate programs, commission structures, and displacement analysis. Re

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Corporate travel programs designate a primary booking channel (GDS, direct API, TMC portal). This FK supports corporate rate loading validation, preferred-channel compliance reporting, and commission

Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Corporate accounts are enrolled under specific loyalty program configurations governing earning rules, redemption terms, and data retention policies. Contract management and loyalty operations teams n
Internal email address of the hotel group's account manager assigned to this corporate account. Used for escalation routing, contract renewal notifications, and CRM workflow automation.
Full name of the hotel group's internal account manager or sales manager responsible for managing the corporate relationship, contract renewals, and production performance.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Owner; AHLA Sales Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the corporate account. Controls rate eligibility, direct billing access, and CRM engagement workflows. Active accounts are eligible for negotiated rates and direct billing.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS AR Module; Salesforce CRM Account Lifecycle

description: Classification of the corporate account by the nature of the client relationship. Drives rate eligibility, billing rules, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|government|consortium|travel_management_company|wholesale|other — promote to reference product if additional types are required]
reference: AHLA Corporate Account Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Account Classification

description: Target total revenue (rooms, Food and Beverage (F&B), events) expected from this corporate account in the contract year. Used for account manager performance tracking and revenue budget planning per USALI standards.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); SAP S/4HANA Revenue Planning

description: Contracted or projected annual room night volume committed by the corporate client. Used for account tiering, rate negotiation leverage, and production performance tracking against actuals.
reference: AHLA Corporate Account Production Standards; STR (Smith Travel Research) Benchmarking

description: City component of the corporate client's billing address. Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic segmentation reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Customer Master; USALI; IFRS/GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the corporate client's billing address. Drives tax treatment, currency defaults, and regulatory compliance requirements (GDPR for EU entities).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR; SAP S/4HANA Country Configuration

description: First line of the corporate client's official billing address used for invoice generation and accounts receivable correspondence in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Customer Master; GDPR Article 4; USALI

description: Free-text billing instruction specifying which charges (room, tax, incidentals, F&B) are to be direct-billed to the corporate account versus charged to the individual guest. Applied automatically at check-in in OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Cashiering Module; USALI

description: Description or reference code for blackout date restrictions applicable to this corporate account's negotiated rates. Specifies periods (e.g., peak demand, special events) when contracted rates are not available.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; AHLA Rate Agreement Standards

description: Full legal registered name of the corporate client organization as it appears on contracts, invoices, and billing documents.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master

description: Date on which the negotiated rate agreement and corporate account contract expires. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Rate eligibility ceases after this date unless renewed.
reference: AHLA Corporate Rate Agreement Standards; USALI

description: Date on which the negotiated rate agreement and corporate account contract becomes effective. Reservations made on or after this date are eligible for contracted rates.
reference: AHLA Corporate Rate Agreement Standards; USALI

description: Timestamp when the corporate account record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 30 (Records of Processing); SOX; Internal Data Governance Audit Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit and billing amounts are denominated for this corporate account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Maximum outstanding balance permitted on the corporate account's direct billing (city ledger) facility in the OPERA AR module. Enforced at check-in and during reservation creation to prevent credit overexposure.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS AR Module; USALI; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Financial Controls

description: External account identifier from Salesforce CRM, used to cross-reference the corporate account record with the CRM system of record for guest profiles and marketing campaigns.

Indicates whether the corporate contact has provided consent for processing of their personal data in accordance with GDPR and CCPA requirements. Required for marketing communications and data sharing with third-party partners.
reference: GDPR Article 6 (Lawful Basis); CCPA Section 1798.100; AHLA Data Privacy Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this corporate account is approved for direct billing (city ledger) to the hotel group. When true, eligible charges are posted to the AR account rather than requiring individual guest payment at checkout.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS AR Module; PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard)

description: Percentage discount off the Best Available Rate (BAR) or rack rate granted to this corporate account under the negotiated agreement. Used by the Revenue Management System (RMS) for displacement analysis and yield optimization.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; AHLA Corporate Rate Agreement Standards

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Subject Region
description: Regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the corporate contact's personal data. Determines which privacy regulations govern data handling, retention, and subject rights requests (GDPR for EU/UK, CCPA for California).
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation); CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act); AHLA Data Privacy Guidelines

business_glossary_term: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code
description: Six-digit NAICS code providing a more granular industry classification for the corporate client, used alongside SIC for regulatory reporting, procurement analysis, and revenue segmentation.
reference: U.S. Census Bureau NAICS Manual; SAP S/4HANA Customer Classification

description: Four-digit Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code identifying the primary industry sector of the corporate client. Used for market segmentation, demand analysis, and MICE opportunity targeting.
reference: U.S. Office of Management and Budget SIC Manual; AHLA Industry Segmentation Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the corporate account record in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
reference: GDPR Article 30; SOX; HTNG Data Exchange Standards; Internal Data Governance

description: Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) requirement in nights applicable to this corporate account's negotiated rate. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Restrictions; AHLA Rate Standards

description: Indicates whether individual travelers booking under this corporate account are eligible to earn loyalty program points. Some negotiated rate codes exclude loyalty accrual per program terms.
reference: Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management Module; AHLA Loyalty Program Standards

business_glossary_term: Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this corporate account is eligible for MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) group rates and event space allocations managed through Delphi by Amadeus.
reference: Delphi by Amadeus Event Sales Module; AHLA MICE Standards

business_glossary_term: Oracle OPERA Accounts Receivable (AR) Account Number
description: Accounts Receivable account number assigned in Oracle OPERA PMS AR module, used for direct billing, city ledger management, and credit limit enforcement at the property level.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS AR Module; USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Agreed payment terms for direct billing invoices issued to this corporate account. Determines invoice due dates and triggers accounts receivable aging in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AR Module; USALI; IFRS/GAAP

description: Comma-separated list of hotel property codes (from OPERA PMS) designated as preferred properties for this corporate account. Drives rate availability, inventory allocation, and channel distribution priority.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Property Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Business email address of the primary corporate contact. Used for contract communications, rate confirmations, and program updates. Subject to GDPR and CCPA data subject rights.
Full name of the primary travel coordinator or procurement contact at the corporate client organization responsible for managing the hotel program and employee travel bookings.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Business phone number of the primary corporate contact. Used for urgent communications regarding reservations, billing disputes, and contract negotiations.

Type of negotiated rate program governing this corporate account. LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees rate access even at high occupancy. NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) offers deeper discounts with cancellation restrictions. Drives Revenue Management System (RMS) displacement analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Program Configuration; AHLA Rate Standards

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this corporate account record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during ETL processing in the Silver Layer.
reference: HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) Data Exchange Standards; Internal Data Governance

description: Tax exemption classification for this corporate account. Government and qualifying non-profit accounts may be fully or partially exempt from occupancy and sales taxes. Drives tax posting rules in OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration; USALI; IFRS/GAAP; Local Tax Regulations

description: Official tax exemption certificate number issued by the relevant tax authority for this corporate account. Required for audit compliance when applying tax-exempt billing. Stored for SOX and USALI audit trail purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Module; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); USALI; Local Tax Authority Requirements

description: Doing-business-as (DBA) or brand name of the corporate client if different from the legal registered company name. Used for correspondence and guest-facing communications.

VIP tier classification assigned to this corporate account based on production volume, strategic importance, and revenue contribution. Drives service recovery priority, amenity entitlements, and account manager assignment level.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Segmentation; AHLA VIP Program Standards; Medallia Guest Experience

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the guest group block record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a contracted room block allocation binding the property to a group organizer for a defined stay window.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks Module

description: Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Group blocks originate through a specific booking source (group desk, GDS group segment, direct sales portal). This FK supports group channel attribution, commission calculation on group bookings, and

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group blocks are frequently generated by marketing campaigns (wedding packages, conference promotions, corporate incentive programs). Group sales teams track campaign attribution for ROI measurement,

Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Group block cancellation and attrition penalty management: group contracts reference a specific cancellation policy governing penalty calculations and deposit forfeiture. The existing cancellation_pol
Reference to the accounts receivable (AR) master account or corporate billing account to which the group master folio charges are posted. Sourced from OPERA PMS AR module.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS AR; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: MICE operations require linking a PMS guest room block to its originating event booking for master billing, attrition tracking, and group pickup reporting. Revenue managers and event coordinators reco
Reference to the guest profile of the primary group leader or organizer responsible for the block. Sourced from OPERA PMS guest profile or Salesforce CRM contact.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Guest Profiles; Salesforce CRM

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Group production reporting and rate application: group blocks are assigned market segments for revenue reporting, rate strategy application, and group displacement analysis. Revenue managers track gro

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Group blocks are frequently tied to loyalty promotions (double points for MICE groups, bonus nights for group bookings). Loyalty and revenue teams must link group blocks to the driving promotion for a
Reference to the property at which the group room block is contracted. Links to the property master in the property domain.

Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_group_block. Business justification: Group block reconciliation: the guest domain's contracted block must link to the reservation domain's pickup block for attrition reporting, rooming list management, and pickup tracking. Group sales an

Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_rate_plan. Business justification: Group contracted rate plan management: group blocks are sold against a specific rate plan governing commissions, LOS requirements, and channel restrictions. The existing group_rate_code is denormalize
Number of ADA-compliant accessible rooms requested within the group block. Tracked for ADA compliance and pre-assignment planning.
reference: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act); Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The first date of the contracted room block period — the date the group is scheduled to begin checking in. Serves as EFFECTIVE_FROM for this agreement.

Contractual minimum pickup percentage the group must achieve to avoid attrition penalties. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 80.00 means the group must pick up at least 80% of contracted rooms). Aligns with revenue.wash_factor domain.
reference: AHLA Group Contract Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Free-text instructions governing how charges are routed and settled for the group block master folio (e.g., 'Room and tax to master; incidentals to individual guest folio', 'All F&B charges to master account'). Sourced from OPERA PMS cashiering module.

Current lifecycle state of the group room block. 'Tentative' indicates a hold pending contract execution; 'Definite' indicates a signed/confirmed block; 'Cancelled' indicates the block was released; 'Closed' indicates the group has departed and the block is reconciled. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; AHLA

description: The date on which the group block was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active or closed blocks. Used for attrition penalty calculation and revenue recovery tracking.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; AHLA Group Contract Standards

description: Number of complimentary (comp) rooms included in the group block contract, typically awarded on a ratio basis (e.g., 1 comp per 40 paid rooms). Tracked separately for revenue accounting under USALI.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; USALI; AHLA

description: The date on which the group block contract was formally executed and the block status moved to 'Definite'. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the agreement lifecycle.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; AHLA Group Contract Standards

description: Negotiated room rate per night per room agreed in the group block contract. Expressed in the property's operating currency. Confidential commercial term.
reference: AHLA Group Contract Standards; USALI; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Total number of room nights committed in the group block contract across all room types and all nights of the block period. Primary quantitative measure of block size.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; USALI

description: Timestamp when the group block record was first created in the source system (OPERA PMS or Delphi). Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this entity.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; GDPR Article 5 (data accuracy); SOX

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this group block record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

The contractual deadline by which individual reservations must be made against the block at the contracted group rate. Rooms not picked up by this date are released back to general inventory per the attrition policy.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; AHLA Group Contract Standards

description: Source system identifier from Delphi by Amadeus event sales platform linking this group block to the originating sales opportunity or group proposal. Enables cross-system traceability.
The last date of the contracted room block period — the date the group is scheduled to check out. Serves as EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this agreement.
Contractual deadline by which the group deposit must be received to maintain the block in 'Definite' status.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Group Contract Standards

description: Actual deposit amount received from the group organizer to date. Compared against deposit_required_amount to determine outstanding deposit balance.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Cashiering; SAP S/4HANA Finance; USALI

description: Contractual deposit amount required from the group to confirm the block. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Cashiering; AHLA Group Contract Standards; USALI

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code used to identify the group block across systems (OPERA PMS, CRS, OTA channels). Used by guests and agents to associate individual reservations to the block. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Human-readable name of the group (e.g., 'Smith Wedding Party', 'Acme Corp Q3 Sales Conference', 'Chicago Bulls Away Team'). Displayed on folios, rooming lists, and internal reports.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; Delphi by Amadeus

description: Free-text operational notes and special instructions for the group block (e.g., VIP handling requirements, early check-in requests, amenity preferences, security arrangements). Sourced from OPERA PMS group block notes field.

Categorical classification of the group travel party type. Drives billing rules, rate eligibility, and operational handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tour|corporate|wedding|sports_team|airline_crew|government|other — promote to reference product]
reference: AHLA Group Business Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the group block record in the source system. Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for this entity. Used for incremental ETL processing.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; GDPR Article 5; SOX

description: Native block identifier from Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks module. Used for reconciliation and lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational source system.
Maximum number of rooms blocked on any single night within the group block period. Used for capacity planning, displacement analysis, and revenue management evaluation.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS Group Evaluation

description: Indicates whether this group has stayed at the property in a prior year or period. Used for loyalty recognition, preferential rate negotiation, and retention analytics.
Contractual deadline by which the group organizer must submit the complete rooming list with individual guest names and room type preferences.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; AHLA Group Contract Standards

description: Current status of the rooming list submission for this group block. Tracks whether the group organizer has provided individual guest names and room assignments required for pre-arrival processing.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; AHLA

description: Current count of individual reservations made against the group block (actual pickup). Compared against contracted_room_nights to calculate pickup rate and attrition exposure.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks; revenue.pickup_report

description: Number of suite-category rooms included in the contracted block. Tracked separately from standard rooms for inventory management and upsell reporting.
Indicates whether this group block has been designated as VIP, triggering elevated service protocols, dedicated concierge assignment, and executive-level attention.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Salesforce CRM; AHLA Guest Experience Standards

description: Estimated percentage of contracted rooms expected to be released back to general inventory based on historical pickup patterns for this group type. Used by revenue management for net block sizing and forecasting.
Unique surrogate identifier for each completed guest stay record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the stay_history data product. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS post-checkout records.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Channel attribution reporting and commission reconciliation require linking each stay to its originating booking_source. Revenue managers and finance teams run source-of-business reports at stay level

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: A stay booked under a promotional campaign offer must reference that offer for offer performance measurement, incremental revenue attribution, and loyalty points multiplier application. rate_plan_code

Reference to the corporate account associated with this stay, applicable when guest_type is 'corporate'. Links to negotiated rate agreements and corporate travel program tracking. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS accounts receivable module.

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Event revenue attribution requires linking actual guest stays to the originating event booking for group pickup analysis, post-event room revenue reporting, and attrition penalty calculation. Hospital

Reference to the group block associated with this stay, applicable when guest_type is 'group'. Links to group contract, pickup tracking, and group revenue attribution. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS group module.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Delphi by Amadeus

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Market segment production reporting: revenue managers join stay history to market segment definitions for ADR-by-segment, LOS analysis, and displacement studies. The current market_segment_code is a d

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Points accrual posting, stay credit reconciliation, and tier qualification tracking all require linking each stay record directly to the loyalty member account. Revenue and loyalty analysts run stay-l
Reference to the guest profile record representing the primary registered guest for this stay. Supports Lifetime Value (LTV) calculation, segmentation, and personalization across all properties.
Reference to the property where the stay occurred. Enables property-level performance analytics including ADR, RevPAR, and OCC reporting per USALI standards.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: Stay history must reference the specific physical room occupied for service recovery correlation, maintenance history cross-referencing, and guest complaint tracking by room. room_number is a denormal

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Stay history records which room type was occupied — essential for loyalty analytics, repeat-guest preference capture, ADR-by-room-type reporting, and USALI revenue reporting. room_type_code is a denor

Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the stay, calculated as total room revenue divided by the number of occupied room nights. A primary KPI per USALI and STR STAR Report standards. Expressed in the property's local currency. Used for RevPAR computation and competitive benchmarking via STR.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Total ancillary charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including spa, parking, laundry, telephone, and other non-room, non-F&B charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS. Contributes to TRevPAR and GOPPAR calculations per USALI.

Actual date the guest checked in to the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved arrival date in cases of early arrival or late arrival. Used as the principal business event date for stay analytics and STR benchmarking.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; STR STAR Report

description: Date on which the reservation was originally created (yyyy-MM-dd). Used to calculate booking lead time (days between booking_date and arrival_date), a key input for demand forecasting and pickup report analysis.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Exact date and time the guest completed the check-in process at the front desk or via mobile/digital check-in (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS front desk module. Used for operational SLA tracking and guest experience analytics.

Exact date and time the guest completed the checkout process (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Marks the point at which the stay record is finalized and posted to the stay history.

Indicates whether the stay was provided on a complimentary (no-charge) basis (True = complimentary; False = revenue-generating stay). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for USALI complimentary room reporting and GOP calculation.

Externally-known alphanumeric confirmation number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) at time of booking. Used by guests and agents to reference the stay. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Timestamp when this stay history record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse, typically upon post-checkout processing from Oracle OPERA PMS (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and ETL pipeline monitoring.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; GDPR Article 5; SOX

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this stay record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency properties and international financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.

Actual date the guest checked out of the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved departure date in cases of early departure or extended stay. Used in conjunction with arrival_date to compute Length of Stay (LOS).
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; STR STAR Report

description: Indicates whether the guest activated Do Not Disturb (DND) status during the stay (True = DND activated at any point; False = DND not activated). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS housekeeping module. Used for housekeeping scheduling and guest preference analytics.

Total Food and Beverage (F&B) charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including restaurant, bar, room service, and minibar charges. Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS. Contributes to TRevPAR calculation.

Overall Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) for the stay as captured by Medallia post-stay survey, typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-100 depending on survey instrument. Distinct from NPS; measures overall satisfaction rather than likelihood to recommend. Used for Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) reporting.

Classification of the guest stay by traveler category. 'fit' (Free Independent Traveler) indicates leisure individual; 'corporate' indicates business traveler under a negotiated account; 'group' indicates part of a group block; 'crew' indicates airline or transport crew; 'complimentary' indicates no-charge stay. Drives segmentation and LTV modeling.

Actual number of nights the guest stayed at the property, computed as departure_date minus arrival_date. A core metric for Average Length of Stay (ALOS) analysis, revenue management, and demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this stay, based on the applicable earning rate for the rate plan, room type, and member tier. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management. Used for loyalty program liability accrual and member engagement analytics.

The guest's loyalty program tier level at the time of the stay (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Captured as a snapshot to preserve historical tier context for LTV analysis and personalization, independent of the guest's current tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product as tiers vary by brand]

Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the guest for this stay, on a scale of 0-10. Sourced from Medallia post-stay survey. Used for Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) tracking, service recovery identification, and experience analytics. Null if guest did not respond to survey.
Count of adult guests (18 years and older) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for occupancy reporting, F&B revenue forecasting, and capacity planning.
Count of child guests (under 18 years) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for family segment analytics, amenity planning, and F&B revenue forecasting.
The arrival date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to measure early arrival or late arrival deviations and to support wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: The departure date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to identify early departure and extended stay patterns for demand forecasting and wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Primary payment instrument used to settle the guest folio at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Used for payment mix analysis and PCI DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|direct_bill|loyalty_points|bank_transfer|voucher|cryptocurrency — promote to reference product]

Number of nights from this stay that count toward the guest's loyalty program tier qualification. May differ from los_nights if the rate plan is non-qualifying (e.g., wholesale, complimentary). Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management.

Code identifying the rate plan under which the stay was booked (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate negotiated, package). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Critical for revenue management analysis and channel contribution reporting.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Total room charges posted to the guest folio for the entire stay, excluding taxes, fees, and ancillary charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Core component of RevPAR and TRevPAR calculations per USALI.

Indicates whether a service recovery action was initiated during or after this stay (True = service recovery case opened; False = no service recovery). Sourced from Medallia or Salesforce CRM case management. Used for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) calculation and experience analytics.

Outcome status of the guest stay upon checkout processing. 'completed' indicates normal checkout on scheduled departure date; 'early_departure' indicates guest checked out before scheduled departure; 'extended' indicates stay was prolonged beyond original departure date; 'no_show' indicates reservation was not cancelled but guest never arrived; 'cancelled' indicates reservation was cancelled post-check-in processing.

Total tax charges applied to the guest folio for the stay, including occupancy tax, VAT, city tax, and other applicable levies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering. Required for IFRS/GAAP financial reporting and SOX compliance.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IFRS/GAAP; SOX; USALI

description: Total amount charged to the guest folio for the entire stay, inclusive of room revenue, F&B revenue, ancillary revenue, and taxes. Represents the gross billing amount settled at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module.

Timestamp when this stay history record was last modified in the silver layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Captures post-checkout adjustments such as folio corrections, survey score updates, or loyalty point recalculations. Used for data lineage and audit compliance.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; GDPR Article 5; SOX

description: VIP designation code assigned to the guest for this stay in Oracle OPERA PMS (e.g., VIP1, VIP2, VVIP, CELEB). Drives special handling, amenity delivery, and management attention protocols. Null if guest has no VIP designation.
Unique identifier for the benefit entitlement record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Benefit entitlements (complimentary upgrades, lounge access) are frequently granted as fulfillment of a targeted campaign offer. This link supports offer attribution reporting and ensures entitlement

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Loyalty benefits such as complimentary breakfast or F&B dining credits are tied to specific outlets for operational delivery. Front-of-house staff need outlet-scoped entitlement records to validate an

Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Elite member benefits include complimentary meeting room access (e.g., Platinum boardroom entitlement). Linking benefit_entitlement to meeting_space enables operational fulfillment teams to confirm av
Reference to the loyalty program member who holds this benefit entitlement.

Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Tier benefits frequently include complimentary access to specific experience programs (e.g., platinum members receive a complimentary culinary program enrollment). Benefit entitlement must reference t

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: benefit_entitlement has an entitlement_source (STRING) field that describes how the benefit was granted. When a benefit is granted as a result of a promotion (e.g., a promotional upgrade benefit, a bo

Foreign key linking to spa.treatment. Business justification: Loyalty benefit entitlements for Platinum/Gold members frequently specify a particular spa treatment (e.g., 'Complimentary 60-min Deep Tissue Massage'). Linking to spa.treatment enables automated book
Reference to the loyalty tier that grants this benefit as a standard entitlement. Null if the benefit is individually granted outside of tier benefits.
The minimum number of days in advance that a reservation must be made to redeem this benefit. Null if no advance booking requirement exists.
Indicates whether this benefit is automatically applied to eligible reservations or requires manual member selection. True if auto-applied, False if opt-in required.
Detailed description of the benefit, including terms, conditions, and any restrictions or exclusions.
Human-readable name of the benefit entitlement for display and communication purposes.
The category of benefit granted to the member. Examples include complimentary breakfast, room upgrade, late checkout, lounge access, welcome amenity, or bonus points multiplier.
Comma-separated list of date ranges during which this benefit cannot be redeemed, typically during peak demand periods or special events.
The brand code if this benefit is restricted to properties within a specific brand. Null if no brand restriction applies.
Indicates whether this benefit can be combined with other benefits or promotions during the same stay. True if combinable, False if mutually exclusive.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value of this benefit.
The date from which this benefit entitlement becomes valid and can be redeemed by the member.
Comma-separated list of property codes where this benefit can be redeemed. Populated only when property_restriction is SPECIFIC.

The origin or reason for granting this benefit. Tier standard benefits are automatic based on member tier; status match benefits are granted when matching status from another program; manual grants are discretionary; promotion benefits are campaign-driven; compensation benefits are service recovery; partner benefits come from co-branded or partner integrations.

Current lifecycle status of the benefit entitlement. Active entitlements can be redeemed; suspended entitlements are temporarily unavailable; expired entitlements have passed their validity period; revoked entitlements have been withdrawn; pending entitlements are awaiting activation.
The date on which this benefit entitlement expires and can no longer be redeemed. Null for benefits with no expiration.
The date and time when this benefit entitlement was created or granted to the member.
The date and time when this benefit entitlement record was last updated in the system.
The maximum length of stay (LOS) in nights for which this benefit can be applied. Null if no maximum stay restriction exists.
The minimum length of stay (LOS) in nights required to redeem this benefit. Null if no minimum stay requirement exists.
The estimated monetary value of this benefit in the property's base currency, used for program economics tracking and liability calculation.
Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this benefit entitlement.
The bonus points multiplier applied to eligible stays when this benefit is active. For example, 1.5 means the member earns 50% bonus points. Null if this benefit does not include a points multiplier.
The priority order for applying this benefit when multiple benefits are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve conflicts when benefits cannot be combined.
Indicates whether this benefit can be redeemed at all properties, only at specific properties, within a specific brand, or within a specific geographic region.
The geographic region code if this benefit is restricted to properties within a specific region. Null if no region restriction applies.
The number of redemptions remaining for this benefit. Calculated as usage_limit minus usage_count. Null if usage is unlimited.
The business reason or justification for revoking this benefit entitlement. Null if the benefit has not been revoked.
The date and time when this benefit entitlement was revoked. Null if the benefit has not been revoked.
The name of the operational system that originated this benefit entitlement record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle OPERA PMS).
The unique identifier of this benefit entitlement in the source operational system.
Indicates whether this benefit can be transferred to another loyalty member. True if transferable, False if non-transferable.
The number of times this benefit has been redeemed to date. Used to track remaining usage against the usage limit.
The maximum number of times this benefit can be redeemed during its validity period. Null indicates unlimited usage.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program enrollment transaction. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Enrollments driven by 'join and earn' marketing campaigns must be linked to the originating campaign for campaign ROI reporting on enrollment volume and quality. loyalty_enrollment.campaign_code is a

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Enrollment campaigns include specific offers (welcome bonus points, eligible rate plans). Linking loyalty_enrollment to campaign_offer enables offer fulfillment tracking and ensures welcome bonus term

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Enrollments must link to distribution channel for measuring channel effectiveness in driving new member acquisition, calculating member acquisition cost by channel, and optimizing enrollment campaigns

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: loyalty_enrollment currently stores the initial tier granted at enrollment as a free-text string (initial_tier). Normalizing this to a FK initial_tier_id → tier.tier_id enforces referential integrity,
Reference to the guest who enrolled in the loyalty program. Links to the guest master record.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: A loyalty enrollment event is always associated with a specific loyalty program configuration (program version, terms version, points policy in effect at enrollment time). loyalty_enrollment currently
Reference to the property where the enrollment occurred. Null for digital enrollments not associated with a specific property.

Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: Enrollment at check-in or during a stay is a primary acquisition channel in hospitality loyalty programs. Linking loyalty_enrollment to the triggering reservation_booking enables welcome-bonus points
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the enrollment was initiated.
Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Type of device used to complete the enrollment. Used for channel and user experience analysis.
Indicates whether the member submitted proof of status from the competitor program. Populated only for status match enrollments.
Indicates whether the member consented to receive email communications at the time of enrollment.
The date the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Represents the business event date of program membership initiation.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Externally visible unique enrollment transaction number used for tracking and customer service reference.
Current status of the enrollment transaction. Tracks the lifecycle state of the enrollment record.
Precise date and time when the enrollment was completed, including timezone information. Used for operational tracking and SLA measurement.
IP address from which the enrollment was submitted. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.
Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language preference selected during enrollment.
Timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The date when the status match tier expires and the member must requalify. Populated only for status match enrollments.
The tier level granted in our program as a result of the status match. Populated only for status match enrollments.
Free-text notes or comments recorded by the enrollment agent or system regarding special circumstances or details of the enrollment.
Indicates whether the member accepted the privacy policy at enrollment. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.
Version number of the privacy policy that the member accepted at enrollment. Used for audit and compliance tracking.
Indicates whether the member consented to receive mobile app push notifications at the time of enrollment.
Referral code used by the enrolling member if they were referred by an existing member. Used for member-get-member program tracking.
Indicates whether the member must meet standard tier qualification criteria to retain the matched tier after expiry. Populated only for status match enrollments.
Indicates whether the member consented to receive SMS text message communications at the time of enrollment.
reference: GDPR Article 7, CCPA Section 1798.120, TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act)

description: Name of the competitor loyalty program from which status was matched. Populated only for status match enrollments.
The operational system that originated the enrollment record (e.g., Salesforce CRM, OPERA PMS, Mobile App).
The tier level the member claimed to hold in the source competitor program. Populated only for status match enrollments.
Indicates whether this enrollment was a competitive status match acquisition from another loyalty program.
Indicates whether the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions at enrollment. Required for legal compliance.
Version number of the terms and conditions that the member accepted at enrollment. Used for audit and compliance tracking.
Number of loyalty points awarded as a welcome bonus upon enrollment. Zero if no welcome bonus was offered.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program member. Primary key.
reference: HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) - Loyalty Data Standards

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Member acquisition costs are allocated to the enrolling property's cost center for departmental P&L. Marketing ROI analysis and CAC (customer acquisition cost) reporting by department require linking

Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Members are assigned to marketing segments for targeted campaign delivery, personalization, and offer optimization. Essential for marketing operations to identify which loyalty members belong to which
Foreign key reference to the property where the member enrolled in the loyalty program, if enrollment occurred at a physical location.

FK to guest.profile.profile_id — MUST-HAVE: Loyalty member must resolve to guest identity for personalization, stay history correlation, and profile merge operations. Without this FK, loyalty benefits cannot be applied at check-in.
Foreign key reference to the guest domain. Links the loyalty member to their guest profile which owns PII and stay history.
Foreign key reference to the property that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location.
Foreign key reference to the loyalty tier product. Represents the current tier assignment (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Elite).
Date when the membership account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.
Reason for membership account closure. Used for churn analysis and program improvement.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive marketing communications and promotional offers from the loyalty program.
reference: GDPR Article 6, CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Timestamp when the member record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Member's preferred currency for displaying points value and pricing. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code.
Current available points balance that can be redeemed by the member. Reflects earned points minus redeemed and expired points.
Indicates whether the member has consented to receive email communications. Separate from general communication opt-in for channel-specific consent.
reference: GDPR Article 6, CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Channel through which the member enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for channel effectiveness analysis.
Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the beginning of the member lifecycle.
Member's preferred language for communications and service delivery. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.
Date of the member's most recent completed stay. Used for recency analysis and churn prediction.
Total number of room nights stayed by the member since enrollment. Used for tier qualification and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculation.
Total cumulative points earned by the member since enrollment. Used for tier qualification and lifetime value tracking.
Total revenue generated by the member across all stays, F&B (Food and Beverage), and ancillary services since enrollment. Key LTV (Lifetime Value) metric.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Total number of completed stays by the member since enrollment. Key metric for member engagement and tier qualification.
Externally-known unique membership identifier assigned to the loyalty program member. Used for member recognition and program transactions.
Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership account. Active members can accrue and redeem points; suspended or closed accounts have restricted access.
Date of the member's next confirmed reservation. Used for engagement tracking and personalized offers.
Most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) rating provided by the member. Scale of -100 to 100, measuring likelihood to recommend the brand.
Indicates whether the member has consented to share their data with loyalty program partners (airlines, car rentals, etc.) for cross-program benefits.
reference: GDPR Article 6, CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Total cumulative points that have expired due to inactivity or program rules.
Total cumulative points redeemed by the member for rewards, upgrades, or benefits since enrollment.
Member's preferred room type for reservations. Used for personalized booking experiences and upgrade offers.
SALT (Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking) composite score measuring member satisfaction and loyalty. Proprietary metric combining multiple engagement and satisfaction indicators.
Indicates whether the member has consented to receive SMS/text message communications.
reference: GDPR Article 6, CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Date when the member's current tier status expires. Used for tier renewal campaigns and retention efforts.
Date when the member qualified for their current tier. Used for tier anniversary tracking and benefit eligibility.
Timestamp when the member record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Indicates whether the member is designated as a VIP for special recognition and service. May be based on tier, LTV (Lifetime Value), or manual assignment.
Number of room nights stayed by the member in the current calendar year. Used for annual tier qualification.
Total revenue generated by the member in the current calendar year. Used for tier qualification and annual performance tracking.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Number of completed stays by the member in the current calendar year. Used for annual tier qualification.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program configuration record. Primary key. This is the single source of truth configuration governing program-wide business rules and economics.

Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: A loyalty program configuration is scoped to a brand (e.g., Marriott Bonvoy for Marriott brands). Linking program_config to brand enables brand-level loyalty program governance, co-branded marketing r

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Hotel loyalty programs maintain a dedicated deferred revenue liability GL account configured at the program level (IFRS 15 / ASC 606 compliance). Role-prefix 'liability_' used because program_config h

business_glossary_term: API (Application Programming Interface) Integration Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether external systems can integrate with this loyalty program via API. True = API access available for partners and properties; False = manual integration only.
Estimated percentage of earned points that will never be redeemed (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used for USALI-compliant revenue recognition and liability estimation. Based on historical redemption patterns.
Indicates whether members can combine cash and points for redemptions. True = hybrid payment allowed; False = points-only or cash-only redemptions.

business_glossary_term: CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether this program configuration meets CCPA requirements for California resident data. True = CCPA controls implemented; False = non-California program or pending compliance.
Date when this specific configuration version became effective. Used for time-based rule application and member communication of program changes.
Date when this configuration version expires and is replaced by a new version. Null if currently active with no planned end date.
Primary email address for member inquiries and loyalty program support. Used for member communication and published on program website.
Primary phone number for member services and loyalty program support. Used for member communication materials and website display.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the points-to-currency conversion rate (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Defines the monetary unit for program economics.
Timestamp when this configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Name of the loyalty currency unit (e.g., 'Points', 'Miles', 'Credits', 'Stars'). This is the term displayed to members for their balance.
Number of months member data is retained after account closure, per GDPR and CCPA requirements. After this period, PII is purged per right-to-erasure regulations.
Indicates whether members can pool points with family members into a shared account. True = pooling allowed; False = pooling disabled.

business_glossary_term: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether this program configuration meets GDPR requirements for EU member data. True = GDPR controls implemented; False = non-EU program or pending compliance.
Indicates whether members can gift points to other members. True = gifting allowed; False = gifting disabled. Subject to anti-fraud controls.
Number of months of account inactivity (no earn or redeem transactions) before points expire, if points_expiry_policy is 'inactivity_based'. Null otherwise.

Accounting method for estimating loyalty program liability. incremental_cost = cost to fulfill; fair_value = market value of rewards; redemption_value = points * conversion rate. Must align with USALI and ASC 606.
Indicates whether the program offers lifetime elite status after achieving cumulative thresholds. True = lifetime tiers available; False = annual re-qualification required.
Maximum number of points a member can earn from a single stay, regardless of spend. Null if no cap applies. Used to manage program liability.
Minimum number of points required to initiate any redemption transaction. Prevents micro-redemptions and reduces operational costs.
Indicates whether a dedicated mobile application is available for this loyalty program. True = mobile app available; False = web-only access.
Indicates whether members can transfer points to partner programs (airlines, car rentals, etc.). True = transfers allowed; False = transfers disabled.

Rule governing when points expire. no_expiry = points never expire; inactivity_based = expire after N months of no activity; fixed_period = expire after N months from earn date; rolling_expiry = expire on a rolling window.
Indicates whether members can purchase additional points with cash. True = purchase allowed; False = earn-only program. Subject to regulatory limits in some jurisdictions.
Exchange rate for converting loyalty points to monetary value (e.g., 0.0100 means 100 points = 1 USD). Used for redemption valuation and breakage accounting per USALI standards.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this loyalty program in operational systems (e.g., 'HNRS', 'ELITE01'). Used for system integration and reporting.
Date when the loyalty program was originally launched. Used for program anniversary communications and historical analysis.
Official marketing name of the loyalty program (e.g., 'Honors Rewards', 'Elite Guest Club'). This is the customer-facing brand name.
