description: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the forecast model for this property-segment-horizon combination, based on historical back-testing. Lower values indicate higher model accuracy. Used to weight forecast confidence and flag low-accuracy forecasts for revenue manager review.
The stay date (arrival date) for which the demand forecast values apply. Represents the future date being predicted, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report

description: The dimensional level at which this forecast record is generated. 'property' is the highest level; 'room_type_segment' is the most granular, combining room type and market segment dimensions as supported by IDeaS G3 RMS.

Number of days from the forecast run date to the forecast date, representing how far into the future this forecast projects. For example, a forecast run on 2024-01-01 for a stay date of 2024-04-01 has a horizon of 90 days. Used to assess forecast accuracy by horizon bucket.

Classification of the algorithmic approach used to generate the forecast. 'time_series' uses historical booking patterns; 'regression' uses demand drivers; 'machine_learning' uses ML-based models; 'hybrid' combines multiple approaches; 'manual_override' indicates a revenue manager manually adjusted the system forecast.

Version identifier of the demand forecasting model or algorithm used to generate this forecast record (e.g., 'IDeaS-G3-v8.2', 'EzRMS-v5.1'). Enables model performance tracking, A/B comparison, and audit of forecast methodology changes.

Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the Revenue Management System (RMS) to the specific forecast generation run that produced this record. Enables traceability back to the originating RMS batch or on-demand run.

The date and time when this forecast record was generated by the Revenue Management System. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Current lifecycle state of this forecast record. 'active' indicates the current operative forecast; 'superseded' indicates a newer run has replaced it; 'draft' indicates a preliminary run not yet published to pricing decisions; 'published' indicates the forecast has been released to the Revenue Management System for pricing action; 'archived' indicates historical retention only.

Classification of the forecast methodology or purpose. 'unconstrained' reflects true market demand without inventory limits; 'constrained' reflects demand limited by available inventory; 'group_evaluation' is used for group block assessment in IDeaS G3 RMS; 'pickup' reflects incremental booking pace; 'budget' reflects the annual budget forecast baseline.
Flag indicating whether the forecast date falls on a public or national holiday in the property's market. Used to apply holiday demand adjustment factors in the forecasting model.

Flag indicating whether a revenue manager manually overrode the system-generated forecast values for this record. When true, the forecast reflects human judgment rather than pure algorithmic output. Used to track override frequency and assess model trust.

Flag indicating whether the forecast date coincides with a special event (e.g., conference, concert, sporting event, public holiday) that is expected to materially impact demand. When true, the forecast may incorporate event-specific demand uplift factors.

Forecasted Market Penetration Index (MPI), representing the property's projected occupancy share relative to the competitive set's projected occupancy. An MPI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to capture more than its fair share of market demand.
reference: STR STAR Report; Infor EzRMS; AHLA

description: Forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property's local currency for the forecast date. Represents the expected average revenue per occupied room. Core revenue management KPI used to drive pricing strategy.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report; USALI

description: Forecasted number of room-nights expected to cancel from the current on-books position before the forecast date. Used to calculate net demand and inform overbooking strategy.
Forecasted number of room-nights expected to result in no-show arrivals on the forecast date. Used alongside cancellation forecasts to calibrate overbooking levels and protect revenue.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Forecasted occupancy rate expressed as a percentage of available rooms expected to be sold on the forecast date. Calculated as constrained demand divided by total available rooms. Key input to RevPAR and yield optimization decisions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report; USALI

description: Forecasted incremental room-nights expected to be booked between the forecast run date and the forecast date. Represents the additional demand the RMS expects to materialize beyond rooms already on books.

Forecasted Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property's local currency for the forecast date. Derived as projected ADR multiplied by projected occupancy rate. Primary yield optimization metric used by Revenue Management.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report; USALI

description: Forecasted total room revenue in the property's local currency for the forecast date. Calculated as projected rooms sold multiplied by projected ADR. Used for budget variance analysis and revenue strategy.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI; IFRS/GAAP

description: Forecasted number of rooms available for sale on the forecast date after accounting for out-of-order, out-of-service, and maintenance blocks. Denominator for occupancy and RevPAR calculations.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI

description: Forecasted number of rooms expected to be sold (occupied) on the forecast date. Used for housekeeping scheduling, staffing planning, and inventory control decisions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI

description: Forecasted Revenue Generation Index (RGI), representing the property's projected RevPAR relative to the competitive set's projected RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to outperform its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data integrated into Infor EzRMS.
reference: STR STAR Report; Infor EzRMS; AHLA

description: Number of rooms already confirmed (on books) for the forecast date at the time the forecast was generated. Represents the definite demand component; the RMS adds pickup forecast to arrive at total projected demand.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; Infor EzRMS

description: Identifies the operational system of record that generated this demand forecast record. 'IDeaS_G3_RMS' indicates the IDeaS G3 Revenue Management System; 'Infor_EzRMS' indicates the Infor EzRMS system; 'Manual' indicates a revenue manager-entered forecast override.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; HTNG

description: Name or description of the special event influencing demand on the forecast date (e.g., 'Super Bowl LIX', 'New Year Eve', 'City Marathon'). Populated only when is_special_event is true.

The total forecasted room demand (in room-nights) without any inventory capacity constraints applied. Represents true market demand as estimated by the RMS model. Used to identify demand compression and inform inventory control decisions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Timestamp when this demand forecast record was last modified in the Silver Layer, such as when a revised forecast supersedes a prior run for the same forecast date and segment.
Unique surrogate identifier for each group business evaluation record generated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Primary key for the group_evaluation data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module

description: Identifier of the corporate account, travel agency, or group organizer account associated with this group inquiry, as maintained in Salesforce CRM and Oracle OPERA PMS AR module.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Account Object; Oracle OPERA PMS AR

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Group displacement analysis must account for active loyalty promotions during the group's stay dates, as promotions affect transient demand and displacement cost. Named process: promotion-adjusted gro

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Group evaluations originate from specific booking sources (group desk, travel agent, OTA group portal). FK enables channel-level group business analysis, commission calculation for group bookings, and

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Group evaluations compare displacement cost and net revenue impact against the property's financial budget. Revenue managers explicitly reference budgeted transient ADR and room revenue when making gr

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group RFPs often originate from marketing campaigns targeting corporate/SMIC segments. Linking the campaign helps measure campaign effectiveness in driving qualified group leads and conversion rates.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Group sales evaluations frequently reference specific campaign offers (e.g., a corporate group requesting a promotional rate tied to an active offer). Revenue managers must validate offer eligibility

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Group evaluations are channel-specific — the proposed group rate, displacement analysis, and commission terms depend on which distribution channel the group inquiry came through. FK enables channel-le

Foreign key linking to revenue.competitive_set. Business justification: group_evaluation.rgi_benchmark (DECIMAL) is a competitive index metric used in group displacement analysis. This metric is computed relative to a specific competitive set. Adding competitive_set_id li

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Group bookings often involve loyalty members as decision-makers, influencing pricing decisions. Business process: tracking member-led group inquiries for loyalty credit attribution and personalized gr

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: group_evaluation.demand_forecast_occupancy_pct (DECIMAL) is a denormalized copy of the projected occupancy from the demand forecast used in displacement cost calculations. Adding demand_forecast_id li

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Group evaluation in revenue management directly produces an accepted event booking. Revenue managers track which evaluation led to which confirmed booking for displacement analysis, conversion reporti

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Group evaluations assess displacement cost and net revenue impact within a fiscal period context. Revenue managers and finance teams review group business decisions against period budgets; this FK ena

Foreign key linking to event.function_space. Business justification: Group displacement analysis requires knowing which function spaces are requested to calculate total revenue displacement (rooms + space rental). Revenue managers evaluate function space availability a

Foreign key linking to event.inquiry. Business justification: Group evaluations are initiated from event inquiries in the standard MICE sales-to-revenue workflow. Linking evaluation to inquiry enables lead-to-evaluation conversion tracking, inquiry qualification

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Group booking decisions impact revenue recognition and require GL account mapping for group revenue tracking, displacement cost accounting, and profitability analysis.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Group business evaluations are classified by market segment (e.g., Group Corporate, Group Association, Group SMERF). Adding market_segment_id links each group evaluation to its market segment classifi

Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: Hotel group evaluations assess both room block revenue and meeting space requirements together. Linking group_evaluation to meeting_space enables total group revenue assessment including space rental

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_negotiated_rate. Business justification: Group evaluations for corporate or wholesale accounts may reference an existing negotiated rate agreement as the basis for the proposed group rate. Adding revenue_negotiated_rate_id links the group ev
Identifier of the hotel or resort property for which the group evaluation is being conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

Foreign key linking to event.proposal. Business justification: Revenue managers evaluate group business by reviewing the sales proposal to assess displacement cost and yield impact. The group evaluation record must reference the originating proposal to support MI

business_glossary_term: Proposed Banquet Menu Package Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_menu_package. Business justification: During group evaluation, revenue managers assess the proposed banquet menu package to calculate total group F&B revenue before a BEO is created. The existing primary_banquet_event_order_id covers post

Foreign key linking to revenue.rate_availability. Business justification: Group evaluations are conducted against the current rate availability snapshot for the requested stay dates. Adding rate_availability_id links the group evaluation to the specific rate availability re

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Group evaluations are performed for specific rate plans. group_evaluation has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N group_evaluation records → 1 revenue_rate_plan. Removes redund

Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Group displacement calculations require the seasonal transient demand baseline (estimated_occupancy_pct, estimated_adr, demand_classification) from seasonal_calendar. Linking group_evaluation to the s

Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Group business evaluations are made within the context of the active revenue strategy. The strategy defines minimum acceptable rates, target occupancy, and displacement thresholds that directly inform
Estimated ancillary revenue from the group including spa, parking, audio-visual, business center, and other non-room, non-F&B spend. Used in total spend calculation for net revenue impact assessment.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; USALI — Other Operated Departments

description: Proposed first night of the group room block. Used as the start of the displacement analysis window in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Expected attrition percentage for the group block by group type, representing the proportion of contracted rooms the group is expected not to use. Distinct from wash factor in that attrition is the contractual exposure, while wash is the statistical pickup adjustment.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Wash Factor Modeling; AHLA Group Business Standards

description: Alternative per-room per-night rate proposed by the revenue manager as a counter-offer to the group organizer when the proposed_group_rate is below the minimum_acceptable_rate. Populated only when revenue_manager_decision = 'counter_offer'.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was first captured in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage and ETL audit trail.
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; SOX — Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this evaluation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI and IFRS/GAAP standards.

The date by which unbooked rooms in the group block are released back to general inventory. After this date, the property may sell unreserved block rooms to transient guests. Critical for inventory management and wash factor actualization.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Group Block Management; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Free-text justification provided by the revenue manager when their decision overrides the RMS recommendation (e.g., strategic account relationship, future business potential, competitive displacement risk). Required when revenue_manager_decision differs from rms_recommendation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Timestamp when the revenue manager recorded their final accept, decline, or counter-offer decision. Used for SLA tracking and decision audit trail.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; SOX — Audit Trail Requirements

description: Proposed last night of the group room block (checkout date). Defines the end of the displacement analysis window.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Total estimated revenue lost from displaced transient business if the group block is accepted, calculated as transient_rooms_displaced multiplied by transient_adr_displaced across the stay period. Key component of the net revenue impact analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Displacement Analysis Module; USALI; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the group evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initial inquiry receipt through revenue manager decision. 'pending' = awaiting RMS analysis; 'under_review' = revenue manager reviewing; 'recommended_accept' = RMS recommends acceptance; 'recommended_decline' = RMS recommends decline; 'accepted' = revenue manager approved; 'declined' = revenue manager rejected.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: The principal real-world event timestamp when the group evaluation was initiated and processed by IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module

description: Estimated Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue contribution from the group, including banquet, catering, and outlet spend. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus event catering estimates and historical group F&B spend patterns.
reference: Delphi by Amadeus — Catering Module; USALI — F&B Department

description: Name of the group or event associated with the booking inquiry (e.g., 'Acme Corp Annual Sales Conference 2025'). Used for identification and reporting across Delphi by Amadeus and Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Delphi by Amadeus — Group Block Management; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Total projected room revenue from the group block at the proposed group rate, calculated as adjusted room block (post-wash) multiplied by proposed_group_rate across all nights. Gross revenue contribution from rooms only.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; USALI

description: Classification of the group by business segment. Drives wash factor parameters and attrition modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|association|government|leisure|sports|wedding|social|tour_operator|incentive — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Group Business Standards

description: Historical average wash percentage for this group type and property, derived from past group performance data in IDeaS G3 RMS. Serves as the baseline input before adjustments for lead time and current demand conditions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Wash Factor Modeling; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the group booking inquiry, as generated by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS). Used to cross-reference the evaluation with the originating inquiry across Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Indicates whether the group inquiry has converted to a definite (contracted) booking following a positive revenue manager decision. True = definite booking confirmed; False = still in tentative or evaluation stage.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Group Block Management; Delphi by Amadeus

description: Categorical bucket representing the number of days between the group inquiry date and the proposed arrival date. Used as a parameter in wash factor modeling, as groups booked further in advance typically exhibit higher wash rates.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Wash Factor Modeling; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Total number of nights in the proposed group stay, calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Represents the Length of Stay (LOS) for the group block used in displacement and revenue impact modeling.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; USALI

description: The minimum per-room per-night rate recommended by IDeaS G3 RMS below which accepting the group would result in negative net revenue impact after accounting for displacement costs. This is the RMS-generated floor rate for negotiation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Net revenue impact of accepting the group, calculated as total group spend (room + F&B + ancillary) minus displacement cost. Positive value indicates the group is accretive; negative value indicates the group destroys revenue versus transient alternative. Primary decision metric in the evaluation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Displacement Analysis Module; USALI; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Maximum number of rooms occupied on any single night within the group block. Used for capacity planning and displacement analysis at the peak demand point.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Group Business Standards

description: The per-room per-night rate proposed by the group organizer or sales manager for the room block, expressed in the property's operating currency. This is the rate under evaluation against the displacement cost and minimum acceptable rate.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; USALI; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Final decision made by the revenue manager after reviewing the RMS recommendation and displacement analysis. May align with or override the RMS recommendation. 'pending' indicates no decision has been recorded yet.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Estimated change in Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the property during the group stay dates if the group is accepted, compared to the transient-only forecast. Negative RevPAR impact is a key indicator for decline recommendation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; STR STAR Report; USALI

description: The property's Revenue Generation Index (RGI) from the STR STAR Report for the competitive set during the evaluation period. Used as a competitive context benchmark to assess whether accepting the group at the proposed rate is competitively rational.
reference: STR STAR Report — Revenue Generation Index (RGI); Infor EzRMS — Competitive Benchmarking

description: Automated accept, decline, or counter-offer recommendation generated by IDeaS G3 RMS based on displacement analysis, wash factor modeling, and net revenue impact. This is the system-generated recommendation prior to revenue manager override.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module

description: Total number of guest rooms requested in the group block across all nights of the stay. This is the peak or contracted room block size as submitted in the group inquiry.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The native record identifier from the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation ID or Delphi by Amadeus opportunity ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Group Evaluation Module; Delphi by Amadeus

description: The forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) of the transient business that would be displaced by the group block, as estimated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Used to quantify the revenue opportunity cost of accepting the group.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Displacement Analysis Module; STR STAR Report; USALI

description: Estimated number of transient (individual) room nights that would be displaced if the group block is accepted, as calculated by IDeaS G3 RMS displacement analysis. Core input to the net revenue impact calculation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Displacement Analysis Module; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, including revenue manager decision updates or status changes.
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; SOX — Audit Trail Requirements

description: The adjusted wash factor percentage applied to the contracted room block to estimate actual pickup. Derived from historical wash percentage, group type attrition patterns, and booking lead time bucket. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15% wash). Used to calculate the adjusted room block for revenue modeling.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Wash Factor Modeling; AHLA Group Business Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each pickup report record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete revenue management snapshot event tracking booking pace against forecast for a given stay date and property.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Pickup velocity spikes are frequently driven by loyalty promotions; revenue managers correlate booking pace with active promotions in daily pickup reports. Named process: promotion-driven pickup veloc

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports are used to measure whether a marketing campaign is driving booking pace — a named revenue management process. Revenue managers monitor pickup reports during campaign flights t

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Pickup reports track booking pace by channel — a core revenue management daily process. FK from pickup_report to channel enables channel-level pickup velocity analysis and channel mix optimization dec

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pickup velocity and booking pace are tracked by cost center for departmental revenue forecasting and operational planning at the department level.

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: pickup_report tracks reservation pace against forecast for a given stay date. Adding demand_forecast_id links each pickup report to the specific demand forecast it measures pace against. The forecaste

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports track room block pickup rates for specific event bookings against cutoff dates. Essential for group pace reporting, attrition risk assessment, and event booking performance mon

Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Pickup reports monitor room pickup velocity against group blocks — the event_group_block is the primary source of group room pickup data. Revenue managers use this link to track block-to-pickup ratios

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Pickup reports are used in period-close revenue reconciliation processes. Finance teams require pickup data tagged to fiscal periods for month-end accruals and revenue recognition reporting under USAL

Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: Revenue managers run F&B pickup reports segmented by revenue center (restaurant vs. bar vs. banquet) to track F&B pace alongside room pickup for total revenue management. The existing monitored_fnb_ou

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Pickup reports track booking pace by market segment. pickup_report has segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N pickup_report records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant segment_cod

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Pickup reports track outlet reservation pace for outlets accepting reservations (fine dining, specialty restaurants). Required for outlet capacity planning, labor scheduling, inventory management alig
Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this pickup report is generated. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Property Configuration Module

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: pickup_report.rate_plan_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the rate plan being tracked. Adding revenue_rate_plan_id normalizes this to the authoritative revenue_rate_plan record. rate_plan_c

Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports are analyzed in the context of seasonal demand periods to assess booking pace relative to seasonal norms. Linking pickup_report to seasonal_calendar enables pace-vs-season benc

Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Pickup reports measure booking pace against the revenue strategy targets for a given stay date. Linking pickup_report to strategy enables revenue managers to assess whether pace is on track to meet st

Average Daily Rate (ADR) of all rooms currently on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. Calculated as total room revenue on books divided by rooms_on_books. Core revenue performance indicator alongside occupancy pace. Expressed in the property's operating currency.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); STR STAR Report benchmarking standard; IDeaS G3 RMS

business_glossary_term: Average Daily Rate (ADR) Variance to Forecast
description: Difference between adr_on_books and forecasted_adr (adr_on_books minus forecasted_adr). Positive value indicates rate is tracking above forecast; negative value indicates rate is below forecast. Used alongside rooms_variance_to_forecast to assess overall revenue pace.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Timestamp when this pickup report record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (data integrity and accountability); SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — audit trail requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this pickup report record (adr_on_books, forecasted_adr, revpar_on_books, forecasted_revpar, prior_year_adr, adr_variance_to_forecast). Typically the property's local operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP (International Financial Reporting Standards / Generally Accepted Accounting Principles)

description: Day of the week corresponding to the stay_date (e.g., Monday, Friday, Saturday). Critical demand pattern dimension — weekday vs weekend demand profiles differ significantly in hospitality, driving different pricing strategies and pace expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Demand Forecasting; Infor EzRMS — Competitive Benchmarking

description: Revenue management system classification of expected demand intensity for the stay_date as of the report_date. Drives pricing strategy and inventory control decisions: 'distressed' triggers promotional actions; 'peak' triggers rate maximization and LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Infor EzRMS — Revenue Strategy

description: Revenue management system forecast for the expected ADR (Average Daily Rate) on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Baseline against which adr_on_books is compared to assess rate performance vs plan.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Forecasting Module; Infor EzRMS — Revenue Strategy

description: Revenue management system forecast for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) on the stay_date. Baseline against which revpar_on_books is compared to assess overall revenue pace vs plan. Expressed in the property's operating currency.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Forecasting Module; Infor EzRMS — Revenue Strategy; USALI

description: Revenue management system forecast for total rooms expected to be occupied on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this report snapshot. Baseline against which rooms_on_books pace is measured to compute variance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Forecasting Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Indicates whether the stay_date coincides with a designated special event (conference, concert, sporting event, holiday, local festival) that materially impacts demand. When true, pace expectations and pricing strategies are adjusted relative to normal demand patterns.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Event Calendar; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Indicates whether the stay_date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Boolean flag used to segment pickup reports into weekday vs weekend cohorts for pace analysis, as weekend demand patterns and pricing strategies differ materially from weekday.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Demand Forecasting; Infor EzRMS

description: Number of days between the report_date and the stay_date (stay_date minus report_date). Represents the booking window or days-out at the time of this snapshot. Critical for pace analysis — revenue managers compare pickup velocity across standard lead-time intervals (90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1 days out).
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Length of Stay (LOS) Restriction Active Flag
description: Indicates whether a LOS (Length of Stay) restriction (minimum stay requirement) is currently active for the stay_date. LOS restrictions are a key revenue management lever applied during high-demand periods to maximize total revenue across multiple nights.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to book the stay_date, when a LOS restriction is active. Null when los_restriction_active is false. Used to enforce stay-through requirements during high-demand periods to prevent short-stay displacement of longer, higher-value bookings.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution

description: Free-text annotations entered by the revenue manager to document observations, strategy decisions, or contextual factors affecting the pickup pace for this stay_date (e.g., 'Rate opened on OTA due to slow pace', 'Group block released — expect transient pickup'). Supports audit trail and strategy documentation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Rate of new reservations being added per day for the stay_date, calculated as the incremental rooms picked up divided by the number of days elapsed in the measurement window. Expressed as rooms per day. Enables revenue managers to assess acceleration or deceleration of booking pace.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: ADR (Average Daily Rate) on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time. Enables year-over-year rate comparison to assess whether current pricing strategy is yielding rate improvement or erosion versus the prior period.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; STR STAR Report benchmarking standard; USALI

description: Number of rooms that were on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time (days out). Enables year-over-year (YOY) pace comparison — a standard revenue management benchmarking technique to contextualize current booking pace against historical performance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; STR STAR Report benchmarking standard; Infor EzRMS

description: The calendar date on which this pickup snapshot was captured. Represents the 'as-of' date for all rooms-on-books and ADR figures in this record. The difference between stay_date and report_date defines the lead time (days out). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP requirement (date-precision is standard for daily pickup reports).
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Externally-known business identifier for this pickup report record, typically formatted as PKP-{PropertyCode}-{StayDate}-{SequenceNumber}. Used by revenue managers to reference specific pickup snapshots in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Current lifecycle state of this pickup report record. 'draft' indicates the snapshot is being assembled; 'published' indicates it is available for revenue manager consumption; 'superseded' indicates a later snapshot for the same stay_date/report_date exists; 'cancelled' indicates the report was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module

description: Classification of the pickup report generation cadence: 'daily' for standard automated daily snapshots, 'weekly' for weekly pace summaries, 'monthly' for month-end pace reviews, or 'adhoc' for analyst-triggered on-demand reports. Determines the reporting frequency context for pace trend analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Revenue Strategy

business_glossary_term: Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) on Books
description: Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) derived from current on-books position, calculated as (adr_on_books multiplied by rooms_on_books) divided by total_available_rooms. Composite pace metric combining rate and occupancy performance. Expressed in the property's operating currency.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); STR STAR Report benchmarking standard; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Room type classification for which this pickup report is scoped (e.g., STD, DLX, STE, KNG, DBL). When null, the report covers all room types for the property. Enables room-type-level pace analysis to support inventory controls and upsell strategy.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Room Type Configuration; IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls

description: Total number of rooms confirmed on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. This is the primary pace indicator — the current committed inventory position. Revenue managers compare this against forecast and prior-year actuals to assess demand trajectory.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Oracle OPERA PMS — Reservations

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 14 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 14 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 14 days prior to stay_date). Critical short-window pace indicator used to trigger last-minute pricing tactics and inventory controls.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 1 Day Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 1 day (i.e., the snapshot captured 1 day prior to stay_date). Final pre-arrival pace indicator representing near-final committed occupancy before day-of walk-ins.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Oracle OPERA PMS — Reservations

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 30 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 30 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 30 days prior to stay_date). Key pace benchmark for near-term demand assessment and rate strategy adjustments.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 60 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 60 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 60 days prior to stay_date). Standard pace benchmark used by revenue managers to assess mid-range booking momentum.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 7 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 7 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 7 days prior to stay_date). Near-arrival pace indicator used to assess last-week demand and trigger promotional or restriction actions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 90 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 90 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 90 days prior to stay_date). Used as a historical pace benchmark for comparison against current rooms_on_books to measure pickup velocity over the 90-day window.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Difference between rooms_on_books and forecasted_rooms (rooms_on_books minus forecasted_rooms). Positive value indicates ahead-of-pace (over-performing vs forecast); negative value indicates behind-pace (under-performing). Primary KPI for revenue manager intervention decisions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; Infor EzRMS — Revenue Strategy

description: Operational system of record from which this pickup report data was sourced (IDeaS_G3_RMS, Infor_EzRMS, OPERA_PMS, or Manual for analyst-entered overrides). Enables data lineage tracking and source-system reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Technology (HTNG) data standards

description: Name or description of the special event associated with the stay_date when is_special_event is true (e.g., 'Super Bowl Weekend', 'Annual Tech Conference', 'New Year's Eve'). Provides context for anomalous pace patterns in revenue management reporting.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Event Calendar; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: The specific calendar date for which booking pace and pickup velocity are being measured. This is the future arrival/occupancy date that revenue managers are monitoring. Central dimension of the pickup report — all rooms-on-books and ADR snapshots relate to this date.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pickup Report Module; STR STAR Report benchmarking standard

description: Total number of rooms available for sale for the stay_date at the property (net of out-of-order and out-of-service rooms). Denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculation and RevPAR computation. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS inventory controls.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Housekeeping and Inventory; USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Timestamp when this pickup report record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — audit trail requirements; GDPR Article 5

description: Primary key for strategy

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue strategies are implemented at cost center level for tactical execution, departmental accountability, and operational performance management.

Reference to the demand forecast record that informed and underpins this revenue strategy. Links to the revenue.demand_forecast product to provide the analytical basis for strategy targets and tactical decisions.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Revenue strategies are anchored to fiscal planning periods for budget cycle alignment, target-setting (RevPAR, GOPPAR), and period-close variance reporting. A travel-hospitality revenue manager always

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue strategies explicitly target GSS improvement as a demand driver (experience-driven revenue strategy). Strategic plans link rate positioning to satisfaction goals—properties pursuing rate growt

Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Revenue strategies are defined at multiple organizational levels (property, region, brand portfolio) aligned with hierarchy nodes. A hierarchy_id on strategy enables regional and portfolio-level prici

Foreign key linking to finance.hma_contract. Business justification: Revenue strategies must be calibrated against HMA performance test thresholds and fee structures. Revenue managers reference HMA terms (base fee basis, incentive fee triggers) when setting target RevP

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: strategy.primary_market_segment_focus (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the primary market segment the strategy targets. Adding market_segment_id normalizes this to the authoritative market_segm

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue strategies in full-service hotels are scoped to profit centers (rooms, F&B, spa, events) for GOPPAR and TRevPAR targeting. Finance and revenue management jointly set profit-center-level strate

Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this revenue strategy applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Property Standards

description: Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Revenue managers build pricing strategies around seasonal demand periods defined in seasonal_calendar (demand_classification, estimated_revpar, blackout dates). Linking strategy to the seasonal period
Reference to the newer revenue strategy record that replaced this one when status is 'superseded'. Enables lineage tracking of strategy evolution and supports audit trail for revenue governance.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Revenue strategies are often tier-targeted (e.g., drive Platinum member bookings in shoulder season, protect Gold tier inventory). Named process: tier-targeted revenue strategy planning and execution.

Date and time when the strategy status transitioned to 'active' and began governing live pricing and inventory decisions. Distinct from approval timestamp — a strategy may be approved in advance and activated at a later point.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Name or employee identifier of the Revenue Director or authorized revenue management leader who formally approved this strategy. Provides governance and audit trail for strategic revenue decisions per SOX internal controls requirements.

Date and time when the revenue strategy was formally approved by the authorizing Revenue Director. Establishes the official approval event in the strategy lifecycle for governance and audit trail purposes.

The primary distribution channel(s) this strategy targets for demand generation (e.g., 'Direct Web', 'GDS', 'OTA', 'Voice/CRS', 'Corporate Direct'). Informs channel mix optimization and OTA partnership management decisions. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS channel codes.
Date and time when this revenue strategy record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the strategy lifecycle.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary target values in this strategy record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency property portfolios.

Categorizes the temporal scope of the strategy planning horizon. 'weekly' for short-term tactical adjustments, 'monthly' for rolling monthly plans, 'quarterly' for seasonal strategies, 'annual' for full-year budget-aligned strategies, 'event_based' for strategies tied to specific demand events.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report

description: The approved inventory management methodology for this strategy period. 'open' allows all bookings, 'hurdle_rate' applies minimum rate thresholds, 'length_of_stay' (LOS) enforces minimum/maximum stay restrictions, 'nested' uses tiered availability controls, 'closed' restricts availability for specific segments or channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA

description: Free-text narrative documenting the specific tactical actions approved as part of this strategy (e.g., 'Open BAR for all channels on shoulder nights', 'Apply 5-night minimum LOS restriction over peak weekend', 'Close OTA channels when occupancy exceeds 85%'). Provides governance documentation for revenue decisions.
reference: USALI; SOX Section 302; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: The scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this revenue strategy. Drives workflow triggers in the revenue management calendar and ensures timely strategy adjustments based on actual vs. target performance.
reference: AHLA Revenue Management Standards; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Additional free-text commentary, rationale, or contextual notes provided by the revenue management team regarding this strategy. May include market intelligence, competitive observations, or risk considerations not captured in structured fields.
The last date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Together with planning_horizon_start_date, defines the full strategy window for yield optimization and performance tracking.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: The first date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Defines the beginning of the stay-date window over which the strategy governs pricing and inventory decisions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: The approved pricing methodology governing rate-setting for this strategy period. 'bar_based' uses Best Available Rate (BAR) as the anchor, 'dynamic' applies algorithmic Dynamic Rate Rules (DRR), 'fixed' uses static rate plans, 'hybrid' combines approaches, 'competitive_parity' aligns rates to competitive set benchmarks.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: The approved cadence at which this strategy is scheduled for performance review and potential revision by the revenue management team. Governs the operational rhythm of the revenue management process.
reference: AHLA Revenue Management Standards; IDeaS G3 RMS

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Strategy Reference ID
description: The native strategy or scenario identifier from the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Enables traceability back to the originating RMS record for reconciliation and system-of-record alignment.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this strategy record originated. 'IDeaS_G3_RMS' for strategies generated by IDeaS G3, 'Infor_EzRMS' for Infor-sourced strategies, 'Manual' for strategies entered directly by revenue management staff.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; HTNG

description: Indicates whether this strategy was created in response to a specific demand-driving special event (e.g., major conference, sporting event, concert, holiday period). When True, the strategy may include event-specific pricing premiums and inventory controls.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA

description: Name of the demand-driving special event associated with this strategy when special_event_flag is True (e.g., 'Super Bowl LX', 'Annual Medical Conference', 'New Year Holiday Period'). Provides context for strategy rationale and post-event performance analysis.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this revenue strategy record within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS strategy identifiers.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Human-readable name describing the revenue strategy (e.g., 'Q3 2025 Transient Yield Push', 'Holiday Season Total Revenue Strategy'). Used for identification in reporting and governance workflows.

Current lifecycle state of the revenue strategy record. 'draft' indicates work-in-progress, 'pending_approval' awaits revenue director sign-off, 'approved' is authorized but not yet in effect, 'active' is currently governing pricing decisions, 'superseded' has been replaced by a newer strategy, 'archived' is retained for historical audit.
reference: USALI; SOX Section 302 — internal controls documentation; AHLA

description: Classification of the revenue strategy by business segment. 'transient' covers individual guest pricing, 'group' covers group block strategies, 'total_revenue' encompasses all revenue streams (TRevPAR focus), 'food_and_beverage' targets F&B revenue optimization, 'events' covers MICE and event revenue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|total_revenue|food_and_beverage|events|loyalty — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: The approved target Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property's operating currency for the strategy period. ADR is the primary pricing KPI representing average revenue earned per occupied room per night. Used to benchmark actual ADR performance against strategic intent.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: The approved target Average Rate Index (ARI) for the strategy period. ARI = Property ADR / Competitive Set ADR. Measures the property's rate positioning relative to its competitive set. Used alongside MPI and RGI for comprehensive competitive benchmarking.
reference: STR STAR Report; AHLA; Infor EzRMS

business_glossary_term: Target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: The approved target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR) for the strategy period. GOPPAR incorporates operating costs and provides a profitability-adjusted view of revenue performance, aligned with USALI GOP reporting standards.

The approved target Market Penetration Index (MPI) for the strategy period. MPI = Property Occupancy / Competitive Set Occupancy. Measures the property's occupancy share relative to its competitive set as defined in STR STAR Report.
reference: STR STAR Report; AHLA; Infor EzRMS

description: The approved target Occupancy Rate (OCC) expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for the strategy period. Represents the proportion of available rooms expected to be sold. Core input to RevPAR calculation and yield optimization decisions.

The approved target Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property's operating currency for the strategy period. RevPAR = ADR × Occupancy Rate. The primary composite KPI for hotel revenue performance and competitive benchmarking via STR STAR Report.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: The approved target Revenue Generation Index (RGI) for the strategy period. RGI = Property RevPAR / Competitive Set RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data.
reference: STR STAR Report; AHLA; Infor EzRMS

business_glossary_term: Target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR)
description: The approved target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) encompassing all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, spa, events, ancillary) per available room for the strategy period. Relevant for total revenue strategies beyond rooms-only yield optimization.
Name or employee identifier of the last person to modify this strategy record. Supports change management and audit trail for revenue governance.
Date and time when this revenue strategy record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change event for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Monotonically incrementing version number for this strategy record. Increments each time the strategy is revised and re-approved. Supports audit trail and change management for revenue governance workflows.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue management professional who created this strategy record. Supports governance, accountability, and audit trail requirements.

Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) applied to derive the current_bar for this room type and stay date. Links to the pricing strategy configuration governing automated rate adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS.

description: Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdle rates, overbooking caps) are adjusted based on event booking displacement analysis. Essential for inventory optimization around events and group block allocatio
