description: Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_group_block. Business justification: Inventory control records manage group_block_rooms allotments. Linking inventory_control to reservation_group_block enables allotment-vs-contracted room count reconciliation — a standard revenue/inven

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Inventory controls reserve allotments for specific loyalty tiers (e.g., guaranteed availability for Platinum members per program benefits). Named process: tier-based inventory allotment and guaranteed
Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, package) associated with this inventory control record. Determines the pricing tier and restrictions applied to the available inventory.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which this inventory control record is defined. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration and IDeaS G3 RMS room class setup.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: inventory_control.inventory_control_approach (STRING) references the strategic approach governing inventory decisions. Adding strategy_id links each inventory control record to the revenue strategy th

Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date allocated to contracted allotments (OTA allotments, wholesale, tour operator contracts). Managed via Sabre SynXis CRS channel management and reduces the freely-sellable transient inventory.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards

description: Designates the level of authority required to approve the overbooking level for this inventory control record. Ensures governance and accountability for overbooking decisions per property SOPs and SOX internal control requirements.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX Internal Controls; AHLA Overbooking Policy Guidelines

description: Real-time count of rooms available for sale for this property, room type, stay date, and rate plan combination at the time of last update. Reflects physical inventory minus confirmed reservations, group blocks, and out-of-order rooms. Distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS for reservation fulfillment.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this inventory control record as referenced in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Data Dictionary

description: Current lifecycle state of this inventory control record indicating whether the room type/rate plan combination is open for sale, closed, restricted, pending review, or subject to a manual override. This is the real-time availability state distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Data Dictionary

description: Classifies the category of inventory control applied to this record. Determines how the control_value is interpreted and enforced by the Revenue Management System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hurdle_rate|sell_limit|overbooking_level|los_restriction|cta_restriction|ctd_restriction|allotment|group_block — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; HTNG Data Dictionary

description: The operative value for this control record as applied by the Revenue Management System. Interpretation depends on control_type: for hurdle_rate it is the minimum acceptable rate in local currency; for sell_limit it is the maximum rooms to sell; for overbooking_level it is the number of rooms above physical capacity authorized to sell.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: The date and time when this inventory control record was first created in the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and SOX compliance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary rate fields in this record (current_bar, min_rate, max_rate, hurdle_rate). Ensures multi-currency properties report rates in the correct denomination.

The current Best Available Rate (BAR) in local currency for this room type and stay date as published via Sabre SynXis CRS and channel distribution. Represents the lowest publicly available non-restricted rate and is the primary pricing signal for transient demand.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Rate Transparency Guidelines

description: The date and time from which this inventory control record's control_value and restrictions became operative in the Revenue Management System. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical rate/availability grid reconstruction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; USALI

description: The date and time at which this inventory control record's control_value and restrictions ceased to be operative. Null for currently active records. Enables point-in-time historical analysis of the rate/availability grid.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; USALI

description: Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date allocated to group blocks (MICE, tour groups, corporate groups). Reduces the transient sell limit and is managed via Delphi by Amadeus group block management and IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Delphi by Amadeus; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Minimum acceptable rate (in local currency) below which a reservation for this room type on the stay date should not be accepted, as determined by the Revenue Management System. Used in displacement analysis and group evaluation in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Indicates whether arrivals are blocked for this room type and rate plan on the stay date (Closed to Arrival / CTA restriction). When True, guests may not check in on this date but may stay through it as part of a longer booking.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Indicates whether departures are blocked for this room type and rate plan on the stay date (Closed to Departure / CTD restriction). When True, guests may not check out on this date, requiring stays to extend beyond it.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Indicates whether the applied control_value represents a manual override of the system_recommended_value. True when a revenue manager has manually intervened; False when the system recommendation was accepted as-is. Critical for RMS model performance auditing.

The date and time when this inventory control record's rate and availability state was last successfully distributed to Sabre SynXis CRS and downstream channels (OTA, GDS). Critical for SLA monitoring and channel distribution reconciliation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; SLA Monitoring

description: Maximum number of nights a guest may stay when booking this room type at this rate plan on the stay date. Used to prevent long-stay displacement of higher-value transient demand during peak periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: The ceiling rate in local currency above which no rate plan for this room type and stay date may be sold. Used to enforce rate integrity, prevent rate gouging, and comply with local pricing regulations during high-demand periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA; Local Pricing Regulations

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this room type at this rate plan on the stay date. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction used to optimize revenue during high-demand periods by requiring longer stays.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: The absolute floor rate in local currency below which no rate plan for this room type and stay date may be sold, regardless of channel or negotiated agreement. Enforced as a hard constraint in the Revenue Management System and CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; USALI

description: Current confirmed occupancy rate (OCC) percentage for this room type on the stay date at the time of last update, calculated as confirmed reservations divided by physical_room_count. Key input to dynamic pricing and sell limit decisions in the Revenue Management System.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI; STR STAR Report

description: Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date designated as out of order (OOO) due to maintenance, renovation, or FF&E replacement. These rooms are excluded from sellable inventory and occupancy calculations per USALI standards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; AHLA Property Improvement Plan (PIP) Standards

description: Hard ceiling on the absolute number of rooms that may be overbooked above physical capacity for this room type on the stay date, regardless of the overbooking_pct calculation. Prevents excessive walk exposure during high-demand periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Overbooking Policy Guidelines

description: Maximum authorized overbooking level expressed as a percentage above physical room capacity for this room type on the stay date. Defined in the overbooking policy and used by IDeaS G3 RMS to set the sell limit above physical_room_count.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Overbooking Policy Guidelines

description: Free-text or coded justification provided by the revenue manager when is_override is True. Documents the business rationale for deviating from the system recommendation (e.g., local event, competitive action, group displacement). Required for audit and RMS calibration.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; HTNG Data Dictionary

description: Total number of physical rooms of this room type at the property, representing the hard capacity ceiling before overbooking allowances are applied. Used as the denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculations.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; STR STAR Report

description: Identifies the originating system or process that set the current rate and availability state for this control record. Supports data lineage, audit, and reconciliation between IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Data Dictionary

description: Maximum number of rooms authorized to be sold for this room type on the stay date, inclusive of overbooking allowance. Set by the Revenue Management System and may exceed physical_room_count when overbooking is authorized. Enforced at the CRS and PMS booking engine.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The specific calendar date (night of stay) for which this inventory control record applies. Represents the primary dimension of the rate/availability grid alongside property, room type, and rate plan.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI; STR STAR Report

description: The value recommended by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) prior to any manual override. Enables comparison between algorithmic recommendation and the applied control_value for yield optimization analysis and RMS model performance tracking.

The date and time when this inventory control record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Type of compensation offered to a walked guest when the walk_policy requires compensation. Governs the financial and loyalty implications of overbooking execution and is tracked for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) and CPOR reporting.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Salesforce CRM; AHLA Guest Relocation Standards; USALI CPOR

description: Defines the operational procedure to follow when a guest with a confirmed reservation cannot be accommodated due to overbooking (a 'walk'). Determines whether the guest is walked to a partner hotel, upgraded, or compensated per AHLA guest relocation standards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Guest Relocation Standards; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each daily rate and availability snapshot record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the rate_availability data product.
reference: HTNG Data Dictionary Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Conventions

description: Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Rate availability snapshots distributed to OTAs and GDS must include the specific cancellation policy for rate shopping compliance and guest transparency. Channel managers need this link to ensure acc

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: Daily rate availability snapshots are directly driven by demand forecast levels. rate_availability.demand_forecast_level (STRING) is a denormalized categorical reference; adding demand_forecast_id as

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Rate/availability snapshots are channel-specific - each channel sees different rates and inventory based on connectivity and contracts. Core distribution management requires tracking what each channel

Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) applied by IDeaS G3 RMS to derive the current BAR for this snapshot. DRRs define the algorithmic pricing logic (demand-based adjustments, day-of-week factors, lead-time curves) that drive dynamic pricing decisions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Dynamic Rate Rules; Infor EzRMS Revenue Strategy

description: Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this rate and availability snapshot applies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Property Configuration; AHLA Property Standards

description: Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, package) governing the pricing for this snapshot. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS rate plan catalog.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Plan Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution Standards

description: Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which this rate and availability snapshot is captured. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Type Configuration; HTNG Room Type Standards

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Daily rate availability snapshots are generated within the context of the active revenue strategy. Adding strategy_id links each rate availability record to the governing strategy, enabling analysis o

Minimum number of days in advance a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on the snapshot date (e.g., 7-day advance purchase, 21-day advance purchase for NRR rates). A key rate restriction parameter managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Rate Plan Standards

description: Current open/closed availability status for this property, room type, rate plan, and date combination as managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS. 'open' = bookable; 'closed' = inventory blocked; 'stop_sell' = yield-driven closure; 'on_request' = requires manual confirmation; 'min_stay_restriction' = LOS restriction active; 'closed_to_arrival' = no new arrivals permitted on this date.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management; HTNG Availability Standards

description: Number of rooms of this room type available for sale on the snapshot date at this property. Represents net sellable inventory after deducting out-of-order, out-of-service, and already-committed rooms. Core input for OCC (Occupancy Rate) and RevPAR calculations.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; USALI Rooms Division Reporting; STR STAR Report

description: The Best Available Rate (BAR) for this room type, property, and stay date — the lowest publicly available non-restricted rate as determined by IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing engine. BAR is the anchor rate from which other rate plans are derived and is the primary rate distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS and OTA channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Pricing Engine; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Rate Transparency Guidelines; USALI Revenue Reporting

description: Indicates whether new arrivals are blocked on the snapshot date for this room type and rate plan. When True, guests cannot check in on this date but may continue an existing stay through it. A yield management restriction used to optimize multi-night booking patterns.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Restriction Management; HTNG Availability Standards

description: Indicates whether departures are blocked on the snapshot date for this room type and rate plan. When True, guests cannot check out on this date, forcing stays to extend through it. Used in conjunction with CTA restrictions to shape booking patterns during peak periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Restriction Management; HTNG Availability Standards

description: The date and time when this rate and availability snapshot record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and ETL pipeline tracking.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards; HTNG Data Governance; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all rate amounts (BAR, min, max, rack) for this record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency properties and international channel distribution via Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; Sabre SynXis CRS Multi-Currency Standards; IFRS/GAAP

description: Demand forecast classification for the snapshot date as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Drives dynamic pricing decisions and inventory restriction strategies. Levels range from very_low (deep discounting warranted) to very_high (maximum rate and stop-sell controls active).
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Demand Forecasting; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting; STR STAR Report Benchmarking

description: The date and time from which this rate and availability record became active and was distributed to booking channels. Used for bi-temporal modeling of rate history and supports point-in-time rate reconstruction for revenue audits and dispute resolution.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI Revenue Audit Standards; IFRS/GAAP

description: The date and time until which this rate and availability record remains active. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for bi-temporal modeling and historical rate reconstruction. When a new rate update is received, the prior record's effective_until_timestamp is set to the new record's effective_from_timestamp.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI Revenue Audit Standards; IFRS/GAAP

description: The rate code as published on GDS (Global Distribution System) platforms (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport) via Sabre SynXis CRS. GDS rate codes follow standardized formats required for travel agent booking systems and corporate travel management programs.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS GDS Distribution; GDS Rate Code Standards (Sabre/Amadeus/Travelport); HTNG Channel Distribution

description: Number of rooms on the snapshot date allocated to group blocks (MICE, corporate groups, tour operators) as managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus. Group block inventory is excluded from transient availability and tracked separately for yield optimization.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Group Block Management; Delphi by Amadeus Group Blocks; IDeaS G3 RMS Group Evaluation

description: Indicates whether this rate plan includes bundled package components (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, airport transfer) beyond the base room charge. Package rates require component-level revenue allocation across USALI departments in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Package Management; USALI Revenue Allocation Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: The date and time when this rate and availability record was last modified in the source system (IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS). Represents the business event timestamp of the most recent pricing or availability change. Critical for real-time channel distribution synchronization and SLA monitoring.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Real-Time Distribution Standards; SLA Monitoring

description: Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan for the snapshot date. Used to prevent long-stay bookings at promotional rates during high-demand periods. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS as an upper LOS (Length of Stay) restriction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS LOS Restrictions; Infor EzRMS Revenue Strategy

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to qualify for this rate plan on the snapshot date. A key yield optimization restriction managed in IDeaS G3 RMS. LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions are used to maximize revenue during high-demand periods by requiring multi-night commitments.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS LOS Restrictions; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: The ceiling rate (maximum sellable rate) for this room type, property, rate plan, and stay date as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS. Caps pricing during peak demand periods to maintain guest trust and brand integrity, and supports rate parity compliance across channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Ceiling Controls; Infor EzRMS Revenue Strategy; USALI Revenue Management

description: Code indicating the meal plan inclusion for this rate plan (RO = Room Only; BB = Bed and Breakfast; HB = Half Board; FB = Full Board; AI = All Inclusive). Affects rate composition, F&B revenue allocation, and package pricing in Oracle OPERA PMS and MICROS POS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Package Configuration; Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS; USALI F&B Revenue Reporting; IH&RA Meal Plan Standards

description: The floor rate (minimum sellable rate) for this room type, property, rate plan, and stay date as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the lowest price at which the room may be sold under any circumstance, enforcing revenue floor controls and preventing rate erosion.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Floor Controls; Infor EzRMS Revenue Strategy; USALI Revenue Management

description: Forecasted occupancy rate (OCC%) for the property on the snapshot date as projected by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this snapshot. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used to validate pricing decisions and benchmark against STR STAR Report actuals.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Forecasting; Infor EzRMS; USALI OCC Reporting; STR STAR Report

description: The rate identifier as assigned by the primary OTA (Online Travel Agency) partner (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) for this rate plan and property combination. Used for rate parity monitoring, OTA reconciliation, and channel performance reporting.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS OTA Channel Management; OTA Rate Parity Agreement Standards; AHLA Channel Distribution Guidelines

description: Number of rooms of this room type that are out of order (OOO) on the snapshot date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Out-of-order rooms are excluded from sellable inventory and impact OCC denominator calculations per USALI standards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Housekeeping; USALI Rooms Division Reporting; PIP (Property Improvement Plan) Standards

description: Number of rooms booked (picked up) for the snapshot date since the previous snapshot capture. Pickup velocity is a key revenue management signal used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS to detect demand acceleration and trigger dynamic rate adjustments.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Pickup Analysis; Infor EzRMS Demand Forecasting; USALI Revenue Management

description: Indicates whether the current rate for this snapshot was manually overridden by a revenue manager, superseding the automated recommendation from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Manual overrides are tracked for revenue strategy audit and RMS model calibration.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Override Tracking; Infor EzRMS Revenue Strategy; USALI Revenue Management Controls

description: Free-text or coded reason provided by the revenue manager when manually overriding the RMS-recommended rate. Populated only when pricing_override_flag is True. Used for revenue strategy review, RMS model improvement, and audit trail compliance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Override Audit; Infor EzRMS; SOX Financial Controls

description: The published full undiscounted rate (rack rate) for this room type and stay date. Serves as the reference rate for discount calculations, loyalty redemption valuations, and walk-in pricing. Typically the highest rate tier and the baseline for USALI revenue reporting.
reference: USALI Rooms Division Reporting; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Configuration; AHLA Rate Standards

description: Indicates whether this rate record is subject to rate parity obligations with OTA (Online Travel Agency) partners. When True, the rate must match or be lower than rates published on OTA channels. Critical for OTA contract compliance and channel distribution management.
reference: OTA Rate Parity Agreement Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Channel Distribution Guidelines

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS (e.g., BAR, CORP01, PKG_BB, NRR_ADV21). Used for channel distribution, GDS mapping, and OTA rate loading.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Distribution; IDeaS G3 RMS; GDS Rate Code Standards

description: Classification of the rate plan governing this availability record. Determines pricing logic, cancellation policy, and channel eligibility. BAR (Best Available Rate) = dynamic public rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) = guaranteed availability; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) = prepaid non-cancellable; corporate = negotiated account rate; package = bundled inclusions; group = block rate; loyalty = member rate; promotional = limited-time offer; wholesale = OTA/tour operator net rate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|lra|nrr|corporate|package|group|loyalty|promotional|wholesale — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Plan Classification; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Rate Standards

description: Identifies the Revenue Management System (RMS) that generated or last updated the pricing recommendation for this snapshot record. Supports data lineage tracking and system-of-record attribution for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Data Lineage Standards

description: The stay date (arrival night) for which this rate and availability record applies. Represents the business event date — the night a guest would occupy the room at the stated rate. Core dimension for RevPAR and ADR reporting.
reference: USALI Revenue Reporting Standards; STR STAR Report Date Conventions; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: The native record identifier from the originating source system (IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS) for this rate and availability record. Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation, reprocessing, and audit purposes.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Data Lineage Standards; ETL Reconciliation Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this room type and rate plan combination has been closed to all new bookings on the snapshot date regardless of available inventory. A hard yield management control applied in IDeaS G3 RMS to protect inventory for higher-value bookings or group blocks.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS Inventory Controls; Sabre SynXis CRS Channel Management

description: Total physical room count for this room type at the property on the snapshot date. Used as the denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations. Reflects the property's sellable capacity before any restrictions.
reference: USALI Rooms Division Reporting; STR STAR Report Capacity Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Primary key for competitive_set

Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Competitive sets are defined by brand tier comparability — revenue managers scope STR comp sets to matching brand tiers. Brand alignment is a required input for comp set approval and STR submission. A

Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Competitive sets are defined at market and geographic levels that align with hierarchy nodes (geographic_region, str_market_name). Linking competitive_set to hierarchy enables portfolio-level STR benc

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: competitive_set.market_segment (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the market segment focus of the competitive set. Adding market_segment_id normalizes this to the authoritative market_segment rec
Identifier of the subject property (the 'home' property) that owns this competitive set definition. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

The booking lead time window in days used for rate shopping comparisons within this competitive set (e.g., 0 = same-day, 7 = 7-day advance, 30 = 30-day advance). Ensures rate intelligence is captured at consistent booking horizons for fair ADR comparison.
reference: Infor EzRMS — Rate Intelligence Configuration; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: The date on which the competitive set definition was formally approved by the revenue management authority. Supports audit trail requirements for comp set governance and STR STAR Report submission integrity.
reference: Internal Revenue Management Governance Policy; SOX Financial Controls

description: Name or identifier of the revenue management leader or Director of Revenue who approved this competitive set definition. Required for governance and audit trail of comp set changes that affect STR STAR Report submissions.
reference: Internal Revenue Management Governance Policy; SOX Financial Controls

description: The Average Rate Index (ARI) target threshold for this competitive set. ARI = Property ADR / Comp Set ADR. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets a higher ADR than the competitive set average. Drives BAR pricing strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: STR STAR Report — ARI Methodology; USALI Revenue Performance Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for normalizing ADR, RevPAR, and TRevPAR benchmarking metrics across all properties in the competitive set (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Ensures consistent monetary comparison in STR STAR Report analysis.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Code Standard; STR STAR Report — Currency Normalization

description: Human-readable name assigned to the competitive set (e.g., 'Downtown Luxury Primary Set', 'Airport Corridor Secondary Set'). Used in Infor EzRMS and STR STAR Report submissions to identify the grouping.
reference: STR STAR Report — Competitive Set Naming Convention; Infor EzRMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the competitive set. 'active' sets are used in live RMS benchmarking and STR reporting; 'inactive' sets are suspended; 'pending_approval' awaits revenue management sign-off; 'archived' are historical sets retained for trend analysis.

The timestamp when this competitive set record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: The primary guest demand segment this competitive set is designed to benchmark. 'transient' focuses on FIT (Free Independent Traveler) and leisure; 'group' focuses on MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) and group blocks; 'contract' focuses on negotiated corporate rates; 'all_segments' covers the full mix.
reference: USALI — Demand Segmentation Standards; Infor EzRMS

description: The date from which this competitive set definition becomes active and is used for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, MPI, and ARI calculations. Aligns with STR reporting period start.
reference: STR STAR Report — Reporting Period Standards; Infor EzRMS

description: The date on which this competitive set definition expires and is no longer used for active benchmarking. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active set. Supports historical trend analysis when comp set membership changes.
reference: STR STAR Report — Reporting Period Standards; Infor EzRMS

description: The geographic market or submarket in which the competitive set operates (e.g., 'Manhattan Midtown', 'Chicago O'Hare Corridor', 'Miami Beach'). Aligns with STR market definitions used for Market Penetration Index (MPI) calculations.
reference: STR STAR Report — Market Definition Standards

business_glossary_term: IDeaS G3 Revenue Management System (RMS) Competitive Set Code
description: The competitive set reference code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS for pricing strategy alignment. Enables synchronization of comp set definitions between Infor EzRMS benchmarking and IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing rules (DRR) and BAR optimization.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Competitive Intelligence Configuration

description: Indicates whether this competitive set has been formally submitted to STR (Smith Travel Research) for STAR Report benchmarking. True means the set is registered with STR and receives STAR Report data; False means it is an internal-only comp set.

The most recent date on which the competitive set membership and parameters were reviewed by the revenue management team. Best practice requires annual or semi-annual review to ensure comp set relevance as market conditions evolve.
reference: Infor EzRMS — Comp Set Review Workflow; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: The standard Length of Stay (LOS) in nights used as the basis for rate comparison within this competitive set. Typically set to 1 night for transient benchmarking. Used to normalize ADR comparisons across properties with different LOS mix.
reference: STR STAR Report — LOS Normalization; Infor EzRMS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary geographic market for this competitive set (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Used for multi-national portfolio benchmarking and currency normalization in revenue performance reporting.

Maximum number of guestrooms a competitor property may have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Used alongside min_room_count to define the acceptable size range for comp set membership.
reference: STR STAR Report — Comp Set Eligibility Criteria

description: Total number of competitor properties currently included in this competitive set. STR STAR Report typically requires a minimum of 3 and maximum of 10 competitor properties for statistically valid benchmarking. Derived from the comp set membership roster but stored here for quick reference.
reference: STR STAR Report — Minimum Comp Set Size Requirements

description: Minimum number of guestrooms a competitor property must have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Ensures size comparability for RevPAR and occupancy benchmarking. Typically aligned with the subject property's room count range.
reference: STR STAR Report — Comp Set Eligibility Criteria

description: The Market Penetration Index (MPI) target threshold for this competitive set. MPI = Property Occupancy Rate / Comp Set Occupancy Rate. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets higher occupancy than the competitive set average. Used in revenue strategy planning.
reference: STR STAR Report — MPI Methodology; USALI Revenue Performance Standards

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this competitive set definition. Ensures comp set membership remains current and aligned with market dynamics. Triggers a review workflow in the Revenue Management System.
reference: Infor EzRMS — Comp Set Review Workflow; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Free-text field for revenue management team annotations regarding this competitive set, such as rationale for competitor inclusion/exclusion, market context, or pending changes. Not used in automated benchmarking calculations.

The primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) used to evaluate competitive performance for this comp set. Options include RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room), ADR (Average Daily Rate), Occupancy Rate (OCC), TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room), or GOPPAR (Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room).
reference: STR STAR Report — Benchmarking Metric Definitions; USALI

description: Filter defining the property service type composition of this competitive set. Ensures benchmarking is conducted against properties of comparable service level and amenity offering. Aligns with AHLA property classification standards.
reference: AHLA Hotel Classification Standards; STR Chain Scale Definitions

description: Frequency at which competitor rates are retrieved from OTA and GDS channels for properties in this competitive set. Drives the cadence of dynamic pricing adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS. Only applicable when rate_shopping_enabled is True.
reference: Infor EzRMS — Rate Shopping Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Indicates whether automated rate shopping (competitive rate intelligence) is active for this competitive set. When True, the RMS retrieves competitor BAR rates from OTA channels and GDS for dynamic pricing decisions. When False, only STR STAR Report data is used.
reference: Infor EzRMS — Rate Shopping Configuration; HTNG Rate Intelligence Standards

description: The Revenue Generation Index (RGI) target threshold set by the revenue management team for this competitive set. RGI = Property RevPAR / Comp Set RevPAR. A target above 1.00 indicates the property aims to outperform the comp set. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS for performance goal-setting.
reference: STR STAR Report — RGI Methodology; USALI Revenue Performance Standards

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Competitive Set ID
description: The native competitive set identifier as stored in the Revenue Management System (Infor EzRMS or IDeaS G3 RMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational RMS.
reference: Infor EzRMS — Competitive Benchmarking Module; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Classification of the competitive set indicating its strategic purpose. 'primary' is the main STR STAR Report comp set; 'secondary' is an alternate benchmarking group; 'custom' is an ad-hoc internal set; 'str_defined' is a set defined by STR directly; 'internal' is used for internal rate intelligence only.
reference: STR STAR Report — Competitive Set Classification; Infor EzRMS

description: The operational system of record from which this competitive set definition was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. 'infor_ezrms' = Infor EzRMS; 'ideass_g3' = IDeaS G3 RMS; 'manual' = manually entered; 'str_direct' = received directly from STR.
reference: Internal Data Governance — Data Lineage Standards; HTNG Data Exchange Standards

business_glossary_term: STR (Smith Travel Research) Competitive Set Code
description: The unique alphanumeric code assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) to identify this competitive set in the STAR Report system. Required for submitting and retrieving benchmarking data from STR. Maps directly to the STR comp set identifier in Infor EzRMS.
reference: STR STAR Report — Competitive Set Identifier Standard

description: The STR-assigned market code identifying the geographic market in which this competitive set is benchmarked. Used to retrieve market-level aggregates for MPI calculation and to align with STR STAR Report market definitions.
reference: STR STAR Report — Market Code Reference

description: Frequency at which STR STAR Report data is received and processed for this competitive set. Determines the cadence of RGI, MPI, and ARI metric refreshes in the Revenue Management System.
reference: STR STAR Report — Data Delivery Specifications

description: The STR-assigned submarket code for more granular geographic benchmarking within a broader STR market. Used when the competitive set operates in a distinct submarket (e.g., airport vs. downtown within the same city market).
reference: STR STAR Report — Submarket Code Reference

description: The date on which this competitive set was submitted to STR for STAR Report registration. Relevant only when is_str_submitted is True. Used for audit and compliance tracking of STR data agreements.
reference: STR STAR Report — Submission Audit Trail

description: The timestamp when this competitive set record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes in comp set definitions, track membership updates, and support incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the market segment record. Primary key for the market_segment data product in the revenue domain. Role classification: MASTER_RESOURCE — this entity represents a classification resource used across revenue, reservation, and channel domains.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); STR (Smith Travel Research) segment taxonomy

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Market segments map to typical loyalty tiers for segmentation alignment and forecasting. Business process: aligning revenue management segments with loyalty tier definitions for consistent guest class

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Accommodation Typical
description: Indicates whether guests in this market segment typically require ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible accommodations. Relevant for government, medical, and certain group segments. Informs room inventory allocation and accessibility compliance reporting.
reference: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act); AHLA accessibility standards

description: Average number of days between booking date and arrival date for this market segment. Key input for demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS. Segments with short lead times (e.g., OTA last-minute) require different yield strategies than long-lead corporate or group segments. Represents the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this resource.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting parameters; Infor EzRMS; STR

description: Average number of nights guests in this segment stay per booking (ALOS — Average Length of Stay). Used in revenue management for LOS (Length of Stay) restriction optimization, demand forecasting, and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report; USALI

description: Qualitative classification of the historical cancellation rate tendency for this market segment. Informs overbooking strategy, no-show reserve calculations, and revenue protection policies in IDeaS G3 RMS. High-propensity segments (e.g., OTA flexible rate) require higher overbooking buffers.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS overbooking configuration; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Primary distribution channel through which this market segment predominantly books. Drives channel strategy, OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission management, and GDS (Global Distribution System) rate loading decisions in Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|ota|gds|voice|group|wholesale|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration; HTNG (Hotel Technology Next Generation) / AHLA Technology; STR

description: Indicates whether bookings in this segment are subject to travel agent or OTA commission payments. Direct corporate and government segments are typically non-commissionable; OTA and GDS segments carry commission obligations. Drives accounts payable commission processing in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP module; USALI; PCI DSS

description: Standard commission rate percentage applicable to bookings in this segment when commission_eligible is true. Expressed as a percentage of room revenue (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis, OTA contract management, and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations. Confidential as it reflects contracted commercial terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; USALI; OTA partnership agreements

description: Classification of the segment's typical contribution margin profile relative to other segments. Reflects net revenue after channel commissions, OTA fees, GDS fees, and loyalty redemption costs. Premium tiers (e.g., direct corporate) yield higher GOP (Gross Operating Profit) than low-margin OTA or wholesale segments. Used in GOPPAR analysis.

Timestamp when this market segment record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record provenance tracking. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for MASTER_RESOURCE entities.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); USALI

description: The segment code as configured in Sabre SynXis CRS for central reservations and channel distribution. Used for rate plan distribution, availability controls, and channel-level segment reporting. Enables cross-system segment reconciliation.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG / AHLA Technology

description: Classification of the temporal demand pattern exhibited by this market segment. Corporate transient segments typically show weekday concentration; leisure segments show weekend and seasonal peaks; event-driven segments spike around MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) events. Used in Infor EzRMS demand forecasting.
reference: Infor EzRMS; IDeaS G3 RMS; STR STAR Report

description: Date from which this market segment definition becomes active and available for use in reservations, rate plans, and revenue reporting. Supports temporal validity management for segment taxonomy changes aligned with fiscal year or STR reporting period updates.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Date after which this market segment definition is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Supports segment lifecycle management and historical reporting continuity when segment taxonomies are revised.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; Oracle OPERA PMS

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Subject Applicable
description: Indicates whether guest data associated with bookings in this market segment is subject to GDPR data subject rights (right to erasure, portability, access). Relevant for segments involving EU/EEA guests. Drives data retention and anonymization policies in the data lakehouse.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 4, 17; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. Controls rate loading and availability display in Sabre SynXis CRS for GDS-connected travel agencies.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG / AHLA Technology; GDS distribution standards

description: Indicates whether this market segment supports group room block reservations. Group-eligible segments (e.g., MICE, sports groups, tour groups) require group block management in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus for BEO (Banquet Event Order) coordination.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS group module; Delphi by Amadeus; USALI

description: Numeric level of this segment within the market segment hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level categories (e.g., Transient, Group); Level 2 represents sub-categories (e.g., Corporate, Leisure); Level 3 represents granular segments (e.g., Corporate Negotiated, Leisure OTA). Used for hierarchical roll-up in revenue reporting.
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research); USALI; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Indicates whether this market segment is for internal reporting and analytics purposes only and should not be exposed to guests, OTA partners, or external distribution channels. Internal segments (e.g., complimentary, house use, employee rate) are excluded from external rate distribution in Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; USALI

description: Indicates whether bookings under this market segment are eligible to earn loyalty program points and benefits. Wholesale, crew, and certain contracted segments may be excluded from loyalty accrual per program rules. Drives loyalty point calculation logic in Salesforce CRM loyalty management.
reference: Salesforce CRM loyalty program configuration; AHLA

description: Detailed narrative description of the market segment, including its defining characteristics, typical guest profile, booking behavior, and applicable rate strategies. Used for training, documentation, and business glossary alignment across revenue management, reservations, and sales teams.

Current lifecycle status of the market segment record. Active segments are available for rate plan assignment, reservation coding, and revenue reporting. Inactive or deprecated segments are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new bookings. Aligns with MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.
reference: USALI; Oracle OPERA PMS segment configuration

description: Indicates whether revenue managers are permitted to override the minimum rate floor for this segment. Restricting overrides for high-value segments (e.g., LRA corporate) protects rate integrity. Configurable in IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls and Oracle OPERA PMS rate management.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Indicates whether bookings in this segment require a pre-negotiated rate agreement (e.g., corporate account rate, government per diem, airline crew contract). When true, a valid rate agreement must exist before reservations can be accepted at segment rates in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS rate agreement module; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: Qualitative classification of the historical no-show rate tendency for this market segment. Distinct from cancellation propensity — no-shows occur without prior cancellation and have different revenue recovery implications. Used in overbooking model calibration within IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Expedia, Booking.com, and Hotels.com. Controls channel availability in Sabre SynXis CRS and impacts OTA commission cost tracking.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG / AHLA Technology

description: Indicates whether payment card data associated with bookings in this segment falls within PCI DSS scope. Segments with direct card-present or card-not-present transactions require PCI-compliant data handling. Drives data masking and tokenization requirements in the lakehouse.
reference: PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard); USALI

description: The segment code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for reservation coding, folio routing, and rate plan assignment at the property level. Enables reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the operational PMS source system.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS segment configuration; HTNG / AHLA Technology

description: Classification of the segment's sensitivity to rate changes. Inelastic segments (e.g., airline crew, government contracted) book regardless of rate fluctuations. Highly elastic segments (e.g., leisure OTA) respond strongly to price changes. Drives DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) configuration in IDeaS G3 RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS pricing strategy; Infor EzRMS; STR

description: Classification of the rate plan type typically associated with this segment. BAR (Best Available Rate) for transient leisure, negotiated for corporate accounts, LRA (Last Room Availability) for top-tier corporate, NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) for advance purchase segments. Drives rate loading in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|negotiated|lra|nrr|wholesale|package|complimentary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS rate plan configuration; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: Primary revenue category associated with this market segment for TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) and USALI departmental reporting. Rooms-focused segments contribute primarily to rooms revenue; MICE segments contribute across rooms, F&B (Food and Beverage), and events revenue buckets.

The segment code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Used for demand forecasting, pricing optimization, and inventory control at the segment level. May differ from PMS segment code due to system-specific taxonomy requirements.

High-level classification grouping the segment into a primary demand category. Drives pricing strategy, channel allocation, and revenue reporting roll-ups. Aligns with STR segment category definitions used in STAR Report benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|contract|wholesale|ota|corporate|government|leisure|crew|airline — promote to reference product]
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research) STAR Report segment category definitions; USALI

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the market segment, used as the externally-known business identifier across Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS. Examples: CORP, TRANS, LEIS, OTA, GRP, GOVT, CREW. Aligns with STR segment code taxonomy for benchmarking and STAR Report mapping.
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research) STAR Report segment taxonomy; USALI

description: Full descriptive name of the market segment as displayed in reporting, dashboards, and revenue management systems. Examples: Corporate Negotiated, Leisure Transient, Online Travel Agency, Government/Military, Group Meetings. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this resource entity.
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research) STAR Report; USALI

description: Secondary classification distinguishing whether the segment represents individual (FIT — Free Independent Traveler) bookings, group blocks, negotiated corporate accounts, wholesale/tour operator contracts, or consortia arrangements. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS for demand segmentation and pricing strategy.
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research); IDeaS G3 RMS segment configuration; USALI

description: Numeric value controlling the display sequence of market segments in reports, dashboards, and system configuration screens. Ensures consistent ordering in Oracle OPERA PMS segment lists, IDeaS G3 RMS segment configuration, and BI reporting tools.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this market segment definition originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS segment taxonomies in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: HTNG / AHLA Technology; ETL data lineage standards

description: The official STR (Smith Travel Research) segment code used for competitive benchmarking and STAR Report alignment. Enables mapping of internal segment definitions to the industry-standard STR taxonomy for RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index) calculations.
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research) STAR Report; American Hotel and Lodging Association (AHLA)

description: Timestamp when this market segment record was last modified. Tracks configuration changes to segment definitions, status updates, and taxonomy revisions. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines feeding IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.

business_glossary_term: Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (USALI) Segment Code
description: The USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) standardized segment code used for financial reporting and departmental P&L allocation. Ensures consistency in GOP (Gross Operating Profit), NOI (Net Operating Income), and EBITDA reporting across properties and for external financial audits under GAAP/IFRS.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); IFRS/GAAP; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act)

description: Primary key for performance_actuals

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Actuals are compared against financial budgets for variance reporting, board presentations, and management performance evaluation against approved financial plans.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign ROI reporting requires linking revenue performance actuals directly to the driving marketing campaign — a core hotel commercial reporting process. Enables direct measurement of campaign-attri
