Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Loyalty promotion ROI reporting requires linking actual revenue performance to the promotion that drove it. Named process: promotion performance measurement and incremental revenue attribution. Revenu

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: RevPAR and room revenue actuals are tracked by cost center for departmental P&L, USALI reporting, and operational performance management.

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: performance_actuals.demand_forecast_rooms (INT) is a denormalized copy of the forecast rooms figure used for variance analysis. Adding demand_forecast_id links each daily actuals record to the specifi

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Daily actuals track which room nights and revenue came from specific event bookings for segment reporting. Required for event contribution analysis, MICE segment performance reporting, and group vs tr
Reference to the fiscal calendar period (month/quarter/year) to which this performance date belongs, enabling USALI-aligned financial period reporting and variance analysis against budget.

Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: USALI-compliant departmental P&L reporting requires performance actuals to be tied to a specific F&B revenue center (e.g., restaurant, bar, banquet). Hotels report F&B RevPASH and GOP at revenue cente

Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise fees (royalty, marketing, reservation fees) are calculated as percentages of revenue actuals per the franchise_agreement terms. Direct FK enables automated franchise fee computation and fran

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue performance reporting includes GSS scores as a KPI alongside financial metrics for balanced scorecards (SALT program requirement). Daily flash reports show RevPAR and GSS together—mandatory fo

Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Performance actuals must be reported through the organizational hierarchy for regional and portfolio-level P&L rollups. Capturing hierarchy_id at the time of the performance record preserves the hiera

Foreign key linking to finance.hma_contract. Business justification: HMA contracts include performance tests (RevPAR index, GOP thresholds) evaluated against actual results. Linking performance_actuals to hma_contract enables automated performance test calculations, ow

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Actual revenue performance posts to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation, revenue recognition, and external financial reporting compliance.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Performance reporting breaks down revenue contribution by member tier for loyalty program ROI analysis. Business process: tier-level revenue attribution reporting required for evaluating loyalty progr

Foreign key linking to property.ownership_entity. Business justification: Performance actuals are reported to ownership entities for investor reporting, management fee calculations, and REIT compliance. Linking performance_actuals to ownership_entity enables direct owner-le

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Performance actuals track channel_direct_rooms and channel_ota_rooms as metrics but lack a proper FK to the channel entity. Linking to channel.channel enables channel-level RevPAR and ADR performance

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Daily actuals track outlet-level F&B revenue for TRevPAR calculation and USALI reporting. Required for total property revenue reporting, GOP analysis, outlet performance attribution in consolidated fi

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Property performance actuals roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial reporting, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level performance analysis.

Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Performance actuals must attribute ancillary and total revenue to specific experience programs for program ROI reporting. experience.program has revenue_impact_flag indicating this attribution is expe
Reference to the property for which this daily revenue performance record is captured. Links to the property master entity in the Property domain.

Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Performance actuals are compared against seasonal plan targets (estimated_adr, estimated_occupancy_pct, rgi_target) from seasonal_calendar. Direct FK enables variance reporting between actual RevPAR a

Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Daily performance actuals must be evaluated against the revenue strategy that was in effect on that performance_date. Linking performance_actuals to strategy enables strategy effectiveness measurement

Average Daily Rate in the property's operating currency, calculated as total room revenue divided by rooms sold (excluding complimentary). Core KPI for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, ARI, and MPI calculations.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; AHLA; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Total ancillary revenue on the performance date from upsells, packages, resort fees, and add-on services not captured in departmental revenue lines. Supports total revenue and TRevPAR reporting.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; SAP S/4HANA; USALI

description: Average Rate Index representing the property's ADR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. ARI > 1.0 indicates the property is achieving a rate premium over its comp set.
reference: STR STAR Report; AHLA; Infor EzRMS

description: Budgeted Average Daily Rate for the performance date. Used for ADR variance analysis against actuals in the DOR, revenue management reporting, and STR STAR Report benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: Budgeted occupancy rate for the performance date as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75.00%). Used for occupancy variance analysis against actuals in the DOR and revenue management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI

description: Budgeted room revenue for the performance date as approved in the annual operating budget. Used for variance analysis against actual room revenue in the DOR and ownership reporting.
Budgeted total property revenue for the performance date as approved in the annual operating budget. Used for total revenue variance analysis in the DOR and ownership reporting.
Number of rooms sold through direct channels (brand website, call center, walk-in) on the performance date. Supports direct vs. indirect channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI

business_glossary_term: Online Travel Agency (OTA) Channel Rooms Sold
description: Number of rooms sold through Online Travel Agency (OTA) channels (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) on the performance date. Supports channel distribution mix analysis and OTA commission cost management.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI

description: Cost Per Occupied Room on the performance date, calculated as total rooms department operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Measures operational efficiency of the rooms department per USALI.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this performance record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency property portfolio reporting and consolidation.

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Contribution
description: Property-level EBITDA contribution on the performance date, representing operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Used for ownership reporting, asset valuation, and management contract performance measurement.

Total Food and Beverage revenue generated on the performance date across all F&B outlets (restaurants, bars, banquets, in-room dining). Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA. Included in TRevPAR calculation per USALI.
reference: Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA; USALI; ISO 22000

description: Gross Operating Profit on the performance date, calculated as total operated department revenue minus total operated department expenses per USALI. Key profitability metric for ownership reporting and management contracts.

business_glossary_term: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency. Core KPI for ownership reporting and management fee calculations.
reference: USALI; AHLA; SAP S/4HANA; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Indicates whether this daily performance actual record has been reconciled between Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA General Ledger. True = reconciled and approved for reporting; False = pending reconciliation. Supports financial close and SOX controls.
Total miscellaneous and rental income on the performance date including rentals, commissions, and other non-departmental income per USALI non-operating income classification.

Market Penetration Index representing the property's occupancy performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. MPI > 1.0 indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of market occupancy.
reference: STR STAR Report; AHLA; Infor EzRMS

description: Daily occupancy rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%), calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available. Core KPI for the STR STAR Report, DOR, and ownership reporting. Stored as a raw ratio; display layer applies percentage formatting.
Total parking revenue generated on the performance date from valet, self-park, and garage operations. Classified under other operated departments per USALI and included in TRevPAR.

The calendar date (business date) for which this revenue performance actual record applies. Represents the stay date or operating day used in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and all KPI calculations.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; STR STAR Report

business_glossary_term: Prior Year Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR)
description: RevPAR for the same calendar date in the prior year. Used for year-over-year (YOY) variance analysis in the DOR, STR STAR Report benchmarking, and ownership reporting.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS

description: Lifecycle status of this performance actual record indicating whether figures are preliminary (intra-day), final (end-of-day close), adjusted (post-close correction), or closed (period-locked). Supports audit and reconciliation workflows.
reference: USALI; SAP S/4HANA Financial Close; SOX

description: Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as ADR multiplied by occupancy rate (or total room revenue divided by rooms available). Primary rooms revenue KPI for ownership reporting, STR STAR Report, and RGI benchmarking.
reference: USALI; STR STAR Report; AHLA; IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS

description: Revenue Generation Index representing the property's RevPAR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. RGI > 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its comp set. Core competitive benchmarking KPI.
reference: STR STAR Report; AHLA; Infor EzRMS

description: Total number of rooms available for sale on the performance date after deducting out-of-order (OOO) and out-of-service (OOS) rooms. Denominator for Occupancy Rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations per USALI and STR STAR Report standards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; STR STAR Report

description: Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary (no-charge) basis on the performance date. Tracked separately per USALI for accurate ADR and revenue reporting, as complimentary rooms are excluded from ADR calculation.
Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the performance date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Excluded from rooms available per USALI inventory standards.
Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the performance date, including complimentary rooms. Used to calculate Occupancy Rate (OCC) and Average Daily Rate (ADR) per USALI standards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; STR STAR Report

description: Number of rooms sold under negotiated contract rates (airline crew, long-stay corporate contracts) on the performance date. Supports contract segment revenue analysis and channel mix reporting.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Number of rooms sold to group accounts (MICE, corporate groups, tour groups) on the performance date. Supports group vs. transient mix analysis and group revenue contribution reporting per USALI.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Delphi by Amadeus; USALI; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Number of rooms sold to transient (individual/FIT) guests on the performance date. Supports segment-level revenue analysis and channel mix reporting per USALI market segmentation standards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this performance actual record was sourced or reconciled (e.g., Oracle OPERA PMS for rooms data, SAP S/4HANA for financial totals). Supports data lineage and reconciliation audits.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; SAP S/4HANA; IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS

description: Total spa and wellness revenue generated on the performance date. Included in total property revenue and TRevPAR calculation per USALI other operated departments classification.

Total gross revenue across all operated departments and income streams on the performance date (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous + ancillary). Denominator basis for TRevPAR. Reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL per USALI.

Total gross room revenue generated on the performance date across all rate plans and channels, before deductions. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL. Numerator for ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; SAP S/4HANA; USALI; IFRS/GAAP

description: Total Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as total property revenue divided by rooms available. Measures total revenue productivity of the property beyond rooms. Core KPI for ownership and management reporting.
reference: USALI; AHLA; STR STAR Report; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Timestamp when this performance actual record was last modified, including post-close adjustments or reconciliation corrections. Supports audit trail and change tracking.
Unique surrogate identifier for a Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) record in the revenue management system. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules are often triggered by marketing campaigns (e.g., increase rates during high-awareness periods). Revenue managers need campaign context to evaluate rule performance and campaign-

Identifier of the competitive set (comp set) used as the benchmark for competitive-based Dynamic Rate Rules. References the STR STAR Report comp set configuration in Infor EzRMS for RGI, MPI, and ARI-driven pricing decisions.
reference: Infor EzRMS; STR (Smith Travel Research) STAR Report

description: Identifier of the demand forecast record from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS that this forecast-based Dynamic Rate Rule references for its trigger evaluation.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific channels differently (e.g., OTA rates adjust based on commission costs, direct channel gets best rate). RMS systems apply channel-specific rate optimization strat

Foreign key linking to guest.segment. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific guest segments for personalized rate adjustments. Revenue management systems apply segment-specific pricing logic (e.g., loyalty tier discounts, corporate negotia

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Dynamic pricing adjustments must post to specific GL accounts for audit trail, revenue variance tracking, and SOX compliance in automated pricing systems.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules adjust rates based on member tier for personalized revenue optimization. Business process: applying tier-specific rate adjustments in revenue management systems to reward high-va

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules can be scoped to specific market segments. dynamic_rate_rule has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N DRR records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant m

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules monitor outlet occupancy for total revenue optimization (restaurant full = adjust room+dining packages). Required for integrated revenue management systems that optimize property
Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Identifier of the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all eligible rate plans for the property.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe suite) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all room types at the property.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules implement the automated pricing adjustments defined within the revenue strategy. Adding strategy_id links each DRR to the governing strategy, enabling revenue managers to audit whic

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules are triggered by or aligned with loyalty promotions (e.g., rate floor adjustments during a tier-challenge promotion). Named process: promotion-triggered dynamic pricing rule configu

Indicates whether the Dynamic Rate Rule increases or decreases the rate when triggered. Provides explicit directional intent separate from the sign of adjustment_value to prevent ambiguity in RMS configuration.

Method by which the Dynamic Rate Rule adjusts the published rate when triggered. Percentage applies a relative increase or decrease to the current rate; absolute_amount adds or subtracts a fixed currency value; bar_offset moves the rate a fixed amount above or below the Best Available Rate (BAR); bar_multiplier applies a multiplier to the BAR.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The magnitude of the rate adjustment applied when the Dynamic Rate Rule is triggered. Interpreted according to adjustment_type: a value of 0.10 with type 'percentage' means a 10% rate increase; a value of 25.00 with type 'absolute_amount' means a $25 rate increase; a value of -15.00 with type 'bar_offset' means $15 below BAR.
Maximum number of days in advance of the arrival date within which a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Null indicates no upper advance booking restriction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Minimum number of days in advance of the arrival date that a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Supports advance purchase pricing strategies.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Workflow approval state of the Dynamic Rate Rule indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by a revenue manager before activation. Supports SOX-compliant pricing governance controls.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this Dynamic Rate Rule for activation. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail on pricing decisions.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was approved for activation by the authorized revenue manager. Part of the pricing governance audit trail required under SOX financial controls.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for pricing configuration governance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the property in which rate floor, ceiling, and absolute adjustment values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Seven-character binary string (positions 1–7 = Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this Dynamic Rate Rule is eligible to fire. A value of '1111100' means Monday through Friday only. Enables day-of-week yield optimization strategies.
The calendar date from which this Dynamic Rate Rule becomes active and eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments in the RMS. Aligns with the stay date range for yield optimization.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: The calendar date on which this Dynamic Rate Rule expires and ceases to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Indicates whether this Dynamic Rate Rule can be combined (stacked) with other active rules for the same stay date, room type, and rate plan. When False, this rule takes exclusive precedence based on rule_priority and no other rules are applied concurrently.

Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was most recently evaluated as true and triggered an automated pricing adjustment in the RMS. Used by revenue managers to monitor rule activity and effectiveness.
Maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights beyond which this Dynamic Rate Rule does not apply. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights that must be satisfied for this Dynamic Rate Rule to apply. Used in LOS-based yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The highest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from exceeding the property's maximum acceptable rate cap. Sensitive pricing strategy data.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI; AHLA

description: The lowest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from falling below the property's minimum acceptable ADR threshold. Sensitive pricing strategy data.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI; AHLA

description: Number of days in the booking pickup window used to evaluate pickup velocity for pickup-based Dynamic Rate Rules. For example, a value of 7 means the rule evaluates rooms booked in the last 7 days.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Rule Reference ID
description: Native rule identifier assigned by the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for this Dynamic Rate Rule. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RMS.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the Dynamic Rate Rule within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference between IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; HTNG (AHLA Technology)

description: Detailed narrative description of the Dynamic Rate Rule's business purpose, pricing logic, and intended yield optimization outcome.
Human-readable name describing the Dynamic Rate Rule, used by revenue managers to identify and manage pricing strategies within the RMS dashboard.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the order of evaluation when multiple Dynamic Rate Rules are eligible to fire simultaneously for the same property, room type, and rate plan. Lower numeric values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).

Current lifecycle state of the Dynamic Rate Rule governing whether it is eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Active rules are evaluated by the RMS engine; draft rules are under configuration; suspended rules are temporarily disabled; archived rules are retained for historical audit.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Classification of the Dynamic Rate Rule by the trigger mechanism driving automated pricing adjustments. Occupancy-based rules fire on OCC thresholds; pickup-based on booking velocity; competitive-based on RGI/ARI/MPI benchmarks from STR STAR Report; event-based on MICE or local demand events; LOS-based on Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions; forecast-based on RMS demand forecasts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: occupancy_based|pickup_based|competitive_based|event_based|los_based|forecast_based — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR (Smith Travel Research) STAR Report

description: Operational system of record from which this Dynamic Rate Rule was originated or last synchronized. Supports data lineage tracking across the revenue management technology stack.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG (AHLA Technology)

description: Cumulative number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule has been triggered and applied a pricing adjustment since its activation date. Used to assess rule utilization and yield optimization effectiveness.

Comparison operator applied to the trigger threshold value to determine whether the rule condition is met. For example, 'greater_than' with a threshold of 0.80 means the rule fires when OCC exceeds 80%.

Unit of measure for the trigger threshold value. Percentage applies to OCC-based rules; room_count applies to pickup-based rules; index_value applies to competitive benchmarking rules (RGI, MPI, ARI); currency_amount applies to ADR-based triggers; nights applies to LOS-based rules.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR (Smith Travel Research)

description: The numeric threshold at which this Dynamic Rate Rule fires. For occupancy-based rules, this is the OCC percentage (e.g., 0.80 = 80%). For pickup-based rules, this is the number of rooms picked up within the pickup window. For competitive-based rules, this is the RGI or ARI index value.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR (Smith Travel Research)

description: Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer ETL pipeline.
Incremental version counter tracking the number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule configuration has been modified. Supports audit trail and change management for pricing strategy governance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); USALI

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the negotiated rate agreement record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: HTNG Data Dictionary; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Management

description: Foreign key linking to event.event_account. Business justification: Negotiated rates are contracted with specific event accounts (corporate, consortia, national accounts). Essential for contract rate management, event planner rate loading, and account-specific pricing

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates are sometimes created as part of B2B marketing campaigns. Tracking the originating campaign supports contract performance analysis and measures campaign success in driving c

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Negotiated rates are sometimes tied to specific campaign offers (e.g., consortia rates bundled with a promotional offer). Rate loading process requires referencing the campaign offer for offer code va

Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Contract management and rate loading: revenue managers must link negotiated rates to the guest corporate account to validate credit limits, billing instructions, and contracted room-night commitments

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are loaded to specific channels (GDS with specific chain codes, OTA corporate programs, direct booking engines). Rate loading and distribution tracking requires knowing whic

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Bespoke negotiated rates are frequently created for a single large event booking (e.g., a corporate conference). Linking the negotiated rate to the specific event booking enables rate loading validati

Foreign key linking to event.event_contract. Business justification: Negotiated rates are formalized and referenced in event contracts. Linking the negotiated rate record to the event contract enables contract compliance auditing, rate loading verification against cont

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Corporate and consortia negotiated rates are loaded and audited by fiscal year for rate parity compliance, commission reconciliation, and financial reporting. Finance teams require fiscal period taggi

Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Negotiated rates at franchised properties must comply with franchise agreement brand standards, minimum rate requirements, and loyalty fee obligations. Linking revenue_negotiated_rate to franchise_agr

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates include outlet benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, lounge access). Required for contract fulfillment, benefit tracking, outlet revenue attribution, guest entitlement ver

Foreign key linking to fnb.menu. Business justification: Corporate and consortia negotiated rate contracts that include F&B (e.g., breakfast-inclusive corporate rates) specify the exact menu included. This is a contractual element — rate loading in the CRS/

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates post to specific GL accounts for contract revenue tracking, corporate account profitability analysis, and revenue recognition compliance.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Negotiated rates are classified by market segment (e.g., Corporate, Wholesale). revenue_negotiated_rate has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N negotiated_rate records → 1

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates often include tier-based eligibility or enhanced benefits. Business process: enforcing tier requirements for corporate rate access, common in negotiated contracts that offer

Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates frequently bundle specific experience programs (e.g., executive lounge access, welcome amenity). Linking negotiated rates to experience programs enables contract compliance

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Negotiated corporate rates are sometimes bundled with loyalty promotions (e.g., bonus points for corporate travelers on negotiated rates). Named process: promotion-enhanced negotiated rate configurati
Reference to the property for which this negotiated rate agreement applies. A single negotiated rate may be scoped to one property or replicated per property for multi-property contracts.
