description: Reference to the parent rate plan definition under which this negotiated rate is structured. Links to the rate plan master in Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Plan; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Configuration

description: Reference to the specific room type to which this negotiated rate applies. Null if the rate applies to all room types under the agreement.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Room Type Configuration; HTNG Room Type Standards

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Negotiated rate agreements are established within the context of the revenue strategy (e.g., LRA rates, corporate rates aligned to strategy targets). Adding strategy_id links each negotiated rate to t

Foreign key linking to reservation.travel_agent. Business justification: Negotiated rates are contracted with specific travel agents. Linking revenue_negotiated_rate to travel_agent enables rate loading verification, commission reconciliation, and contract management. reve
Minimum number of days in advance a reservation must be made to qualify for this negotiated rate. Zero indicates same-day booking is permitted.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Advance Purchase Rules; Sabre SynXis CRS Booking Window

description: The contracted nightly room rate (Average Daily Rate) agreed upon for this account. Represents the fixed or base rate per room per night in the specified currency. Core pricing field for revenue management and contract compliance.
reference: USALI ADR Definition; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Amount; STR STAR Report ADR Benchmarking

description: Approval workflow status for this negotiated rate agreement. Rates must be approved by revenue management leadership before loading into CRS/GDS channels.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Approval Workflow; IDeaS G3 RMS Approval Process

description: Name or employee identifier of the revenue management leader or director who approved this negotiated rate agreement for distribution.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Audit Trail

description: Date and time when this negotiated rate agreement received final approval for loading and distribution.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS Audit Trail

description: Comma-separated list or date range expressions representing dates on which this negotiated rate is not available (e.g., peak demand periods, holidays, special events). Stored as a structured string; detailed blackout records are managed in the rate_restriction product.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Blackout Date Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Availability; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Indicates whether breakfast is included in the negotiated rate. Affects F&B (Food and Beverage) revenue allocation and rate value proposition reporting.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Package Configuration; USALI F&B Revenue Allocation

description: Commission percentage payable to the TMC, travel agency, or consortia for reservations booked under this negotiated rate. Expressed as a percentage of the negotiated rate amount.
reference: IATA Commission Standards; AHLA Agency Commission Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA AP Commission Processing

description: Total number of room nights the account has committed to book under this negotiated rate agreement for the contract period. Basis for volume discount qualification and contract performance tracking.
reference: AHLA Corporate Account Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Production Tracking

description: Identifier for the travel consortia (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature, Ensemble, Internova) under which this negotiated rate is offered. Null for direct corporate accounts not affiliated with a consortia.
reference: AHLA Consortia Rate Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS Consortia Configuration

description: The date on which the negotiated rate agreement expires and the rate is no longer available for booking. Null for open-ended agreements. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
reference: USALI Contract Management; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Expiry; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: External contract or agreement reference number assigned by the account, TMC, or legal department. Used for contract management, audit, and dispute resolution.
reference: SOX Contract Management Controls; SAP S/4HANA Contract Management; AHLA Contract Standards

description: The date from which the negotiated rate agreement becomes contractually binding and the rate is available for booking. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
reference: USALI Contract Management; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Effective Date; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Date and time when this negotiated rate record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data lifecycle management.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; SOX Audit Trail Requirements; CCPA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the negotiated rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Multi-Currency Reporting

description: Two-letter GDS chain code used to identify the hotel brand/chain in Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, and Worldspan for rate loading and distribution of this negotiated rate.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS GDS Configuration; HTNG GDS Standards; IATA GDS Chain Code Registry

description: IATA-assigned numeric identifier for the Travel Management Company (TMC) or travel agency associated with this negotiated rate. Used for commission tracking and GDS rate access validation.
reference: IATA Agency Identification Standards; Sabre SynXis CRS TMC Configuration

description: Indicates whether this negotiated rate carries a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the property to honor the contracted rate as long as any room of the applicable type remains available, regardless of demand or yield management controls.
reference: AHLA LRA Contract Standards; IDeaS G3 RMS LRA Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS LRA Flag

description: Indicates whether reservations booked under this negotiated rate are non-refundable (NRR). Affects cancellation policy enforcement and revenue recognition timing.
reference: AHLA Cancellation Policy Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Cancellation Rules; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Maximum number of consecutive nights allowed per reservation under this negotiated rate. Null if no upper limit is imposed.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS LOS Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS Stay Controls

description: Minimum number of consecutive nights required per reservation to qualify for this negotiated rate. Enforced at booking in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS LOS Restrictions; Sabre SynXis CRS Stay Controls; HTNG Rate Restriction Standards

description: Minimum number of room nights the account is contractually committed to consume during the contract period. Used for volume tracking and contract compliance reporting.
reference: AHLA Corporate Account Contract Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Volume Commitment

description: Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or sales manager responsible for negotiating and managing this rate agreement. Used for accountability and performance tracking.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Owner; Oracle HCM Cloud Employee Reference

description: The percentage discount or premium of this negotiated rate relative to the Best Available Rate (BAR) at time of contract negotiation. Used by revenue management to assess rate competitiveness and yield impact.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS BAR Analysis; STR STAR Report ARI Benchmarking; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Alphanumeric rate code used to identify and load this negotiated rate in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. This is the externally-known business identifier referenced in GDS/CRS rate loading and reservation transactions.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Code Standards; HTNG Rate Distribution

description: Indicates whether the negotiated rate amount is inclusive of applicable taxes. Critical for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and guest billing accuracy.
reference: USALI Tax Accounting Standards; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition; Oracle OPERA PMS Tax Configuration

description: Date and time when the negotiated rate was successfully loaded into Sabre SynXis CRS and/or Oracle OPERA PMS for distribution. Null if loading has not yet occurred.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Audit Log; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Loading Audit

description: Current status of the rate loading process in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. Indicates whether the negotiated rate has been successfully distributed to all applicable booking channels.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Loading Workflow; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Distribution

description: Human-readable name describing the negotiated rate agreement, typically referencing the account or program name (e.g., 'Acme Corp 2025 Corporate Rate'). Used in reporting and rate management dashboards.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Management; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Current lifecycle status of the negotiated rate agreement. Controls whether the rate is bookable and distributed through CRS/GDS channels.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS Rate Status; Sabre SynXis CRS Distribution Status

description: Classification of the pricing structure for this negotiated rate. Fixed rates are static for the contract period; dynamic rates may fluctuate within agreed bounds; tiered rates vary by volume; per diem rates are government-mandated ceiling rates.
reference: AHLA Rate Classification; IDeaS G3 RMS Rate Type Configuration; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Operational system of record from which this negotiated rate record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Salesforce CRM.
reference: HTNG Data Integration Standards; SOX Data Lineage Requirements

description: Native identifier of this negotiated rate record in the originating operational system (Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Salesforce CRM). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.
reference: HTNG Data Integration Standards; Oracle OPERA PMS Rate ID; Sabre SynXis CRS Rate Reference

description: Date and time when this negotiated rate record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental ETL processing.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; SOX Audit Trail Requirements; CCPA

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each manual pricing override record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the pricing_override data product.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Pricing overrides are sometimes tier-specific (e.g., a special rate floor applicable only to Platinum members). Named process: tier-specific pricing override authorization and audit. Revenue managers

business_glossary_term: Related Banquet Event Order Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: Manual rate overrides for groups tied to F&B minimum spend commitments from banquet events. Required for group contract evaluation, displacement analysis that includes total property spend, not just r

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Pricing overrides are granted for specific high-value loyalty members (VIP rate requests). Revenue managers need to audit which member received a below-floor override. Named process: member-specific p

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Manual rate overrides are sometimes applied to honor campaign promises or fix campaign-related pricing errors. Tracking the campaign context is essential for audit, reconciliation, and campaign cost a

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Manual rate overrides often apply to specific channels (e.g., override OTA rate due to parity issue, adjust GDS rate for corporate segment). Revenue managers need to track which channel triggered over

Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) that was in effect and being superseded by this manual override. Enables analysis of how frequently manual overrides conflict with automated DRR logic and supports RMS rule tuning.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Dynamic Rate Rules (DRR); Infor EzRMS

description: Reference to a specific event, conference, or MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) event that triggered the pricing override when override_reason_code is 'event_demand'. Links to the event master record in Delphi by Amadeus.
reference: Delphi by Amadeus — Event Sales; MICE industry standards

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Manual rate overrides require GL posting for revenue variance tracking, management override reporting, and SOX control documentation in financial audits.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Manual pricing overrides are often segment-specific (e.g., overriding the corporate rate for a specific segment during a high-demand period). Adding market_segment_id links each override to the market
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which the pricing override was applied. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Reference to the group room block associated with this pricing override when override_reason_code is 'group_wash'. Links to the group block record in Oracle OPERA PMS or Delphi by Amadeus for group displacement analysis.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Group Block Management; Delphi by Amadeus

description: Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, group) against which the pricing override is applied. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS rate plan catalog.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Plan Management; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Suite, Standard Double) to which the pricing override applies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS — Room Type Configuration

description: Foreign key linking to experience.service_recovery_action. Business justification: Pricing overrides issued as service recovery compensation must reference the originating service_recovery_action for audit trail and finance reconciliation. Revenue managers and guest experience teams

Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Pricing overrides represent deviations from system-generated rates. Linking each override to the active revenue strategy enables measurement of override frequency and magnitude relative to strategy ta

Indicates whether this pricing override required formal approval before activation, based on the rate variance threshold or property-level governance policy. True = approval workflow triggered; False = auto-approved within tolerance.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Governance; SOX — internal controls

description: The rate variance percentage threshold (as a decimal, e.g., 0.2000 = 20%) that triggered the approval requirement for this override. Captures the governance rule in effect at the time of submission for audit purposes.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Governance Policy; SOX — internal controls

description: The date and time when the pricing override was formally approved by the authorizing revenue manager or director. Null if override is still pending or was reversed without approval.

Specifies which distribution channels the overridden rate is published to. 'all_channels' = no restriction; 'direct_only' = hotel direct and brand website only; 'ota_excluded' = all channels except Online Travel Agencies (OTA); 'gds_only' = Global Distribution System (GDS) only; 'brand_website_only' = brand.com only.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS — Channel Management; AHLA — Channel Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether the overridden rate includes a Closed to Arrival (CTA) restriction, preventing new reservations with an arrival date falling within the override effective window. True = CTA restriction active; False = arrivals permitted.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Restrictions

description: Indicates whether the overridden rate includes a Closed to Departure (CTD) restriction, preventing check-outs on specific dates within the override window. True = CTD restriction active; False = departures permitted.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Restrictions

description: The competitor or market reference rate (in property local currency) that informed the override decision, particularly when override_reason_code is 'competitive_pressure'. Sourced from STR STAR Report, OTA rate shopping, or RMS competitive benchmarking module.
reference: STR (Smith Travel Research) — STAR Report; Infor EzRMS — Competitive Benchmarking; ARI (Average Rate Index)

description: System-generated timestamp recording when the pricing override record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); GDPR Article 5 — data integrity and accountability

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rates captured in this override record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures multi-currency properties report rates in the correct denomination.

Seven-character binary string (1=active, 0=inactive) representing the days of the week the override applies to, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., '1111100' = Mon–Fri only). Applicable when override_scope is 'day_of_week'.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Restrictions; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Calendar

description: The first calendar date on which the pricing override rate becomes active and is applied to reservations. Defines the start of the override window in the property's local time zone.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Effective Dates; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Calendar

description: The last calendar date on which the pricing override rate remains active. After this date, the override status transitions to 'expired' and the RMS-recommended rate is reinstated. Nullable for open-ended overrides.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Effective Dates; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Calendar

description: Number of room inventory units to which the pricing override applies. Null or zero indicates the override applies to all available inventory for the specified room type and date range.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Inventory Controls; Oracle OPERA PMS — Room Inventory

description: Indicates whether this override specifically targets the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate for a given date. True = BAR is being overridden; False = override applies to a non-BAR rate plan.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — BAR Management; Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution; AHLA

description: Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under the overridden rate. Null if no maximum LOS restriction is applied. Used in conjunction with los_restriction_min to define the eligible stay window.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — LOS Restrictions; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Restrictions

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to qualify for the overridden rate. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction applied alongside the rate override to manage demand patterns and prevent single-night bookings during high-demand periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — LOS Restrictions; Oracle OPERA PMS — Rate Restrictions

description: Free-text narrative provided by the revenue manager explaining the business rationale for the override, particularly when override_reason_code is 'other' or when additional context is required for audit purposes.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — audit trail requirements; USALI

description: The manually set rate (in property local currency) applied by the revenue manager, superseding the RMS-recommended rate. This is the Best Available Rate (BAR) or specific rate plan rate that will be published to distribution channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Manual Override; Sabre SynXis CRS — Rate Distribution; AHLA

description: Standardized reason code classifying why the revenue manager overrode the system-generated rate. 'competitive_pressure' = OTA/competitor rate response; 'group_wash' = group block displacement adjustment; 'event_demand' = MICE or local event surge; 'error_correction' = RMS data or configuration error fix; 'distressed_inventory' = last-minute unsold inventory; 'other' = non-standard reason documented in override_notes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: competitive_pressure|group_wash|event_demand|error_correction|distressed_inventory|other — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Reason Taxonomy; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to the pricing override for audit trail, cross-system reconciliation, and revenue manager communication. Format: PO- followed by 6–20 alphanumeric characters.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry); SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — financial controls

description: Defines the temporal scope of the pricing override. 'single_date' = applies to one specific stay date; 'date_range' = applies across a contiguous range from effective_date to expiry_date; 'day_of_week' = applies to specific days of the week within the effective window.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Rate Override Configuration; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Current lifecycle state of the pricing override. 'pending' = submitted awaiting approval; 'approved' = authorized and scheduled; 'active' = currently in effect; 'expired' = past effective end date; 'reversed' = manually cancelled or rolled back.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Workflow; USALI

description: Absolute monetary difference between the override_rate and the rms_recommended_rate (override_rate minus rms_recommended_rate). Positive value indicates override is above RMS recommendation; negative indicates below. Used for override impact analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Analysis; USALI

description: Percentage deviation of the override_rate from the rms_recommended_rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Calculated as (override_rate - rms_recommended_rate) / rms_recommended_rate. Supports threshold-based alerting and RMS calibration.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Analysis; STR STAR Report benchmarking

description: Free-text or coded explanation for why the pricing override was reversed. Populated only when override_status is 'reversed'. Supports post-mortem analysis of override quality and revenue impact of reversals.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Reversal; SOX — audit trail; USALI

description: The date and time when the pricing override was reversed or cancelled, restoring the RMS-recommended rate. Null for overrides that have not been reversed.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Override Reversal; SOX audit trail

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Forecast Demand Level
description: The demand level classification (low, medium, high, very_high) as forecasted by the RMS at the time the override was submitted. Provides context for evaluating whether the override was aligned with or contrary to demand signals.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Demand Forecasting; Infor EzRMS — Revenue Strategy

description: The original rate (in property local currency) recommended by the automated Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) prior to the manual override. Baseline for override variance analysis and RMS tuning.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS — Pricing Engine; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: Identifies the Revenue Management System (RMS) that generated the original recommended rate being overridden. 'ideas_g3' = IDeaS G3 RMS; 'infor_ezrms' = Infor EzRMS; 'manual' = no RMS recommendation existed (fully manual rate entry).
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS — Demand Forecasting

description: The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the revenue manager submitted the pricing override request for approval. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.

System-generated timestamp recording the most recent modification to the pricing override record in the data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for each rate restriction record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the rate_restriction data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Revenue managers apply cancellation policy restrictions as part of yield management strategy. Rate restrictions must enforce specific cancellation terms (non-refundable, free cancellation windows) to

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Restrictions (stop-sell, min LOS, CTA/CTD) are applied per distribution channel. Revenue managers close channels selectively based on demand - essential inventory control operation. Enables channel-sp

Foreign key linking to revenue.dynamic_rate_rule. Business justification: rate_restriction.rms_recommendation_flag (BOOLEAN) indicates that a restriction was recommended by the RMS (IDeaS G3 or Infor EzRMS). Dynamic rate rules in the RMS trigger rate restrictions (e.g., min

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Revenue managers apply specific rate restrictions (min LOS, CTA/CTD, hurdle rates) when large event bookings create displacement risk. Essential for RMS restriction management around group blocks and

Foreign key linking to event.event_group_block. Business justification: Rate restrictions (minimum LOS, closed-to-arrival, stop-sell) are frequently applied in direct response to active group blocks to protect transient revenue. Linking rate_restriction to event_group_blo

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Rate restrictions include an is_loyalty_exempt flag; revenue managers must reference the specific loyalty promotion that triggers the exemption. Named process: restriction exemption management for loy

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Rate restrictions can be tier-exempt (e.g., Platinum members bypass minimum LOS restrictions). The is_loyalty_exempt flag exists but no tier FK identifies which tier is exempt. Named process: tier-bas

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Rate restrictions can be scoped to market segments (e.g., restrict Leisure segment on peak dates). rate_restriction has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N rate_restrictio
Reference to the property (hotel/resort) to which this rate restriction applies. Enables property-level yield optimization and restriction management across the portfolio.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Reference to the rate plan to which this restriction applies. Links to the rate plan master in the CRS/RMS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, DRR). A restriction may apply to one or multiple rate plans; this field captures the primary rate plan association.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Reference to the specific room type to which this restriction applies. When null, the restriction applies to all room types under the associated rate plan. Enables granular inventory control by room category.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Rate restrictions (CTA, CTD, min LOS) are applied based on seasonal demand periods. rate_restriction.season_code is a denormalized reference to seasonal_calendar. A direct FK enables restriction audit

Foreign key linking to revenue.strategy. Business justification: Rate restrictions are operational implementations of the revenue strategy (e.g., minimum LOS restrictions during high-demand periods as defined in the strategy). Adding strategy_id links each restrict

Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Supports NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase yield strategies. Null when restriction type is not ADVANCE_PURCHASE.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA

description: The date and time when this rate restriction was activated and distributed to connected channels (CRS, GDS, OTA). Represents the principal business event time — when the restriction became operationally effective in the distribution ecosystem. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that created this rate restriction record. Supports SOX financial controls audit trail and accountability for yield management decisions.
reference: SOX; IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The date and time when this rate restriction record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the hurdle_rate_amount and any monetary thresholds defined in this restriction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI standards.

7-character binary string indicating which days of the week the restriction applies, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., '1111100' = Mon–Fri only, '0000011' = Sat–Sun only). Enables day-of-week yield strategies such as weekend minimum stay requirements.

Demand forecast classification at the time this restriction was created or last updated, as determined by the RMS (IDeaS G3 or Infor EzRMS). Provides context for why the restriction was applied and supports post-hoc yield strategy analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; STR STAR Report

description: Indicates the current distribution state of this restriction across connected channels via Sabre SynXis CRS. 'pending' = queued for distribution; 'distributed' = successfully pushed to all channels; 'failed' = distribution error; 'partial' = distributed to some but not all channels.

The calendar date from which this rate restriction becomes active and enforceable. Corresponds to the stay date (not booking date) unless the restriction type is ADVANCE_PURCHASE. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA

description: The last calendar date on which this rate restriction is enforceable (inclusive). Null indicates an open-ended restriction with no defined end. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Minimum acceptable rate (in the property's base currency) below which a booking will be rejected for this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A key RMS yield control used to protect ADR during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not HURDLE_RATE.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: Indicates whether the effective date range is designated as a blackout period, during which the rate plan is completely closed to new bookings. Commonly applied during peak demand events, holidays, or sold-out periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA

description: Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check in on the restricted date, preventing short-stay bookings that fragment inventory during high-demand periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Indicates whether departures are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check out on the restricted date, used to extend stays through high-demand nights and maximize occupancy continuity.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Indicates whether members of the hotel's loyalty program are exempt from this restriction (e.g., LRA — Last Room Availability guarantee for elite members). Supports loyalty program administration and member benefit fulfillment.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Salesforce CRM; AHLA Loyalty Standards

description: The date and time when this restriction was last successfully distributed to external channels (OTA, GDS) via Sabre SynXis CRS. Used to monitor distribution latency and SLA compliance for rate parity management.

Maximum number of days prior to arrival within which a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Defines the booking window ceiling for advance purchase restrictions, preventing bookings made too far in advance at promotional rates.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). Used to prevent long-stay bookings at discounted rates during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not MAX_LOS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A core yield optimization lever in revenue management. Null when restriction type is not MIN_LOS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes or context provided by the revenue manager regarding this restriction. May include references to specific events, competitive intelligence, or strategy rationale not captured in structured fields.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Indicates whether a revenue manager or front desk supervisor is permitted to manually override this restriction for individual bookings. When False, the restriction is hard-enforced and cannot be bypassed in the PMS or CRS.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Minimum staff authorization level required to override this restriction when override_allowed is True. Ensures appropriate governance over yield control exceptions. Null when override_allowed is False.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX

description: Numeric priority rank used to resolve conflicts when multiple restrictions apply to the same rate plan, room type, and date. Lower values indicate higher priority. Ensures deterministic restriction enforcement when overlapping rules exist in the RMS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate restriction rule as defined in IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and distribution to OTA and GDS channels.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Human-readable name describing the rate restriction rule (e.g., 'Weekend Min 2-Night Stay', 'Holiday CTA Block'). Used by revenue managers and front desk staff to identify restrictions in PMS and RMS interfaces.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Free-text or coded explanation for why this restriction was applied (e.g., 'Peak demand event', 'Holiday blackout', 'Group block protection', 'RMS auto-recommendation'). Supports revenue strategy documentation and post-stay performance analysis.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA

description: Defines the level at which this restriction is applied: 'property' = entire property; 'room_type' = specific room category; 'rate_plan' = specific rate plan only; 'channel' = specific distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct). Determines how broadly the restriction is enforced across inventory.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Current lifecycle state of the rate restriction record. 'active' = currently enforced; 'inactive' = disabled but retained for history; 'pending' = scheduled but not yet effective; 'expired' = past end date; 'overridden' = manually superseded by a revenue manager.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Classification of the restriction mechanism applied. MIN_LOS = Minimum Length of Stay; MAX_LOS = Maximum Length of Stay; CTA = Closed to Arrival; CTD = Closed to Departure; ADVANCE_PURCHASE = minimum days before arrival required to book; BLACKOUT = date fully closed to booking; HURDLE_RATE = minimum acceptable rate threshold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MIN_LOS|MAX_LOS|CTA|CTD|ADVANCE_PURCHASE|BLACKOUT|HURDLE_RATE — promote to reference product]
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA Revenue Management Standards

description: Indicates whether this restriction was system-recommended by the RMS (True) or manually created by a revenue manager (False). Enables analysis of RMS automation adoption rates and comparison of system vs. human yield decisions.

Identifies the operational system of record that originated this rate restriction record. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: The native identifier of this restriction record in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, etc.). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the system of record.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG

description: Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that last modified this rate restriction record. Supports audit trail and governance of yield management changes.
reference: SOX; IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The date and time when this rate restriction record was last modified in the source system. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each rate plan record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the revenue_rate_plan data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Rate plans are frequently created to support specific marketing campaigns (flash sales, seasonal promotions). Campaign managers track which rate plans were deployed per campaign to measure ROI and rev

Foreign key linking to event.catering_menu. Business justification: MICE and group rate plans bundle specific catering menus (e.g., all-inclusive day delegate packages). Linking revenue_rate_plan to catering_menu enables package pricing validation, F&B revenue attribu

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Rate plans are distributed through specific channels (OTA, GDS, direct booking). Revenue managers configure which channels can sell which rate plans - core distribution strategy. Replaces denormalized

Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements define royalty_fee_pct, marketing_fee_pct, and loyalty_fee_pct that directly affect rate plan economics and minimum rate floors. Linking revenue_rate_plan to franchise_agreement e

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Rate plans include F&B benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, dining credits). Required for package fulfillment, benefit tracking, revenue allocation between rooms and F&B departments per USALI stand

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Rate plans must post room revenue to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition and financial statement preparation. Essential for USALI compliance and audit trail.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Rate plans enforce tier-specific eligibility (loyalty_tier_required field exists). Business process: validating member tier qualification for rate plan access at booking time. Essential for tier-based

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: revenue_rate_plan.market_segment_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to the market segment the rate plan targets. Adding market_segment_id normalizes this to the authoritative market_segment rec

Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Rate plans must validate against loyalty program rules (points eligibility, conversion rates, tier requirements). Business process: ensuring rate plan configuration aligns with active loyalty program

Foreign key linking to experience.program. Business justification: Rate plans bundle experience programs (e.g., 'Romance Package' rate plan tied to a curated experience program). This FK enables package revenue attribution and program eligibility enforcement at booki

Reference to the property or hotel at which this rate plan is applicable. Enables property-level rate plan management across a multi-property portfolio. A null value indicates a chain-wide or global rate plan applicable to all properties.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS

description: Reference to the specific room type or room category to which this rate plan applies. A null value indicates the rate plan applies to all room types at the associated property.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IDeaS G3 RMS

description: Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Rate plans are designed around guest journey touchpoints (pre-arrival, check-in, in-stay offers) for targeted pricing strategies. Revenue managers create touchpoint-specific rates to optimize conversi

Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Common values are 7, 14, 21, or 30 days for advance purchase promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Name or identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this rate plan for distribution. Supports SOX financial controls and internal audit requirements for rate authorization governance.
reference: SOX; USALI; SAP S/4HANA; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: The timestamp when this rate plan was formally approved for activation and distribution. Distinct from created_timestamp; captures the authorization event in the rate plan lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX; USALI; SAP S/4HANA; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: The principal nightly room rate amount in the operating currency before any adjustments, taxes, or fees. Represents the rack rate or floor price for this rate plan. Used as the reference value for ADR (Average Daily Rate) and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) calculations. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; USALI; STR STAR Report ADR methodology

description: The last date on which new reservations can be made under this rate plan. After this date the rate plan is closed to new bookings even if the stay dates fall within the effective period.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The earliest date on which reservations can be made under this rate plan. Used to control promotional rate availability windows and prevent premature booking of seasonal or event-driven rates.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy associated with this rate plan (e.g., FLEX24H, NRR_NOREF, 48H_PENALTY). Determines guest refund eligibility and penalty charges upon cancellation. Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase rate plans.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Consumer Protection Guidelines

description: The percentage of the room rate payable as commission to the booking agent or OTA when is_commissionable is true. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Null when is_commissionable is false.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; SAP S/4HANA; AHLA Distribution Standards; USALI

description: The timestamp when this rate plan record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5; SOX; USALI; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the base_rate_amount and all monetary fields on this rate plan are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.

Percentage discount applied to the BAR (Best Available Rate) or rack rate to derive this rate plan's selling price. Used for corporate negotiated rates, loyalty member rates, and promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no discount from the reference rate.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; USALI

description: Indicates whether this rate plan is subject to DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) adjustments by the revenue management system. When true, IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS may automatically adjust the rate based on demand forecasts, occupancy levels, and competitive benchmarking.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: The calendar date from which this rate plan becomes active and bookable. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS to control rate availability windows. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: The calendar date after which this rate plan is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended rate plan with no defined end date. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport. Controls rate visibility to travel agents using GDS booking tools.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; AHLA

description: Indicates whether a travel agent or OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission is payable on bookings made under this rate plan. Drives commission accrual and payment processing in SAP S/4HANA AP.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; SAP S/4HANA; AHLA Distribution Standards

description: Indicates whether this rate plan carries LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, meaning the contracted rate must be honored even when only the last room in a category is available. Applicable to corporate negotiated rate contracts.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Corporate Rate Contracting Standards

description: Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for a full refund upon cancellation within the policy window. False for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) plans; true for flexible BAR (Best Available Rate) plans.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Consumer Protection Guidelines; GDPR Article 17

description: The timestamp when this rate plan record was most recently updated in the source system. Used to detect changes for incremental ETL processing and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5; SOX; USALI; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Indicates whether stays booked under this rate plan qualify for loyalty program points accrual. Wholesale, opaque, and certain promotional rates are typically ineligible for points earning.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Loyalty Program Standards

description: Minimum loyalty program membership tier required to access this rate plan. A value of 'none' indicates the rate is open to all guests regardless of loyalty status. Used to gate exclusive member rates.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Loyalty Program Standards

description: Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan. Used to prevent extended stays at promotional or deeply discounted rates. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction.

Indicates the F&B (Food and Beverage) inclusions bundled with this rate plan. Room only includes no meals; bed and breakfast includes morning meal; half board includes breakfast and dinner; full board includes all three meals; all-inclusive covers meals, beverages, and selected activities.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; IH&RA Meal Plan Standards; ISO 22000

description: Minimum number of nights a guest must book to qualify for this rate plan. A LOS (Length of Stay) restriction used in yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Booking.com, Expedia, or Agoda. Controls rate visibility on third-party booking platforms.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; HTNG Channel Distribution Standards; AHLA

description: Secondary classification grouping rate plans by the market segment or distribution channel they serve. Public rates are available to all guests; negotiated rates are contracted with specific accounts; wholesale rates are sold through tour operators; group rates apply to block bookings; loyalty rates are exclusive to program members; employee rates are internal staff benefits.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; STR STAR Report segmentation standards

description: The maximum rate above which this rate plan cannot be sold, used to cap pricing during peak demand periods for contracted or promotional rates. Null indicates no ceiling constraint.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI

description: The minimum rate below which this rate plan cannot be sold, regardless of dynamic pricing adjustments. Protects rate integrity and brand positioning. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS as a hard constraint in yield optimization algorithms.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Infor EzRMS; USALI; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Indicates the organizational scope at which this rate plan is defined and managed. Property-level plans apply to a single hotel; brand-level plans apply across a brand portfolio; chain-level plans apply across the entire company; global plans may span international markets.
reference: Sabre SynXis CRS; IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rate plan across systems (e.g., BAR, CORP001, PKG-SPA). Used as the business key in IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate plan lookup and distribution. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Rate Plan Standards

description: Free-text description of the rate plan's terms, inclusions, and conditions as displayed to guests and booking agents. Includes details on what is included in the rate, any special conditions, and marketing messaging.
reference: Oracle OPERA PMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; AHLA Consumer Transparency Guidelines

description: Human-readable name of the rate plan displayed to guests and revenue managers (e.g., 'Best Available Rate', 'AAA Member Rate', 'Advance Purchase 21-Day'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR (Best Available Rate) is the publicly available flexible rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees availability to contracted accounts; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) carries no cancellation flexibility; corporate rates are negotiated with companies; package rates bundle room with ancillary services; promotional rates are time-limited offers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; AHLA Rate Classification Standards; USALI

description: Current lifecycle state of the rate plan. Active plans are available for booking; inactive plans are temporarily disabled; draft plans are under configuration; suspended plans are paused due to yield or compliance review; archived plans are retired and no longer bookable. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this rate plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same rate plan exists in multiple systems.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; Infor EzRMS; HTNG Data Exchange Standards

description: The native identifier of this rate plan in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Oracle OPERA PMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Sabre SynXis CRS; Oracle OPERA PMS; HTNG Data Exchange Standards

description: Defines any day-of-week or date-specific restrictions on when stays under this rate plan are permitted. Weekday-only and weekend-only restrictions are common for corporate and leisure rates respectively; blackout dates exclude specific high-demand periods.
reference: IDeaS G3 RMS; Oracle OPERA PMS; AHLA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the base_rate_amount includes applicable taxes (VAT, occupancy tax, city tax). True for tax-inclusive markets; false for markets where taxes are added at checkout. Critical for accurate revenue recognition under USALI and IFRS/GAAP.
reference: USALI; IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA; PCI DSS

description: Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key.
Reference to a parent cost center in a hierarchical cost center structure, enabling roll-up reporting and consolidated P&L views. Null if this is a top-level cost center.
Reference to the property to which this cost center belongs.
The total annual budgeted amount allocated to this cost center for the current fiscal year, used for variance analysis and financial control.
Indicates whether all financial transactions posted to this cost center must maintain a full audit trail for compliance and internal control purposes (True) or standard logging is sufficient (False).
Indicates whether this cost center has a dedicated budget allocation and is subject to budget control and variance reporting (True) or operates without a formal budget (False).
The SAP company code representing the legal entity to which this cost center belongs, used for consolidated financial reporting and legal compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) Company Code

description: The SAP controlling area under which this cost center is managed, representing the organizational unit for internal cost accounting and management reporting.
The method used to allocate shared costs or overhead to this cost center: direct assignment, activity-based costing, percentage allocation, headcount-based, or square footage-based.
reference: USALI Cost Allocation Guidelines, SAP S/4HANA CO Assessment/Distribution

description: The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center within the property and financial system. Used in GL postings and financial reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Master, USALI Departmental Structure

description: Detailed description of the cost center's purpose, scope, and operational responsibilities.
The full business name of the cost center (e.g., Rooms Division, Food and Beverage, Spa Operations, Administration).
Current lifecycle status of the cost center: active (operational and accepting postings), inactive (temporarily not in use), suspended (blocked for new postings), or closed (permanently retired).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Master Data Status

description: Classification of the cost center by its primary function: revenue-generating departments, support services, administrative functions, or overhead allocation centers.
The username or employee identifier of the user who created this cost center record.
The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this cost center's financial transactions are recorded (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
High-level USALI category: Operated Departments (revenue-generating), Undistributed Operating Expenses (support), or Fixed Charges (property-level overhead).
The date on which this cost center ceased operations or was closed. Null for currently active cost centers.
The date from which this cost center became active and began accepting financial postings.
Indicates whether this cost center's financial results are included in external financial statements and regulatory filings (True) or used only for internal management reporting (False).
The number of full-time equivalent (FTE) employees allocated to this cost center, used for labor cost analysis and productivity metrics.
The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy (1=top-level, 2=sub-department, etc.), used for roll-up aggregation and reporting.
Indicates whether labor costs (payroll, benefits, overtime) are tracked and allocated to this cost center (True) or excluded from labor cost analysis (False).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Activity-Based Costing, USALI Labor Cost Reporting

description: The username or employee identifier of the user who last modified this cost center record.
The date and time when this cost center record was last updated or modified.
Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the cost center's operations, budget constraints, or reporting requirements.
Indicates whether revenue transactions can be posted to this cost center (True for revenue-generating departments) or only expense postings are allowed (False for support/administrative centers).
Indicates whether this cost center is subject to SOX financial controls and audit requirements (True for publicly traded entities) or exempt (False).
The total square footage of physical space occupied by this cost center, used for space-based cost allocation and facility management analysis.

business_glossary_term: Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (USALI) Department Code
description: The standard USALI departmental code aligning this cost center to the industry-standard chart of accounts for lodging P&L reporting (e.g., 10=Rooms, 20=Food, 30=Beverage, 40=Other Operated Departments, 50=Administrative & General).
Primary key for fiscal_period
Reference to the corresponding fiscal period in the prior year. Used for year-over-year variance analysis and comparative financial reporting.
The budget version or scenario associated with this fiscal period (e.g., Original Budget, Revised Budget, Forecast). Used for budget vs actual variance analysis.
Total number of business days (excluding weekends and holidays) in this fiscal period. Used for operational metrics and productivity calculations.
The calendar month number (1-12) in which the period starts. Used for calendar-based reporting and seasonality analysis.
The calendar quarter number (1-4) in which the period starts. Used for calendar-based comparisons and external benchmarking.
The calendar year in which the period starts (e.g., 2024). May differ from fiscal_year for companies with non-calendar fiscal years.
The actual date when the fiscal period was closed for transaction posting. Null if period is still open. Used for financial close tracking and SOX compliance.
The timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Total number of calendar days in this fiscal period. Used for daily average calculations (e.g., Average Daily Rate, Revenue Per Available Room).
The last calendar date included in this fiscal period. Defines the end boundary for transaction posting and financial reporting.
The fiscal month number within the fiscal year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods. Used for monthly financial close and management reporting.
Additional descriptive text or notes about this fiscal period, including special circumstances, adjustments, or reporting considerations.

Current lifecycle status of the fiscal period. Open: period is active and transactions can be posted. Closed: period is closed for regular posting but can be reopened. Locked: period is finalized and cannot accept new transactions without special authorization. Archived: period is archived for historical reference only.
The fiscal quarter number within the fiscal year (1-4). Null for non-quarterly periods. Used for quarterly financial reporting and SEC filings.
The fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1-52 or 1-53). Null for non-weekly periods. Used for weekly operational and revenue reporting in hospitality operations.
The fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024). Used for year-over-year comparisons and annual financial statement consolidation.
Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (e.g., Period 13) used for year-end adjustments and accruals. True if adjustment period, False if regular operating period.
Indicates whether this fiscal period falls within a leap year. True if leap year, False otherwise. Used for accurate daily average calculations.
The date when the fiscal period was locked and finalized. Null if period is not yet locked. Used for audit trail and financial statement finalization.
The timestamp when this fiscal period record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Business identifier code for the fiscal period, typically formatted as YYYY-MM or YYYY-Pnn (e.g., 2024-01, 2024-P01). Used in financial reports and external communications.
Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., January 2024, Q1 2024, FY2024-P01). Used for display in reports and dashboards.
Sequential period number within the fiscal year (e.g., 1-12 for monthly periods, 1-4 for quarterly periods). Used for period-to-period comparisons and trend analysis.

Classification of the fiscal period granularity: month (standard monthly period), quarter (quarterly period), year (annual fiscal year), week (weekly period for operational reporting), or custom (non-standard period for special reporting needs).
The date when a previously closed period was reopened for adjustments. Null if period has never been reopened. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance monitoring.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency consolidation.
The first calendar date included in this fiscal period. Defines the beginning of the period for transaction posting and financial reporting.
Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Some GL accounts have default cost centers for automatic allocation when no specific cost center is provided on transactions. The cost_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_ce

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Some GL accounts have default profit centers for automatic allocation when no specific profit center is provided on transactions. The profit_center_code STRING should be replaced with a proper FK to p
The unique identifier of the property to which this ledger account belongs. Supports property-level chart of accounts and multi-property financial consolidation. Links to the property master entity.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The USALI-defined category grouping for the account (e.g., Rooms, Food and Beverage, Other Operated Departments, Administrative and General, Sales and Marketing, Property Operations and Maintenance, Utilities, Fixed Charges). Used for departmental P&L reporting and GOP calculation.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The descriptive name of the general ledger account (e.g., Room Revenue, Food and Beverage Cost of Sales, Payroll and Related Expenses, Property Operations and Maintenance).
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The unique account number assigned to this general ledger account per USALI chart of accounts structure. Typically 4-10 digit numeric code used for financial reporting and transaction posting.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The detailed subcategory within the USALI account category (e.g., within Food and Beverage: Restaurant Revenue, Banquet Revenue, Room Service Revenue; within Rooms: Payroll and Related, Guest Supplies, Laundry and Linen). Enables granular financial analysis.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The fundamental accounting classification of the ledger account: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Determines placement in financial statements and normal balance behavior.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: Indicates whether detailed audit trail documentation is required for all transactions posted to this account. Used for high-risk accounts, regulatory compliance, and forensic accounting.

The section of the balance sheet where this account appears (current assets, non-current assets, current liabilities, non-current liabilities, equity). Determines liquidity classification and working capital calculation.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: The cash flow statement category for this account: operating activities, investing activities, or financing activities. Used for automated cash flow statement preparation per GAAP/IFRS.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: The account number used for consolidation at the corporate or regional level. Maps property-level accounts to standardized corporate chart of accounts for multi-property financial consolidation.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: The timestamp when this ledger account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this ledger account is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency accounting for global property portfolios.
The date on which this ledger account becomes inactive and no longer accepts new transactions. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account retirement and chart of accounts cleanup.
reference: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger Master Data

description: The date from which this ledger account becomes active and available for transaction posting. Supports phased chart of accounts implementation and account structure changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger Master Data

description: The functional area classification for financial statement presentation (e.g., Operating Revenue, Cost of Sales, Operating Expenses, Fixed Charges). Used for USALI-compliant income statement formatting and GOP calculation.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The section of the income statement where this account appears per USALI format (e.g., Operated Departments Revenue, Operated Departments Expenses, Undistributed Operating Expenses, Fixed Charges). Supports GOP, EBITDA, and NOI calculation.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Indicates whether the ledger account is currently active and available for transaction posting. Inactive accounts are retained for historical reporting but cannot accept new transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger Master Data

description: Indicates whether this is a control account (summary account) that aggregates balances from subsidiary ledgers (e.g., Accounts Receivable control account summarizing individual guest folios). Control accounts do not accept direct postings.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), SAP S/4HANA General Ledger

description: Indicates whether this account is used for intercompany transactions between properties or legal entities within the hospitality group. Intercompany accounts are eliminated during consolidated financial statement preparation.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: Indicates whether this account requires periodic reconciliation (e.g., bank accounts, accounts receivable, inventory accounts). Used for internal control and SOX compliance.

Indicates whether this is a statistical account used for non-monetary metrics (e.g., room nights sold, covers served, occupied room count). Statistical accounts support KPI calculation (ADR, RevPAR, GOPPAR) but do not carry monetary balances.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The date of the most recent transaction posted to this account. Used for dormant account identification and account usage analysis.
Extended description of the ledger account purpose, usage guidelines, and posting rules. Provides additional context beyond the account name for accounting staff and auditors.
reference: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: The timestamp when this ledger account record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance. Assets and expenses have debit normal balances; liabilities, equity, and revenue have credit normal balances. Used for transaction validation and financial statement preparation.
reference: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: Free-form notes field for special instructions, reconciliation notes, or account-specific documentation. Used by accounting staff for operational guidance and audit documentation.

Indicates whether direct posting to this account is blocked. Posting blocks are used for control accounts, summary accounts, or accounts under investigation. Transactions must be posted to subsidiary accounts instead.
reference: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger Master Data

description: Indicates whether this account is subject to SOX internal control testing and documentation requirements. Typically true for material accounts affecting financial statement accuracy for publicly traded hospitality companies.

The tax category or tax line item code associated with this account for tax reporting purposes (e.g., taxable revenue, non-taxable revenue, deductible expense, non-deductible expense). Supports automated tax return preparation.
reference: IRS (Internal Revenue Service), Local Tax Authorities

description: Unique identifier for the profit center. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key reference to the primary cost center associated with this profit center for expense allocation and departmental accounting per USALI standards.
Foreign key reference to the legal ownership entity responsible for this profit center. Supports consolidated financial statements across the portfolio and inter-company eliminations.

Foreign key reference to the parent profit center in a hierarchical profit center structure. Enables roll-up reporting for complex properties with nested profit centers (e.g., a resort with multiple F&B (Food and Beverage) outlets).

Foreign key reference to the parent property or resort to which this profit center belongs. Enables property-level P&L (Profit and Loss) consolidation and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) contribution analysis.
Foreign key reference to the primary revenue center for this profit center. Used for revenue recognition and departmental revenue reporting per USALI.
Primary street address line for the profit center's physical location. Required for property-level profit centers and standalone F&B (Food and Beverage) or event venues.
Secondary address line for suite, floor, or building information.
Brand or franchise affiliation code for the profit center (e.g., Marriott, Hilton, Hyatt, independent). Used for brand-level performance reporting and franchise fee calculations.

SAP Business Area code for cross-company-code segment reporting. Enables consolidated reporting across multiple legal entities within the same business segment (e.g., luxury hotels, select-service properties).
City or municipality where the profit center is located.
The date the profit center was permanently closed. Used for historical analysis and asset disposition reporting.
SAP Company Code representing the legal entity for external financial reporting. Links profit center results to the general ledger and statutory financial statements.
SAP Controlling Area code to which this profit center is assigned. Defines the organizational boundary for internal cost and revenue accounting.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the profit center's location. Used for multi-country consolidation and compliance with local regulatory reporting requirements.
Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) compliance requirements.

The date on which this profit center ceased operations or was closed. Null for currently active profit centers. Used for historical reporting and closed property analysis.

description: The date from which this profit center became active and began contributing to financial statements. Used for time-based reporting and historical analysis.

description: Primary email address for business correspondence related to this profit center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

description: The level of this profit center within the profit center hierarchy (1 = top-level property, 2 = department, 3 = sub-department, etc.). Used for recursive roll-up calculations.

The original opening or launch date of the profit center. Used for property age analysis and lifecycle reporting.
Primary contact phone number for the profit center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Postal or ZIP code for the profit center's address.

The externally-known unique business identifier for the profit center used in financial reporting and consolidation. Typically alphanumeric code assigned per SAP S/4HANA Profit Center Accounting standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Profit Center Accounting, USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry)

description: Detailed business description of the profit center, including its purpose, services offered, and unique characteristics. Used for internal documentation and reporting context.

description: Current lifecycle status of the profit center. Active centers contribute to consolidated financial statements; inactive or closed centers are excluded from operational reporting.

description: ISO 3166-2 state or province code for the profit center's location. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and regulatory reporting.
Tax jurisdiction code for sales tax, VAT (Value-Added Tax), or GST (Goods and Services Tax) reporting. Used for compliance with local tax regulations and automated tax calculation.

The planned or forecasted monetary amount for this budget line item. Represents the target financial value for the specified GL account, cost center, and fiscal period combination.
Name or identifier of the executive or committee who approved this budget. Supports SOX financial controls and audit trail.
The date on which this budget was formally approved. Critical for audit trail and SOX compliance.

High-level classification of the budget line: revenue (rooms, F&B, events), operating expense (utilities, supplies), labor expense (wages, benefits), capital expenditure (CapEx), or furniture, fixtures, and equipment (FF&E) reserve.
Descriptive name of the budget (e.g., 'FY2024 Annual Operating Budget', 'Q2 2024 Reforecast').
Externally-known unique identifier for this budget document. Used for reference in management reports and approvals.
